judgmentZh: 乾：元，亨，利，贞。
judgmentEn: The Creative works sublime success, furthering through perseverance.
imageZh: 天行健，君子以自强不息。
imageEn: The movement of heaven is full of power. Thus the superior man makes himself strong and untiring.
interpretationZh: 乾卦象征天，代表刚健、创造、进取。六爻皆阳，纯阳至刚，是最具开创力量的卦象。
interpretationEn: Qian represents heaven and pure creative power. All six lines are yang, symbolizing strength, initiative, and the primal creative force.
overviewZh: 乾卦是《周易》六十四卦之首，由六个阳爻组成，纯阳至刚，象征天道运行不息。乾为天，代表宇宙间最原始、最强大的创造力量。此卦以龙为喻，从潜龙到飞龙再到亢龙，描绘了事物从萌芽、发展、鼎盛到衰退的完整生命周期。乾卦的核心精神是“自强不息”——天体运行永不停歇，君子当效法天道，持续精进，刚健有为。乾卦四德“元亨利贞”分别代表万物之始、通达亨通、和谐有利、正固持守，是宇宙运行的根本法则，也是为人处世的最高准则。
overviewEn: Qian (The Creative) is the first hexagram of the I Ching, composed of six unbroken yang lines representing pure creative power. It symbolizes heaven and the ceaseless movement of celestial forces. Using the dragon as its central metaphor, it traces the complete arc of development—from the hidden dragon lying dormant, through the dragon appearing in the field, to the flying dragon in the heavens, and finally the arrogant dragon facing regret. The four virtues of Qian—yuan (sublime), heng (success), li (furthering), and zhen (perseverance)—represent the fundamental principles governing the cosmos and human conduct. The hexagram teaches that true strength lies not in brute force but in tireless self-cultivation and knowing when to advance or retreat.
judgmentDetailZh: 乾卦卦辞“元亨利贞”四字，被称为乾之四德，是整部《周易》最核心的概念之一。“元”为万物之始，代表创始、仁德，是春天万物萌发的力量；“亨”为通达，代表事物顺利发展，如夏天万物繁茂；“利”为和谐适宜，代表秋天收获的正当性；“贞”为正固，代表冬天收藏的坚守。四德循环往复，如同四季更替，生生不息。孔子在《文言传》中将四德与人伦相对应：元为仁，亨为礼，利为义，贞为智。修身之人若能体悟四德，便能与天道合一，成就大业。乾卦告诫我们：真正的刚强不是一味进取，而是在不同阶段采取恰当的行动——该潜则潜，该飞则飞，该止则止。
judgmentDetailEn: The four characters of Qian's judgment—yuan, heng, li, zhen—are known as the Four Virtues of the Creative, forming one of the most essential concepts in the entire I Ching. 'Yuan' (Sublime) represents the origin of all things, the power of spring when life begins to stir. 'Heng' (Success) signifies smooth development, like summer when all things flourish. 'Li' (Furthering) embodies harmony and appropriateness, the rightful harvest of autumn. 'Zhen' (Perseverance) stands for steadfast correctness, the conservation of winter. These four virtues cycle endlessly like the seasons. Confucius, in the Wenyan Commentary, mapped them to human virtues: yuan as benevolence, heng as propriety, li as righteousness, and zhen as wisdom. The hexagram teaches that true strength is not relentless aggression but knowing the right action for each phase—when to lie hidden, when to soar, and when to stop.
imageDetailZh: 象辞“天行健，君子以自强不息”是中华文明最著名的格言之一，出自《大象传》对乾卦的解读。“天行健”描述的是天体运行刚健有力、永不停歇的自然现象——日月星辰各循其道，从不懈怠。“君子以自强不息”则是从天道推及人道：有德行的人应当效法天的精神，持续不断地修养自身、精进学问、完善品格。这里的“自强”不是与他人争强好胜，而是对自我的不断超越；“不息”不是不知疲倦地蛮干，而是持之以恒的坚定意志。清华大学校训“自强不息，厚德载物”即取自乾坤两卦象辞，体现了中华文化刚柔并济的最高理想。
imageDetailEn: The Image text 'The movement of heaven is full of power; thus the superior man makes himself strong and untiring' is one of the most celebrated maxims in Chinese civilization. 'The movement of heaven is full of power' describes the ceaseless, vigorous motion of celestial bodies—the sun, moon, and stars each following their course without rest. 'The superior man makes himself strong and untiring' extends this cosmic principle to human conduct: a person of virtue should emulate heaven's spirit through continuous self-cultivation, learning, and character refinement. The 'strength' here is not about competing with others but about transcending one's own limitations. The 'untiring' quality is not reckless exertion but unwavering perseverance. Tsinghua University's motto—'Self-discipline and social commitment'—draws directly from the Image texts of Qian and Kun, embodying the Chinese ideal of balancing firmness with gentleness.
modernApplicationZh: 【事业】乾卦提醒我们要像龙一样审时度势：创业初期如“潜龙”，应低调积累实力；事业上升期如“见龙在田”，要积极寻找导师和合作伙伴；事业巅峰如“飞龙在天”，可大展宏图但不可骄傲自满；而“亢龙有悔”则警示功成名就后要懂得急流勇退。【感情】乾卦代表阳刚主动的能量，提示在关系中要真诚、有担当，但也要避免过于强势。【财运】乾卦象征创造力和开拓精神，适合开创新事业、投资新领域，但要注意“亢龙有悔”的风险——贪心不足会导致损失。【健康】乾卦对应头部和肺部，提醒注意用脑过度和呼吸系统保养，保持规律运动，效法天道“自强不息”。
modernApplicationEn: Qian offers profound guidance for modern life. In career, it reminds us to read the situation like the dragon: during early stages ('hidden dragon'), build strength quietly; during growth ('dragon in the field'), actively seek mentors and partners; at the peak ('flying dragon'), pursue grand visions without arrogance; and 'the arrogant dragon' warns that after success, one must know when to step back. In relationships, Qian represents proactive yang energy—be sincere and responsible, but avoid being overbearing. For finances, Qian symbolizes creativity and pioneering spirit, favoring new ventures and investments, while cautioning against the 'arrogant dragon' risk of overreach leading to loss. For health, Qian corresponds to the head and lungs—watch for mental exhaustion and respiratory care, maintain regular exercise, and embody heaven's spirit of tireless self-strengthening.
historicalStoryZh: 乾卦最著名的历史典故当属孔子读《易》“韦编三绝”的故事。相传孔子晚年酷爱研读《周易》，尤其对乾卦反复揣摩，以至于编连竹简的牛皮绳断了三次。孔子从乾卦中悟出“天行健，君子以自强不息”的道理，将其作为毕生修身的准则。另一个典故是诸葛亮以乾卦自勉。诸葛亮隐居隆中时如“潜龙勿用”，韬光养晦等待明主；刘备三顾茅庐后出山辅佐，如“见龙在田”；北伐中原时如“飞龙在天”，鞠躬尽瘁。诸葛亮的一生完美诠释了乾卦六爻从潜到飞的精神境界，成为后世“自强不息”的典范。
historicalStoryEn: The most famous historical story associated with Qian is Confucius and his devoted study of the I Ching. In his later years, Confucius studied the Book of Changes so intensely—particularly the Qian hexagram—that the leather straps binding his bamboo scrolls broke three times (a story known as 'wei bian san jue'). From Qian, he derived the principle of tireless self-improvement that guided his entire life. Another celebrated story involves Zhuge Liang, the legendary strategist of the Three Kingdoms period. While living in seclusion at Longzhong, he embodied the 'hidden dragon'—quietly cultivating his abilities while awaiting the right leader. When Liu Bei visited him three times to seek his counsel, Zhuge Liang emerged like the 'dragon appearing in the field.' His Northern Expeditions represented the 'flying dragon in the heavens,' as he devoted himself completely to his cause. Zhuge Liang's life perfectly illustrates the spiritual journey of Qian's six lines.
relatedHexagramsNote: 乾卦的错卦为坤卦（第2卦），阴阳完全相反，刚柔互补；综卦亦为乾卦本身（上下颠倒不变）；互卦为乾卦（取二三四五爻组成的内卦仍为纯阳）。乾坤两卦互为表里，共同构成《周易》的根基。

lines.textZh: 初九：潜龙勿用。
lines.textEn: Nine at the beginning: Hidden dragon. Do not act.
lines.interpretationZh: 龙潜于深渊，时机未到，宜韬光养晦。
lines.interpretationEn: The dragon is hidden. It is not yet time to act.
lines.detailZh: 初九为乾卦最下一爻，阳气初生，力量尚弱，如龙潜伏于深水之中。此时万事草创，条件尚未成熟，不宜贸然行动。“潜”字是关键——不是没有能力，而是时机未到。如同种子在土壤中积蓄力量，等待破土而出的那一刻。智者在此阶段应当韬光养晦，默默积累学识、人脉和资源，为将来的腾飞做好充分准备。历史上许多成大事者，都经历过漫长的“潜龙”阶段。
lines.detailEn: The first nine is the lowest line of Qian, where yang energy has just been born and is still weak—like a dragon hidden deep beneath the waters. Conditions are not yet ripe for action. The key word is 'hidden'—not lacking ability, but awaiting the right moment. Like a seed gathering strength underground before breaking through the soil, the wise person in this phase should quietly accumulate knowledge, connections, and resources, preparing thoroughly for future ascent. Many great historical figures endured long periods as the 'hidden dragon' before achieving greatness.

lines.textZh: 九二：见龙在田，利见大人。
lines.textEn: Nine in the second place: Dragon appearing in the field. It furthers one to see the great man.
lines.interpretationZh: 龙出现在田野，开始崭露头角，宜寻求贵人指引。
lines.interpretationEn: The dragon shows itself. Seek guidance from a wise mentor.
lines.detailZh: 九二阳爻居阴位，刚柔相济，龙从深渊浮出水面，出现在广阔的田野上。这是崭露头角的阶段——才华开始被人发现，但尚未达到权力的中心。“利见大人”意味着此时最重要的是寻找良师益友、贵人提携。田野象征广阔的舞台，龙已经具备了施展才华的基本条件。在现代语境中，这相当于职场新人开始展现实力，需要主动寻求导师指导，建立专业声誉，为进一步发展奠定基础。
lines.detailEn: The second nine is a yang line in a yin position, blending firmness with flexibility. The dragon has risen from the depths to appear in the open field—a stage of emerging visibility where talents begin to be recognized, though one has not yet reached the center of power. 'It furthers one to see the great man' means the most important action now is to seek wise mentors and benefactors. The field symbolizes a broad stage where the dragon has the basic conditions to display its abilities. In modern terms, this is like a professional beginning to demonstrate competence—actively seeking mentorship, building reputation, and laying foundations for further advancement.

lines.textZh: 九三：君子终日乾乾，夕惕若厉，无咎。
lines.textEn: Nine in the third place: All day long the superior man is creatively active. At nightfall his mind is still beset with cares. Danger. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 白天勤勉努力，夜晚保持警惕，虽有危险但无过失。
lines.interpretationEn: Be diligent by day and vigilant by night. Danger exists but no blame.
lines.detailZh: 九三处于下卦之顶，上不在天、下不在田，位置尴尬而危险。“终日乾乾”形容白天不停地勤勉努力，“夕惕若厉”则是夜晚仍保持高度警觉，如临深渊。这一爻描绘的是事业上升期最关键也最危险的阶段——已经取得一定成就，但根基尚不稳固，上有强者压制，下有后来者追赶。唯有日夜不懈、谨慎行事，才能化险为夷。“无咎”二字说明：只要保持这种勤勉警惕的态度，即使处境危险也不会有过失。
lines.detailEn: The third nine sits at the top of the lower trigram—neither in heaven nor in the field—an awkward and dangerous position. 'All day long creatively active' describes ceaseless diligence during the day, while 'at nightfall beset with cares' means maintaining acute vigilance at night, as if standing at the edge of an abyss. This line depicts the most critical and perilous phase of career ascent—having achieved some success but with foundations not yet secure, pressed from above and pursued from below. Only through tireless effort and careful conduct can one navigate safely through. The words 'no blame' confirm that maintaining this diligent, vigilant attitude prevents fault even in dangerous circumstances.

lines.textZh: 九四：或跃在渊，无咎。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fourth place: Wavering flight over the depths. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 在深渊上方跃动，进退皆可，把握时机则无过失。
lines.interpretationEn: Hovering between advance and retreat. Seize the right moment.
lines.detailZh: 九四进入上卦，已接近权力核心但尚未到达顶点。“或跃在渊”中的“或”字极为精妙——表示可进可退、可跃可潜的灵活状态。龙在深渊上方盘旋，随时准备一飞冲天，但也可以选择退回深渊继续蛰伏。这是一个充满选择的关键时刻，考验的是判断力和决断力。此爻教导我们：在重大转折点上，不必急于做出决定，审时度势、保持灵活才是上策。无论最终选择进还是退，只要出于审慎的判断，都不会有过失。
lines.detailEn: The fourth nine enters the upper trigram, approaching the center of power but not yet at the summit. The character 'huo' (perhaps/or) in 'wavering flight over the depths' is brilliantly nuanced—it suggests the flexibility to advance or retreat, to leap or to remain hidden. The dragon hovers above the abyss, ready to soar skyward at any moment yet equally able to return to the depths. This is a critical juncture full of choices, testing one's judgment and decisiveness. The line teaches that at major turning points, there is no need to rush decisions—assessing the situation and maintaining flexibility is the wisest course. Whether one ultimately advances or retreats, as long as the choice comes from careful deliberation, there will be no blame.

lines.textZh: 九五：飞龙在天，利见大人。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fifth place: Flying dragon in the heavens. It furthers one to see the great man.
lines.interpretationZh: 龙飞在天，事业达到巅峰，大展宏图之时。
lines.interpretationEn: The dragon flies in the heavens. A time of great achievement.
lines.detailZh: 九五是乾卦的核心爻位，阳爻居阳位，又处上卦之中，刚健中正，是最理想的位置。飞龙翱翔于天际，象征事业达到巅峰、才德兼备、众望所归。“利见大人”在此有双重含义：一是自己已成为“大人”，德才足以领导众人；二是仍需与其他贤能之士合作共事。九五之尊在中国文化中成为帝王的代称，因为它代表了权力与德行的完美结合。此爻提醒我们：即使身处巅峰，也要保持谦逊，广纳贤才，以德服人，方能长久。
lines.detailEn: The fifth nine is the core line of Qian—a yang line in a yang position at the center of the upper trigram, embodying perfect strength and balance. The flying dragon soaring through the heavens symbolizes reaching the pinnacle of achievement with both virtue and ability, commanding universal respect. 'It furthers one to see the great man' carries dual meaning here: one has become the 'great man' with sufficient virtue to lead others, yet one still needs to collaborate with other worthy individuals. In Chinese culture, 'the nine in the fifth place' became synonymous with the emperor, representing the perfect union of power and virtue. This line reminds us that even at the summit, one must remain humble, welcome talented people, and lead through moral authority to ensure lasting success.

lines.textZh: 上九：亢龙有悔。
lines.textEn: Nine at the top: Arrogant dragon will have cause to repent.
lines.interpretationZh: 龙飞得太高，物极必反，过刚则折，宜知进退。
lines.interpretationEn: The dragon has flown too high. Excess leads to regret.
lines.detailZh: 上九是乾卦最高一爻，阳气已达极盛，物极必反。龙飞得太高，高处不胜寒，已经脱离了与大地的联系。“亢”意为过度、极端，“悔”则是后悔、遗憾。这是整部《周易》最深刻的警示之一：任何事物发展到极端都会走向反面。功成名就后若不知收敛，必将招致灾祸。历史上无数英雄豪杰在巅峰时期因骄傲自满而跌落，正应了“亢龙有悔”的预言。此爻教导我们：知进退、明得失，功成身退才是最高的智慧。月满则亏，水满则溢，这是不可违背的自然法则。
lines.detailEn: The top nine is the highest line of Qian, where yang energy has reached its absolute peak and must inevitably reverse. The dragon has flown too high—isolated at the summit, disconnected from the earth below. 'Kang' means excess or extreme; 'hui' means regret or remorse. This is one of the most profound warnings in the entire I Ching: anything pushed to its extreme will reverse course. Those who do not know restraint after achieving success will inevitably invite disaster. Countless heroes throughout history have fallen from their peaks due to arrogance, fulfilling the prophecy of the 'arrogant dragon.' This line teaches the highest wisdom: know when to advance and when to retreat, understand what to gain and what to release. The full moon must wane, the brimming cup must overflow—these are inviolable laws of nature.

judgmentZh: 坤：元，亨，利牝马之贞。君子有攸往，先迷后得主。利西南得朋，东北丧朋。安贞吉。
judgmentEn: The Receptive brings about sublime success, furthering through the perseverance of a mare.
imageZh: 地势坤，君子以厚德载物。
imageEn: The earth's condition is receptive devotion. Thus the superior man who has breadth of character carries the outer world.
interpretationZh: 坤卦象征地，代表柔顺、包容、承载。六爻皆阴，纯阴至柔，是最具承载力量的卦象。
interpretationEn: Kun represents earth and pure receptive power. All six lines are yin, symbolizing devotion, nurturing, and the capacity to sustain.

lines.textZh: 初六：履霜，坚冰至。
lines.textEn: Six at the beginning: When there is hoarfrost underfoot, solid ice is not far off.
lines.interpretationZh: 踩到薄霜，预示严冬将至，见微知著。
lines.interpretationEn: Frost underfoot signals that solid ice is coming. Heed early signs.
lines.detailZh: 初六为坤卦最下一爻，阴气初凝，如霜降大地。“履霜坚冰至”是一个深刻的因果警示：踩到薄霜时，就要意识到严冬即将来临。事物的发展都有征兆，小的变化积累到一定程度就会引发质变。这一爻教导我们要有远见和敏锐的洞察力——看到苗头就要警觉，防微杜渐，不要等到问题严重了才后悔。在现代生活中，这相当于注意身体的小信号、市场的微妙变化、关系中的细微裂痕。
lines.detailEn: The first six is the lowest line of Kun, where yin energy first condenses, like frost settling on the earth. 'When there is hoarfrost underfoot, solid ice is not far off' is a profound warning about cause and effect: when you step on thin frost, you must realize that deep winter is approaching. All developments show early signs—small changes accumulate until they trigger fundamental transformation. This line teaches foresight and keen observation: notice the first signs and take preventive action rather than waiting until problems become severe.

lines.textZh: 六二：直，方，大，不习无不利。
lines.textEn: Six in the second place: Straight, square, great. Without purpose, yet nothing remains unfurthered.
lines.interpretationZh: 正直、方正、宏大，自然而然，无往不利。
lines.interpretationEn: Upright, square, and great. Natural virtue brings success without effort.
lines.detailZh: 六二是坤卦的核心爻位，阴爻居阴位，得位得中，是坤德最完美的体现。“直方大，不习无不利”描述了大地最本质的品格：正直、方正、广大。这三个字看似简单，却是极高的修养境界——“直”是内心的正直不阿，“方”是行事的规矩方正，“大”是胸怀的宽广包容。达到这种境界的人，不需要刻意学习就能处处顺利，因为他的品德本身就是最好的通行证。
lines.detailEn: The second six is the core line of Kun—a yin line in a yin position, perfectly placed and centered, representing the fullest expression of Kun's virtue. 'Straight, square, great—without purpose, yet nothing remains unfurthered' describes the earth's essential character: upright, principled, and vast. These three qualities represent an extremely high level of cultivation—'straight' is inner integrity, 'square' is principled conduct, and 'great' is expansive tolerance. One who achieves this state needs no deliberate effort to succeed, for their character itself is the best passport.

lines.textZh: 六三：含章可贞，或从王事，无成有终。
lines.textEn: Six in the third place: Hidden lines. One is able to remain persevering.
lines.interpretationZh: 内含文采而不显露，辅佐他人成事，虽无功名但有善终。
lines.interpretationEn: Conceal your talents. Serve others faithfully for a good outcome.
lines.detailZh: 六三阴爻居阳位，处于下卦之顶，位置不安。“含章可贞，或从王事，无成有终”教导一种内敛的智慧：有才华但不张扬（含章），保持正固（可贞），即使参与重大事务也不居功（无成有终）。这是坤卦“辅佐”精神的集中体现——真正的智者懂得隐藏锋芒，把功劳让给领导者，自己甘居幕后。在职场中，这种品质反而最容易获得长久的信任和重用。
lines.detailEn: The third six is a yin line in a yang position at the top of the lower trigram—an uneasy placement. 'Hidden lines, one may persevere. If following in service of a king, seek not works but bring to completion' teaches the wisdom of restraint: possessing talent without flaunting it, maintaining steadfastness, and not claiming credit even when participating in great undertakings. This is the concentrated expression of Kun's spirit of support—the truly wise know how to conceal their brilliance, yield credit to leaders, and willingly work behind the scenes.

lines.textZh: 六四：括囊，无咎无誉。
lines.textEn: Six in the fourth place: A tied-up sack. No blame, no praise.
lines.interpretationZh: 扎紧口袋，谨慎收敛，虽无赞誉但也无过失。
lines.interpretationEn: A tied sack. Caution brings neither blame nor praise.
lines.detailZh: 六四进入上卦，阴爻居阴位，处于谨慎收敛的阶段。“括囊，无咎无誉”的意象是把口袋扎紧——既不会有过失，也不会有赞誉。这是一种极端谨慎的处世态度，适用于危险或敏感的时期。当外部环境不利时，最明智的做法就是收敛锋芒、少说少做、保全自身。虽然不会获得荣耀，但也不会招来灾祸。在现代语境中，这相当于在公司动荡期保持低调，不站队、不表态，静观其变。
lines.detailEn: The fourth six enters the upper trigram—a yin line in a yin position, in a phase of cautious restraint. 'A tied-up sack—no blame, no praise' presents the image of tying a bag shut: neither fault nor glory will come. This represents an extremely cautious approach to life, suitable for dangerous or sensitive periods. When external conditions are unfavorable, the wisest course is to restrain oneself, say less, do less, and preserve one's position. Though no glory comes, neither does disaster. In modern terms, this is like keeping a low profile during corporate upheaval—not taking sides, not making declarations, watching and waiting.

lines.textZh: 六五：黄裳，元吉。
lines.textEn: Six in the fifth place: A yellow lower garment brings supreme good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 黄色下裳，谦逊居中，大吉大利。
lines.interpretationEn: Yellow garment symbolizes modesty and centrality. Supreme good fortune.
lines.detailZh: 六五是坤卦的尊位，阴爻居阳位，以柔居刚，象征以谦逊的姿态处于高位。“黄裳，元吉”中“黄”为中央之色，代表中庸之道；“裳”为下衣，代表谦逊居下。身居高位却保持谦逊中庸，这是最吉祥的状态。历史上许多成功的辅政者都体现了这一爻的精神——如周公辅成王、诸葛亮辅刘禅，身居要职却从不僭越，以“黄裳”之德赢得千古美名。
lines.detailEn: The fifth six occupies Kun's honored position—a yin line in a yang place, softness dwelling in firmness, symbolizing humility in high position. In 'A yellow lower garment brings supreme good fortune,' yellow represents the color of the center and the middle way, while the lower garment represents modesty and deference. Maintaining humility and moderation while in a position of power is the most auspicious state. Many successful regents throughout history embodied this line's spirit—like the Duke of Zhou assisting King Cheng, or Zhuge Liang serving Liu Shan, holding great power yet never overstepping bounds.

lines.textZh: 上六：龙战于野，其血玄黄。
lines.textEn: Six at the top: Dragons fight in the meadow. Their blood is black and yellow.
lines.interpretationZh: 阴极生变，两龙相争，阴阳交战，有所损伤。
lines.interpretationEn: Yin reaches its extreme. Conflict arises with inevitable losses.
lines.detailZh: 上六是坤卦最高一爻，阴气已达极盛，与乾卦的“亢龙有悔”相呼应。“龙战于野，其血玄黄”描绘了阴阳激烈冲突的惨烈场景——阴气过盛到极点，必然与阳气发生正面冲突，两败俱伤。“玄黄”即天地之色（天玄地黄），象征天地秩序被打破。这一爻警示我们：任何力量发展到极端都会引发冲突。柔顺过度就变成了固执，包容过度就变成了纵容。保持阴阳平衡，才是长久之道。
lines.detailEn: The top six is the highest line of Kun, where yin energy has reached its absolute peak, echoing Qian's 'arrogant dragon will have cause to repent.' 'Dragons fight in the meadow. Their blood is black and yellow' depicts the devastating scene of extreme yin-yang conflict—when yin energy reaches its zenith, it inevitably clashes head-on with yang, resulting in mutual destruction. 'Black and yellow' are the colors of heaven and earth, symbolizing the shattering of cosmic order. This line warns that any force pushed to its extreme will provoke conflict. Excessive yielding becomes stubbornness; excessive tolerance becomes enabling. Maintaining yin-yang balance is the only lasting path.

overviewZh: 坤卦是《周易》六十四卦之第二卦，由六个阴爻组成，纯阴至柔，象征大地承载万物。坤为地，代表宇宙间最深厚、最包容的滋养力量。如果说乾卦是创造的源头，坤卦则是孕育的母体——万物依赖大地而生长，却从不见大地自夸其功。坤卦的核心精神是“厚德载物”——大地沉默而宽广，以无限的包容承载一切。坤卦强调顺势而为、柔中有刚，不是被动的服从，而是以柔克刚的大智慧。
overviewEn: Kun (The Receptive) is the second hexagram of the I Ching, composed of six broken yin lines representing pure receptive power. It symbolizes the earth and the nurturing force that sustains all life. If Qian is the source of creation, Kun is the womb of cultivation—all things depend on the earth to grow, yet the earth never boasts of its contributions. The core spirit of Kun is 'supporting all things with great virtue'—the earth is silent and vast, bearing everything with infinite tolerance. Kun emphasizes following the natural flow and finding strength within gentleness, not passive submission but the great wisdom of overcoming hardness with softness.
judgmentDetailZh: 坤卦卦辞“元亨，利牝马之贞。君子有攸往，先迷后得主，利。西南得朋，东北丧朋，安贞吉”蕴含深刻的处世哲学。“牝马”即母马，性情柔顺而善于奔走，象征坤卦柔顺而不失行动力的品质。“先迷后得主”告诫我们：坤道以辅佐为本，不宜争先，初时可能迷失方向，但只要坚守正道，终将找到值得追随的方向。“西南得朋”指同类相聚，“东北丧朋”指与异类分离——这是告诉我们要善于选择同道中人。《彖传》释曰：“至哉坤元，万物资生，乃顺承天”，坤的伟大在于它是万物生长的根基，顺应天道而成就一切。
judgmentDetailEn: The judgment of Kun—'Supreme success, furthering through the perseverance of a mare. If the superior man undertakes something and tries to lead, he goes astray; but if he follows, he finds guidance'—contains profound philosophy. The mare symbolizes Kun's quality of being gentle yet capable of action. 'First confusion, then finding the master' teaches that the way of Kun is to support rather than lead; one may initially lose direction, but by holding to the right path, one will find worthy guidance. 'In the southwest, find friends; in the northeast, lose friends' advises choosing companions wisely. The Commentary states: 'Perfect indeed is the sublimity of the Receptive. All beings owe their birth to it, because it receives the heavenly with devotion.'
imageDetailZh: 象辞“地势坤，君子以厚德载物”与乾卦的“自强不息”并称为中华文明最核心的两大精神。“地势坤”描述大地的特质——低处而厚重，沉稳而广阔，默默承载山川河流、万物生灵。“厚德载物”则是从地道推及人道：有德行的人应当效法大地的精神，以宽厚的品德包容万事万物。这里的“厚德”不是软弱，而是一种深沉的力量——能容纳不同意见，能承受巨大压力，能在沉默中滋养万物。清华大学校训的后半句正是取自此处，与乾卦的“自强不息”共同构成刚柔并济的完整人格理想。
imageDetailEn: The Image text 'The earth's condition is receptive devotion; thus the superior man who has breadth of character carries the outer world' stands alongside Qian's 'tireless self-strengthening' as the two most fundamental spiritual principles of Chinese civilization. 'The earth's condition' describes the nature of the ground—low yet substantial, steady yet vast, silently bearing mountains, rivers, and all living things. 'Carrying the outer world with breadth of character' extends this earthly principle to human conduct: a person of virtue should emulate the earth's spirit, embracing all things with generous virtue. This 'breadth of character' is not weakness but a profound strength—the capacity to accommodate different views, withstand great pressure, and nurture all things in silence.
modernApplicationZh: 【事业】坤卦提示在职场中要善于辅佐和配合，不必事事争先。作为团队成员，发挥支撑作用往往比争当领导更有价值；作为管理者，要学会倾听和包容，以柔性管理凝聚团队。“先迷后得主”提醒我们：初入新环境时不要急于表现，先观察学习，找到正确的方向再发力。【感情】坤卦代表包容和滋养的能量，提示在关系中要学会倾听、理解和支持对方。不是一味迁就，而是以温柔的力量化解矛盾。“厚德载物”的精神用在感情中，就是用宽广的胸怀接纳对方的不完美。【财运】坤卦象征稳健和积累，适合长期投资、稳扎稳打，不宜冒进投机。像大地一样厚积薄发，财富自然水到渠成。【健康】坤卦对应脾胃和腹部，提醒注意消化系统保养，饮食规律，避免过度操劳。效法大地的沉稳，保持心态平和。
modernApplicationEn: 【Career】Kun advises excelling in support and collaboration rather than always competing for the lead. As a team member, playing a supporting role often creates more value than vying for leadership. 'First confusion, then finding guidance' reminds us not to rush to prove ourselves in new environments—observe, learn, then act.【Love】Kun represents nurturing energy, suggesting we listen, understand, and support our partners. Not blind accommodation, but resolving conflicts with gentle strength.【Wealth】Kun symbolizes stability and accumulation, favoring long-term investment and steady progress over speculation. Like the earth building up gradually, wealth comes naturally through patience.【Health】Kun corresponds to the spleen, stomach, and abdomen—maintain regular eating habits and avoid overwork. Emulate the earth's steadiness and keep a peaceful mindset.
historicalStoryZh: 坤卦最著名的典故与周文王有关。相传周文王被商纣王囚禁于羑里七年，正是在这段至暗时刻，他深悟坤卦“厚德载物”的道理——以大地般的忍耐承受苦难，韬光养晦，最终为周朝八百年基业奠定了根基。另一个典故是大禹治水。大禹效法坤卦“顺势而为”的智慧，不与洪水硬抗，而是疏导引流，顺应水性，终于平定水患。这正是坤卦“柔顺”精神的最佳体现——不是软弱的屈服，而是顺应自然规律的大智慧。
historicalStoryEn: The most famous story associated with Kun involves King Wen of Zhou. Imprisoned by the tyrant King Zhou of Shang for seven years at Youli, King Wen deeply understood Kun's principle of 'bearing all with great virtue'—enduring suffering with earth-like patience, biding his time, ultimately laying the foundation for the 800-year Zhou dynasty. Another classic story is Yu the Great's flood control. Rather than fighting the waters head-on, Yu followed Kun's wisdom of 'going with the flow,' channeling and redirecting the floods according to water's natural tendencies. This perfectly embodies Kun's spirit of yielding—not weak submission, but the great wisdom of working with natural laws.
relatedHexagramsNote: 坤卦的错卦为乾卦（第1卦），阴阳完全相反，刚柔互补；综卦亦为坤卦本身（上下颠倒不变）；互卦为坤卦（取二三四五爻组成的内卦仍为纯阴）。乾坤两卦互为表里，共同构成《周易》的根基，一刚一柔，一创一育。

judgmentZh: 屯：元，亨，利，贞，勿用有攸往，利建侯。
judgmentEn: Difficulty at the Beginning works supreme success, furthering through perseverance. Nothing should be undertaken. It furthers one to appoint helpers.
imageZh: 云雷屯，君子以经纶。
imageEn: Clouds and thunder: the image of Difficulty at the Beginning. Thus the superior man brings order out of confusion.
interpretationZh: 屯卦象征初生的艰难，如草木破土而出。万事开头难，但蕴含生机，宜坚守正道。
interpretationEn: Zhun symbolizes the difficulty of new beginnings, like a sprout breaking through earth. Persevere through initial hardship.

lines.textZh: 初九：磐桓，利居贞，利建侯。
lines.textEn: Nine at the beginning: Hesitation and hindrance. It furthers one to remain persevering. It furthers one to appoint helpers.
lines.interpretationZh: 徘徊不前，宜坚守正道，寻求帮助。
lines.interpretationEn: Hesitation at the start. Stay firm and seek capable helpers.

lines.textZh: 六二：屯如邅如，乘马班如。匪寇婚媾，女子贞不字，十年乃字。
lines.textEn: Six in the second place: Difficulties pile up. Horse and wagon part. He is not a robber; he wants to woo.
lines.interpretationZh: 困难重重，但来者并非敌人而是求婚者，耐心等待终有好结果。
lines.interpretationEn: Difficulties mount but the stranger comes in peace. Patience brings union.

lines.textZh: 六三：即鹿无虞，惟入于林中，君子几不如舍，往吝。
lines.textEn: Six in the third place: Whoever hunts deer without the forester only loses his way in the forest.
lines.interpretationZh: 没有向导就去追鹿，只会迷失在林中，不如放弃。
lines.interpretationEn: Hunting without a guide leads to getting lost. Better to let go.

lines.textZh: 六四：乘马班如，求婚媾，往吉，无不利。
lines.textEn: Six in the fourth place: Horse and wagon part. Strive for union. To go brings good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 虽有阻碍，但主动出击求合作，前往则吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: Despite obstacles, actively seek partnership. Going forward brings fortune.

lines.textZh: 九五：屯其膏，小贞吉，大贞凶。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fifth place: Difficulties in blessing. A little perseverance brings good fortune. Great perseverance brings misfortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 恩泽难以施展，小事坚持则吉，大事强求则凶。
lines.interpretationEn: Blessings are blocked. Small perseverance succeeds; forcing big plans fails.

lines.textZh: 上六：乘马班如，泣血涟如。
lines.textEn: Six at the top: Horse and wagon part. Bloody tears flow.
lines.interpretationZh: 进退两难，悲痛流泪，处境极为艰难。
lines.interpretationEn: Stuck in despair with tears of blood. An extremely difficult situation.

judgmentZh: 蒙：亨。匪我求童蒙，童蒙求我。初筮告，再三渎，渎则不告。利贞。
judgmentEn: Youthful Folly has success. It is not I who seek the young fool; the young fool seeks me. At the first oracle I inform him. If he asks two or three times, it is importunity. I give no information to the importunate. Perseverance furthers.
imageZh: 山下出泉，蒙；君子以果行育德。
imageEn: A spring wells up at the foot of the mountain: the image of Youth. Thus the superior man fosters his character by thoroughness in all that he does.
interpretationZh: 蒙卦象征蒙昧初开，如山下泉水初涌。启蒙教育需因势利导，学生主动求教方可施教。
interpretationEn: Meng symbolizes youthful ignorance, like a spring emerging from a mountain. Education requires the student to seek the teacher.

lines.textZh: 初六：发蒙，利用刑人，用说桎梏，以往吝。
lines.textEn: Six at the beginning: To make a fool develop, it furthers one to apply discipline.
lines.interpretationZh: 启发蒙昧，需要适当的纪律约束，但不可过度。
lines.interpretationEn: Discipline helps develop the young, but do not overdo it.

lines.textZh: 九二：包蒙吉；纳妇吉；子克家。
lines.textEn: Nine in the second place: To bear with fools in kindliness brings good fortune. To know how to take women brings good fortune. The son is capable of taking charge of the household.
lines.interpretationZh: 包容蒙昧者则吉，以宽厚之心教导，如同治家有方。
lines.interpretationEn: Bearing with the foolish in kindness brings fortune. Lead with tolerance.

lines.textZh: 六三：勿用取女，见金夫，不有躬，无攸利。
lines.textEn: Six in the third place: Take not a maiden who loses her composure at the sight of a man of bronze.
lines.interpretationZh: 不要取没有定力的女子，见利忘义者不可信任。
lines.interpretationEn: Do not trust those who lose composure at the sight of wealth.

lines.textZh: 六四：困蒙，吝。
lines.textEn: Six in the fourth place: Entangled folly brings humiliation.
lines.interpretationZh: 困于蒙昧之中，闭塞不通，有所困窘。
lines.interpretationEn: Trapped in ignorance leads to humiliation. Seek to break free.

lines.textZh: 六五：童蒙，吉。
lines.textEn: Six in the fifth place: Childlike folly brings good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 保持童真的求学之心，虚心受教则吉。
lines.interpretationEn: Childlike openness to learning brings good fortune.

lines.textZh: 上九：击蒙；不利为寇，利御寇。
lines.textEn: Nine at the top: In punishing folly, it does not further one to commit transgressions. The only thing that furthers is to prevent transgressions.
lines.interpretationZh: 惩戒蒙昧不可过度，防患于未然才是正道。
lines.interpretationEn: Punishing ignorance should not go too far. Prevention is better than punishment.

judgmentZh: 需：有孚，光亨，贞吉。利涉大川。
judgmentEn: Waiting. If you are sincere, you have light and success. Perseverance brings good fortune. It furthers one to cross the great water.
imageZh: 云上于天，需；君子以饮食宴乐。
imageEn: Clouds rise up to heaven: the image of Waiting. Thus the superior man eats and drinks, is joyous and of good cheer.
interpretationZh: 需卦象征等待，云气上升于天尚未成雨。有诚信则前途光明，宜耐心等待时机。
interpretationEn: Xu symbolizes waiting. Clouds rise but rain has not yet come. Sincerity and patience lead to success.

lines.textZh: 初九：需于郊，利用恒，无咎。
lines.textEn: Nine at the beginning: Waiting in the meadow. It furthers one to abide in what endures. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 在郊外等待，保持恒心，不会有过失。
lines.interpretationEn: Waiting in the open. Maintain constancy and there will be no blame.

lines.textZh: 九二：需于沙，小有言，终吉。
lines.textEn: Nine in the second place: Waiting on the sand. There is some gossip. The end brings good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 在沙滩上等待，虽有小口舌，最终吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: Waiting on the sand. Minor talk arises but the outcome is fortunate.

lines.textZh: 九三：需于泥，致寇至。
lines.textEn: Nine in the third place: Waiting in the mud brings about the arrival of the enemy.
lines.interpretationZh: 在泥泞中等待，招致敌人到来，处境危险。
lines.interpretationEn: Waiting in the mud invites danger. The enemy approaches.

lines.textZh: 六四：需于血，出自穴。
lines.textEn: Six in the fourth place: Waiting in blood. Get out of the pit.
lines.interpretationZh: 身处血泊之中，需从险境中脱身。
lines.interpretationEn: Waiting amid blood. Escape from the dangerous pit.

lines.textZh: 九五：需于酒食，贞吉。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fifth place: Waiting at meat and drink. Perseverance brings good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 在酒食宴席中等待，安享其成，坚守正道则吉。
lines.interpretationEn: Waiting with food and drink. Enjoy and persevere for good fortune.

lines.textZh: 上六：入于穴，有不速之客三人来，敬之终吉。
lines.textEn: Six at the top: One falls into the pit. Three uninvited guests arrive. Honor them, and in the end there will be good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 落入洞穴，有三位不速之客到来，以礼相待则终获吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: Falling into the pit. Three unexpected guests arrive. Treat them with respect for good fortune.

judgmentZh: 讼：有孚，窒惕，中吉，终凶。利见大人，不利涉大川。
judgmentEn: Conflict. You are sincere and are being obstructed. A cautious halt halfway brings good fortune. Going through to the end brings misfortune. It furthers one to see the great man. It does not further one to cross the great water.
imageZh: 天与水违行，讼；君子以作事谋始。
imageEn: Heaven and water go their opposite ways: the image of Conflict. Thus in all his transactions the superior man carefully considers the beginning.
interpretationZh: 讼卦象征争讼，天水相违背。有诚信但受阻，中途停止则吉，执意到底则凶。
interpretationEn: Song symbolizes conflict. Heaven and water move in opposite directions. Stop halfway for fortune; pushing to the end brings misfortune.

lines.textZh: 初六：不永所事，小有言，终吉。
lines.textEn: Six at the beginning: If one does not perpetuate the affair, there is a little gossip. In the end, good fortune comes.
lines.interpretationZh: 不要把争讼之事持续下去，虽有小口舌，最终吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: Do not prolong the conflict. Despite minor gossip, the end is fortunate.

lines.textZh: 九二：不克讼，归而逋，其邑人三百户，无眚。
lines.textEn: Nine in the second place: One cannot engage in conflict. One returns home, gives way. The people of his town, three hundred households, remain free of guilt.
lines.interpretationZh: 争讼不胜，退回家乡避让，可保一方平安。
lines.interpretationEn: Unable to win the conflict, retreat home. This protects your community.

lines.textZh: 六三：食旧德，贞厉，终吉。或从王事，无成。
lines.textEn: Six in the third place: To nourish oneself on ancient virtue induces perseverance. Danger. In the end, good fortune comes.
lines.interpretationZh: 依靠旧有的德行生活，虽有危险但终获吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: Live on past virtues. Danger exists but the end brings fortune.

lines.textZh: 九四：不克讼，复即命，渝，安贞吉。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fourth place: One cannot engage in conflict. One turns back and submits to fate, changes one's attitude, and finds peace in perseverance. Good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 争讼不胜，回归正命，改变态度，安守正道则吉。
lines.interpretationEn: Cannot win the conflict. Return to fate, change attitude, find peace.

lines.textZh: 九五：讼，元吉。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fifth place: To contend before him brings supreme good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 在公正的裁判面前争讼，大吉大利。
lines.interpretationEn: Contending before a just judge brings supreme good fortune.

lines.textZh: 上九：或锡之鞶带，终朝三褫之。
lines.textEn: Nine at the top: Even if by chance a leather belt is bestowed on one, by the end of a morning it will have been snatched away three times.
lines.interpretationZh: 即使侥幸获赏，也会被反复夺去，争讼到底终无好结果。
lines.interpretationEn: Even if rewarded, it will be taken away repeatedly. Conflict to the end fails.

judgmentZh: 师：贞，丈人吉，无咎。
judgmentEn: The Army. Perseverance and a strong man bring good fortune and no blame.
imageZh: 地中有水，师；君子以容民畜众。
imageEn: In the middle of the earth is water: the image of the Army. Thus the superior man increases his masses by generosity toward the people.
interpretationZh: 师卦象征军队，地中有水，蓄势待发。需要德高望重之人统帅，方可无咎。
interpretationEn: Shi symbolizes the army. Water within the earth gathers strength. A worthy leader is needed for success.

lines.textZh: 初六：师出以律，否臧凶。
lines.textEn: Six at the beginning: An army must set forth in proper order. If the order is not good, misfortune threatens.
lines.interpretationZh: 出师必须有纪律，纪律不严则凶。
lines.interpretationEn: The army must march in order. Without discipline, misfortune follows.

lines.textZh: 九二：在师中，吉无咎，王三锡命。
lines.textEn: Nine in the second place: In the midst of the army. Good fortune. No blame. The king bestows a triple decoration.
lines.interpretationZh: 身在军中，吉利无咎，得到君王多次嘉奖。
lines.interpretationEn: In the midst of the army. Fortune and royal recognition.

lines.textZh: 六三：师或舆尸，凶。
lines.textEn: Six in the third place: Perchance the army carries corpses in the wagon. Misfortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 军队可能载着尸体回来，凶险。
lines.interpretationEn: The army may carry corpses home. Misfortune.

lines.textZh: 六四：师左次，无咎。
lines.textEn: Six in the fourth place: The army retreats. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 军队向左后退驻扎，无过失。
lines.interpretationEn: The army retreats to camp. No blame in strategic withdrawal.

lines.textZh: 六五：田有禽，利执言，无咎。长子帅师，弟子舆尸，贞凶。
lines.textEn: Six in the fifth place: There is game in the field. It furthers one to catch it. No blame. Let the eldest lead the army. The younger transports corpses; then perseverance brings misfortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 田中有猎物可捕获，应让长子统帅军队，若用幼子则凶。
lines.interpretationEn: Game in the field may be caught. Let the eldest lead; the young bring misfortune.

lines.textZh: 上六：大君有命，开国承家，小人勿用。
lines.textEn: Six at the top: The great prince issues commands, founds states, vests families with fiefs. Inferior people should not be employed.
lines.interpretationZh: 大君颁布命令，分封建国，但不可任用小人。
lines.interpretationEn: The great prince issues commands and founds states. Do not employ petty people.

judgmentZh: 比：吉。原筮元永贞，无咎。不宁方来，后夫凶。
judgmentEn: Holding Together brings good fortune. Inquire of the oracle once again whether you possess sublimity, constancy, and perseverance; then there is no blame. Those who are uncertain gradually join. Whoever comes too late meets with misfortune.
imageZh: 地上有水，比；先王以建万国，亲诸侯。
imageEn: On the earth is water: the image of Holding Together. Thus the kings of antiquity bestowed the different states as fiefs and cultivated friendly relations with the feudal lords.
interpretationZh: 比卦象征亲附团结，地上有水，水依附于地。宜寻求团结，但迟来者不利。
interpretationEn: Bi symbolizes union and holding together. Water on earth flows together. Seek unity, but latecomers face misfortune.

lines.textZh: 初六：有孚比之，无咎。有孚盈缶，终来有它，吉。
lines.textEn: Six at the beginning: Hold to him in truth and loyalty; this is without blame. Truth, like a full earthen bowl. Thus in the end good fortune comes from without.
lines.interpretationZh: 以诚信亲附，无过失。诚信满溢如缶，终有意外之喜。
lines.interpretationEn: Hold together with sincerity. Truth overflows like a full bowl, bringing fortune.

lines.textZh: 六二：比之自内，贞吉。
lines.textEn: Six in the second place: Hold to him inwardly. Perseverance brings good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 从内心亲附，坚守正道则吉。
lines.interpretationEn: Hold together from within. Inner perseverance brings good fortune.

lines.textZh: 六三：比之匪人。
lines.textEn: Six in the third place: You hold together with the wrong people.
lines.interpretationZh: 亲附了不该亲附的人，交友不慎。
lines.interpretationEn: Holding together with the wrong people. Be careful in choosing allies.

lines.textZh: 六四：外比之，贞吉。
lines.textEn: Six in the fourth place: Hold to him outwardly also. Perseverance brings good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 从外部亲附贤者，坚守正道则吉。
lines.interpretationEn: Hold together outwardly with the worthy. Perseverance brings fortune.

lines.textZh: 九五：显比，王用三驱，失前禽。邑人不诫，吉。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fifth place: Manifestation of holding together. In the hunt the king uses beaters on three sides only and forgoes game that runs off in front. The citizens need no warning. Good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 光明正大地团结，如君王狩猎三面围合留一面，不强求，吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: Open union. Like the king hunting with three beaters, letting some escape. No coercion brings fortune.

lines.textZh: 上六：比之无首，凶。
lines.textEn: Six at the top: Holding together without a head. Misfortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 团结没有领头人，群龙无首，凶险。
lines.interpretationEn: Union without a leader. Leaderless groups face misfortune.

nameEn: The Taming Power of the Small
judgmentZh: 小畜：亨。密云不雨，自我西郊。
judgmentEn: The Taming Power of the Small has success. Dense clouds, no rain from our western region.
imageZh: 风行天上，小畜；君子以懿文德。
imageEn: The wind drives across heaven: the image of The Taming Power of the Small. Thus the superior man refines the outward aspect of his nature.
interpretationZh: 小畜卦象征以柔蓄刚，风行天上，力量虽小但能积聚。密云未雨，蓄势待发，宜修养文德。
interpretationEn: Xiao Xu symbolizes the small restraining the great. Wind blows across heaven, gathering force. Cultivate virtue while awaiting the right moment.

lines.textZh: 初九：复自道，何其咎，吉。
lines.textEn: Nine at the beginning: Return to the way. How could there be blame in this? Good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 回归正道，有何过失？吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: Returning to one's own path. How could this bring blame? Good fortune.

lines.textZh: 九二：牵复，吉。
lines.textEn: Nine in the second place: He allows himself to be drawn into returning. Good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 被牵引而回归正道，吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: Drawn back to the right path by another. Good fortune.

lines.textZh: 九三：舆说辐，夫妻反目。
lines.textEn: Nine in the third place: The spokes burst out of the wagon wheels. Man and wife roll their eyes.
lines.interpretationZh: 车轮辐条脱落，夫妻反目不和，进退失据。
lines.interpretationEn: The wagon breaks down. Husband and wife turn against each other.

lines.textZh: 六四：有孚，血去惕出，无咎。
lines.textEn: Six in the fourth place: If you are sincere, blood vanishes and fear gives way. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 心怀诚信，忧患消除，恐惧远去，无过失。
lines.interpretationEn: With sincerity, bloodshed is averted and anxiety departs. No blame.

lines.textZh: 九五：有孚挛如，富以其邻。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fifth place: If you are sincere and loyally attached, you are rich in your neighbor.
lines.interpretationZh: 以诚信紧密相连，与邻里共享富裕。
lines.interpretationEn: Bound in sincerity and loyalty, sharing wealth with neighbors.

lines.textZh: 上九：既雨既处，尚德载，妇贞厉。月几望，君子征凶。
lines.textEn: Nine at the top: The rain comes, there is rest. This is due to the lasting effect of character. Perseverance brings the woman into danger. The moon is nearly full. If the superior man persists, misfortune comes.
lines.interpretationZh: 雨已降下，蓄积已满。德行已载，妇人守正则有危险。月近满盈，君子此时出征则凶。
lines.interpretationEn: Rain falls and rest comes. Virtue has accumulated. The moon is nearly full; the superior man should not press forward now.

judgmentZh: 履虎尾，不咥人，亨。
judgmentEn: Treading upon the tail of the tiger. It does not bite the man. Success.
imageZh: 上天下泽，履；君子以辨上下，定民志。
imageEn: Heaven above, the lake below: the image of Treading. Thus the superior man discriminates between high and low, and thereby fortifies the thinking of the people.
interpretationZh: 履卦象征行为礼仪，天在上泽在下，尊卑有序。踩到虎尾而不被咬，以礼行事则亨通。
interpretationEn: Lü symbolizes proper conduct. Heaven above, lake below — order is maintained. Treading carefully with propriety brings success even in danger.

lines.textZh: 初九：素履往，无咎。
lines.textEn: Nine at the beginning: Simple conduct. Progress without blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 以朴素的态度前行，不会有过失。
lines.interpretationEn: Simple, unadorned conduct. Moving forward brings no blame.

lines.textZh: 九二：履道坦坦，幽人贞吉。
lines.textEn: Nine in the second place: Treading a smooth, level course. The perseverance of a dark man brings good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 行走在平坦的大道上，隐逸之人坚守正道则吉。
lines.interpretationEn: Walking a smooth and level path. A quiet person's perseverance brings fortune.

lines.textZh: 六三：眇能视，跛能履，履虎尾，咥人，凶。武人为于大君。
lines.textEn: Six in the third place: A one-eyed man is able to see, a lame man is able to tread. He treads on the tail of the tiger. The tiger bites the man. Misfortune. Thus does a warrior act on behalf of his great prince.
lines.interpretationZh: 独眼勉强能看，跛足勉强能行，却踩了虎尾被咬，凶险。能力不足而强行冒进。
lines.interpretationEn: A one-eyed man tries to see, a lame man tries to walk. Treading on the tiger's tail — it bites. Overreaching brings misfortune.

lines.textZh: 九四：履虎尾，愬愬终吉。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fourth place: He treads on the tail of the tiger. Caution and circumspection lead ultimately to good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 踩到虎尾，小心翼翼，最终吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: Treading on the tiger's tail with utmost caution. In the end, good fortune.

lines.textZh: 九五：夬履，贞厉。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fifth place: Resolute conduct. Perseverance with awareness of danger.
lines.interpretationZh: 果断行事，坚守正道但须警惕危险。
lines.interpretationEn: Resolute treading. Persevere but remain aware of danger.

lines.textZh: 上九：视履考祥，其旋元吉。
lines.textEn: Nine at the top: Look to your conduct and weigh the favorable signs. When everything is fulfilled, supreme good fortune comes.
lines.interpretationZh: 回顾自己的行为，审视吉凶征兆，周全圆满则大吉。
lines.interpretationEn: Look back on your conduct and examine the omens. When all is complete, supreme good fortune.

judgmentZh: 泰：小往大来，吉亨。
judgmentEn: Peace. The small departs, the great approaches. Good fortune. Success.
imageZh: 天地交，泰；后以财成天地之道，辅相天地之宜，以左右民。
imageEn: Heaven and earth unite: the image of Peace. Thus the ruler divides and completes the course of heaven and earth; he furthers and regulates the gifts of heaven and earth, and so aids the people.
interpretationZh: 泰卦象征通泰安宁，天地交感，阴阳和谐。小人退去，君子到来，万事亨通。
interpretationEn: Tai symbolizes peace and harmony. Heaven and earth commune, yin and yang are in balance. The petty depart and the great arrive.

lines.textZh: 初九：拔茅茹，以其汇，征吉。
lines.textEn: Nine at the beginning: When ribbon grass is pulled up, the sod comes with it. Each according to his kind. Undertakings bring good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 拔起茅草连根带土，同类相随，出征则吉。
lines.interpretationEn: Pulling up ribbon grass brings the sod with it. Like attracts like. Undertakings bring fortune.
lines.detailZh: 初九“拔茅茹，以其汇，征吉”以拔茅草为喻：拔起一根茅草，根系相连带出一丛。这象征志同道合的人相互牵引、共同前进。泰卦初爻阳气初动，正是事业起步的好时机，而且不是孤军奋战——你的行动会带动一批同道中人。“征吉”鼓励积极行动，此时出发必然吉祥。
lines.detailEn: The first nine uses the image of pulling up ribbon grass—one stalk brings up a whole cluster by the roots, symbolizing like-minded people advancing together. Yang energy has just begun to stir; this is an excellent time to start, and you won't be alone—your action will rally fellow travelers. 'Advancing brings good fortune.'

lines.textZh: 九二：包荒，用冯河，不遐遗，朋亡，得尚于中行。
lines.textEn: Nine in the second place: Bearing with the uncultured in gentleness, fording the river with resolution, not neglecting what is distant, not regarding one's companions: thus one may manage to walk in the middle.
lines.interpretationZh: 包容荒芜，果断渡河，不遗远方，不结朋党，行中庸之道。
lines.interpretationEn: Bear with the uncultured, ford the river boldly, neglect nothing distant, avoid cliques. Walk the middle path.
lines.detailZh: 九二“包荒，用冯河，不遐遗，朋亡，得尚于中行”描述了泰世领导者应有的四种品质：“包荒”——包容荒芜之地（宽容大度）；“用冯河”——敢于徒步渡河（果敢决断）；“不遐遗”——不遗忘远方的人（关怀周全）；“朋亡”——不结党营私（公正无私）。做到这四点，就能“得尚于中行”——获得中庸之道的最高境界。
lines.detailEn: The second nine describes four qualities of a leader in times of peace: 'embracing the uncultivated' (broad tolerance), 'fording the river' (bold decisiveness), 'not neglecting the distant' (comprehensive care), and 'losing companions' (impartial fairness). Achieving all four attains the highest level of the middle way.

lines.textZh: 九三：无平不陂，无往不复。艰贞无咎。勿恤其孚，于食有福。
lines.textEn: Nine in the third place: No plain not followed by a slope. No going not followed by a return. He who remains persevering in danger is without blame. Do not complain about this truth; enjoy the good fortune you still possess.
lines.interpretationZh: 没有平地不变成斜坡，没有去而不返。艰难中坚守正道则无咎，享受当下的福分。
lines.interpretationEn: No plain without a slope, no departure without return. Persevere through hardship without blame. Enjoy present blessings.
lines.detailZh: 九三“无平不陂，无往不复。艰贞无咎。勿恤其孚，于食有福”是泰卦最重要的警示。“无平不陂”——没有永远平坦的路，平地终会变成斜坡；“无往不复”——没有一去不返的事，去了终会回来。这是“泰极否来”的预言：太平盛世不会永远持续，要在顺境中保持警觉。只要“艰贞”（在艰难中坚守正道），就不会有过失。
lines.detailEn: The third nine delivers Tai's most important warning: 'No plain not followed by a slope, no going not followed by a return.' No road stays flat forever; no departure lacks a return. This prophesies that peace will eventually turn to stagnation—maintain vigilance during good times. As long as one 'perseveres through difficulty,' there will be no blame.

lines.textZh: 六四：翩翩不富，以其邻，不戒以孚。
lines.textEn: Six in the fourth place: He flutters down, not boasting of his wealth, together with his neighbor, guileless and sincere.
lines.interpretationZh: 翩然而下，不以富有自夸，与邻里坦诚相待，不设防而以诚信相交。
lines.interpretationEn: Fluttering down without boasting of wealth. Meeting neighbors with sincerity and without pretense.
lines.detailZh: 六四“翩翩，不富以其邻，不戒以孚”描绘了上卦阴爻开始活跃的景象。“翩翩”形容轻快飘动，阴气开始向下运动，与下方的阳气交融。“不富以其邻”意为不以财富笼络邻人，而是以真诚感化；“不戒以孚”意为不需要刻意防备，因为彼此信任。这是泰世最美好的状态——人与人之间以诚相待，不需要防备和算计。
lines.detailEn: The fourth six depicts yin lines becoming active, moving downward to mingle with yang below. 'Fluttering, not boasting of wealth to neighbors, not guarding but trusting'—in times of peace, people relate through sincerity rather than wealth, trust rather than suspicion. This is Tai's most beautiful state: genuine mutual trust without calculation.

lines.textZh: 六五：帝乙归妹，以祉元吉。
lines.textEn: Six in the fifth place: The sovereign I gives his daughter in marriage. This brings blessing and supreme good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 帝乙嫁女，带来福祉，大吉大利。
lines.interpretationEn: The sovereign gives his daughter in marriage. Blessing and supreme good fortune.
lines.detailZh: 六五“帝乙归妹，以祉元吉”引用了商朝帝乙嫁女的历史典故。帝乙将女儿下嫁给周文王的父亲季历，这是一次“上下交”的政治联姻——高贵的商朝公主嫁给地位较低的周族首领，体现了泰卦“尊者下交”的精神。“以祉元吉”意为因此获得大福大吉。这一爻告诉我们：真正的尊贵不在于高高在上，而在于愿意放下身段与人交流。
lines.detailEn: The fifth six references Emperor Yi of Shang marrying his daughter to the Zhou leader—a 'downward exchange' where nobility humbles itself to connect with those below. 'Great good fortune and blessing' results from this union. True nobility lies not in remaining aloof but in willingness to reach down and connect with others.

lines.textZh: 上六：城复于隍，勿用师。自邑告命，贞吝。
lines.textEn: Six at the top: The wall falls back into the moat. Use no army now. Make your commands known within your own town. Perseverance brings humiliation.
lines.interpretationZh: 城墙倒塌回填护城河，不可用兵。在自己城邑中发布命令，坚持则有困窘。
lines.interpretationEn: The wall falls into the moat. Do not use the army. Issue commands locally. Perseverance brings humiliation.
lines.detailZh: 上六“城复于隍，勿用师，自邑告命，贞吝”描绘了泰极否来的转折：城墙倒塌回到护城河中，象征太平盛世走向终结。此时不宜动用军队（勿用师），只能从自己的城邑发布命令（自邑告命），即使坚守正道也会困窘（贞吝）。这是泰卦最后的警告：盛世终将过去，当衰退的迹象出现时，要收缩防守而非盲目扩张。
lines.detailEn: The top six depicts the turning point from peace to stagnation: 'The wall falls back into the moat. Use no army now.' The city wall crumbles, symbolizing the end of prosperity. This is not the time for military action but for issuing orders from one's own city—contracting and defending rather than blindly expanding. Tai's final warning: prosperity must end; when signs of decline appear, consolidate rather than overreach.

overviewZh: 泰卦是《周易》第十一卦，由上坤（地）下乾（天）组成。天本在上、地本在下，但泰卦将天置于下、地置于上，象征天地之气相交融合。天气下降、地气上升，阴阳交感，万物通泰。这就是“否极泰来”中“泰”的本义——不是简单的好运，而是阴阳和谐、上下沟通、万物畅达的理想状态。泰卦的核心精神是“交通”——领导者放下身段倾听民意，天地之气相互流通，这才是真正的太平盛世。
overviewEn: Tai (Peace) is the 11th hexagram, composed of Kun (earth) above and Qian (heaven) below. Though heaven naturally belongs above and earth below, Tai reverses them—heaven's energy descends while earth's energy rises, creating the interchange that brings universal harmony. This is the true meaning of 'peace' in the I Ching: not mere good luck, but the ideal state where yin and yang harmonize, communication flows between high and low, and all things prosper. The core spirit of Tai is 'interchange'—leaders humble themselves to hear the people, energies circulate freely, creating genuine prosperity.
judgmentDetailZh: 泰卦卦辞“泰：小往大来，吉亨”中“小往大来”是关键。“小”指阴、指柔、指下；“大”指阳、指刚、指上。阴气（小）向外消散，阳气（大）向内聚集，正是万物生长、事业兴旺的征兆。《彖传》释曰：“天地交而万物通也，上下交而其志同也”——天地之气交融则万物通达，君臣上下沟通则志向一致。这揭示了泰卦最核心的治理智慧：真正的太平不是靠强权维持，而是靠上下之间的真诚沟通和相互理解。当领导者愿意倾听、下属敢于直言，组织才能真正兴旺。
judgmentDetailEn: The judgment 'Peace. The small departs, the great approaches. Good fortune. Success' contains a crucial concept. 'Small' refers to yin, softness, the lower; 'great' refers to yang, strength, the upper. Yin energy disperses outward while yang energy gathers inward—signs of growth and prosperity. The Commentary explains: 'Heaven and earth unite, and all things communicate; high and low unite, and their wills are the same.' True peace is not maintained by force but through genuine communication and mutual understanding between leaders and followers.
imageDetailZh: 象辞“天地交，泰。后以财成天地之道，辅相天地之宜，以左右民”中的“后”指君主。“财成”意为裁制成就，“辅相”意为辅助完成。君主应当顺应天地交融的规律，裁制和完善治理之道，辅助天地化育万物，以此来引导和帮助百姓。这段象辞揭示了泰卦的治理哲学：好的领导者不是凌驾于自然规律之上，而是顺应规律、因势利导，让每个人都能在合适的位置上发挥作用。这与现代管理学中“仆人式领导”的理念不谋而合。
imageDetailEn: The Image states that the ruler should follow the way of heaven and earth's union, shaping and perfecting governance to assist nature's nurturing of all things, thereby guiding the people. This reveals Tai's governance philosophy: good leaders don't impose themselves above natural laws but follow them, guiding by circumstance and enabling everyone to contribute from their proper position. This remarkably aligns with modern 'servant leadership' concepts.
modernApplicationZh: 【事业】泰卦是事业发展最吉利的卦象之一。“天地交”提示团队内部沟通顺畅是成功的关键——打破层级壁垒，让信息自由流通。领导者要“下交”于员工，员工要敢于“上达”建议。此时适合扩张、招聘、开拓新市场。【感情】泰卦代表关系中最和谐的状态——双方坦诚相待、心意相通。如果正在追求某人，泰卦鼓励主动表达；如果已在关系中，这是加深感情、共同规划未来的好时机。【财运】“小往大来”预示投入小而回报大，是投资的好时机。但要注意泰极否来的规律，在顺利时就要为可能的逆转做准备。【健康】泰卦对应身体的整体协调状态，气血通畅、阴阳平衡。适合开始新的健身计划或调理方案，身体会有良好的响应。
modernApplicationEn: 【Career】Tai is one of the most auspicious hexagrams for career development. 'Heaven and earth unite' suggests that smooth internal communication is key to success—break down hierarchical barriers and let information flow freely. This is a good time for expansion and new markets.【Love】Tai represents the most harmonious state in relationships—mutual openness and understanding. If pursuing someone, Tai encourages expressing your feelings; if already together, it's a great time to deepen bonds and plan the future.【Wealth】'The small departs, the great approaches' suggests small investments yielding large returns. But remember that peace can turn to stagnation—prepare for potential reversals during good times.【Health】Tai corresponds to overall bodily harmony with smooth qi and blood flow. A good time to start new fitness or wellness programs; the body will respond well.
historicalStoryZh: 泰卦最著名的典故是“否极泰来”，出自《周易》泰否两卦的相互转化。历史上最经典的“否极泰来”案例是越王勾践。勾践被吴王夫差打败后，卧薪尝胆二十年，从“否”的绝境中一步步走向“泰”的复兴，最终灭吴称霸。另一个典故是唐太宗李世民的“贞观之治”。李世民深谙泰卦“上下交”的道理，广开言路、虚心纳谏，魏征等诤臣敢于直言，君臣之间形成了良性互动，开创了中国历史上最开明的治世之一。
historicalStoryEn: The most famous story of Tai is the proverb 'after stagnation comes peace' (pi ji tai lai). The classic example is King Goujian of Yue, who after defeat by Wu endured twenty years of hardship—'sleeping on brushwood and tasting gall'—transforming from the depths of stagnation to the heights of prosperity, ultimately conquering Wu. Another example is Emperor Taizong's 'Reign of Zhenguan,' where he embodied Tai's principle of interchange between high and low, welcoming frank counsel from ministers like Wei Zheng, creating one of China's most enlightened eras of governance.
relatedHexagramsNote: 泰卦的综卦为否卦（第12卦），泰否互为表里，“否极泰来”正是描述二者的转化关系；错卦为否卦。互卦为归妹卦（第54卦）。泰与否是《周易》中最经典的对立统一卦对，揭示了盛衰循环的永恒规律。

judgmentZh: 否之匪人，不利君子贞，大往小来。
judgmentEn: Standstill. Evil people do not further the perseverance of the superior man. The great departs, the small approaches.
imageZh: 天地不交，否；君子以俭德辟难，不可荣以禄。
imageEn: Heaven and earth do not unite: the image of Standstill. Thus the superior man falls back upon his inner worth in order to escape the difficulties. He does not permit himself to be honored with revenue.
interpretationZh: 否卦象征闭塞不通，天地不交，阴阳隔绝。君子退隐，小人当道，宜俭朴避难。
interpretationEn: Pi symbolizes stagnation and obstruction. Heaven and earth do not commune. The superior man withdraws and cultivates inner virtue to avoid calamity.

lines.textZh: 初六：拔茅茹，以其汇，贞吉亨。
lines.textEn: Six at the beginning: When ribbon grass is pulled up, the sod comes with it. Each according to his kind. Perseverance brings good fortune and success.
lines.interpretationZh: 拔起茅草连根带土，同类相随，坚守正道则吉利亨通。
lines.interpretationEn: Pulling up ribbon grass brings the sod. Like attracts like. Perseverance brings fortune and success.
lines.detailZh: 初六“拔茅茹，以其汇，贞吉亨”与泰卦初九的“拔茅茹”相呼应，但含义不同。泰卦是阳气带动同类前进，否卦是阴气牵连同类退守。在否塞之初，志同道合的人应该抱团取暖、共同坚守正道。“贞吉亨”说明只要坚守正道，即使在逆境中也能获得吉祥和通达。关键是找到同道中人，不要孤军奋战。
lines.detailEn: The first six echoes Tai's first line but with different meaning. In stagnation, like-minded people should band together and hold to the right path. 'Perseverance brings good fortune and success'—even in adversity, maintaining righteousness brings blessings. The key is finding allies rather than fighting alone.

lines.textZh: 六二：包承，小人吉，大人否亨。
lines.textEn: Six in the second place: They bear and endure; this means good fortune for inferior people. The standstill serves to help the great man to attain success.
lines.interpretationZh: 包容承受，小人得吉，大人在否塞中反能亨通。
lines.interpretationEn: Bearing and enduring. Good fortune for the small. The great man finds success even in standstill.
lines.detailZh: 六二“包承，小人吉，大人否亨”揭示了否卦时期的社会现实：小人善于逢迎（包承），所以小人得志（小人吉）；而大人（君子）虽然处于否塞之中，但正因为经历否塞的磨练，反而能获得更深层的通达（否亨）。这一爻告诉我们：逆境对小人是温床，对君子是熔炉。小人在逆境中投机取巧，君子在逆境中淬炼品格。
lines.detailEn: The second six reveals social reality during stagnation: petty people thrive through flattery, while the great person, though experiencing stagnation, achieves deeper understanding through the ordeal. Adversity is a greenhouse for the petty and a crucible for the noble—the petty exploit difficulties while the noble refine their character through them.

lines.textZh: 六三：包羞。
lines.textEn: Six in the third place: They bear shame.
lines.interpretationZh: 心怀羞愧，所行不正，自取其辱。
lines.interpretationEn: They bear shame. Their actions bring disgrace upon themselves.
lines.detailZh: 六三“包羞”二字简洁有力——心中包藏着羞耻。在否塞之世，那些助纣为虐的人终将感到羞耻。六三处于下卦之顶，是阴气最盛的位置，代表小人势力的顶峰。但“包羞”暗示即使在小人得势的时候，良知也不会完全泯灭。这一爻预示着否极泰来的转机即将出现——当羞耻感觉醒，改变就开始了。
lines.detailEn: The third six—simply 'They bear shame'—is concise yet powerful. Those who aid wrongdoing during stagnation will eventually feel shame. Though this position represents the peak of petty influence, 'bearing shame' suggests that conscience never fully dies. This line foreshadows the turning point: when shame awakens, change begins.

lines.textZh: 九四：有命无咎，畴离祉。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fourth place: He who acts at the command of the highest remains without blame. Those of like mind partake of the blessing.
lines.interpretationZh: 奉天命而行则无咎，同道之人共享福祉。
lines.interpretationEn: Acting under heaven's command brings no blame. Like-minded people share in the blessing.
lines.detailZh: 九四“有命无咎，畴离祉”标志着否卦开始转变。九四是上卦第一个阳爻，阳气开始回归。“有命”意为顺应天命，“畴离祉”意为同类都能获得福祉。当否塞到了极点，天命开始转向，有德之人开始重新聚集。这一爻鼓励我们：在最黑暗的时刻不要放弃希望，转机往往就在眼前。
lines.detailEn: The fourth nine marks Pi's turning point. As the first yang line in the upper trigram, yang energy begins returning. 'He who acts at the command of the highest remains without blame. Those of like mind partake of the blessing.' When stagnation reaches its extreme, heaven's mandate shifts and virtuous people begin gathering again. Don't abandon hope at the darkest moment—the turning point is often just ahead.

lines.textZh: 九五：休否，大人吉。其亡其亡，系于苞桑。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fifth place: Standstill is giving way. Good fortune for the great man. 'What if it should fail, what if it should fail?' In this way he ties it to a cluster of mulberry shoots.
lines.interpretationZh: 否塞将止，大人吉利。常怀危亡之心，如系于坚韧的桑树根，方可稳固。
lines.interpretationEn: Standstill begins to wane. Good fortune for the great man. Constant vigilance — 'what if it fails?' — secures stability like tying to mulberry roots.
lines.detailZh: 九五“休否，大人吉。其亡其亡，系于苞桑”是否卦最关键的一爻。“休否”意为终止否塞，大人（有德有位的领导者）能够扭转局面。“其亡其亡，系于苞桑”是一句深刻的警句：时刻念着“要灭亡了，要灭亡了”，反而能像系在丛生桑树上一样牢固安全。这就是“居安思危”的极致表达——越是担心失败，越不容易失败。
lines.detailEn: The fifth nine is Pi's most crucial line. 'Standstill is giving way. Great good fortune.' The great person can reverse stagnation. 'It is about to perish, it is about to perish! So it is bound to a cluster of mulberry shoots'—constantly fearing destruction actually ensures safety, like being tied to sturdy mulberry roots. This is the ultimate expression of vigilance: the more you worry about failure, the less likely you are to fail.

lines.textZh: 上九：倾否，先否后喜。
lines.textEn: Nine at the top: The standstill comes to an end. First standstill, then good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 否极泰来，闭塞终将倾覆，先有困顿后有喜悦。
lines.interpretationEn: The standstill is overthrown. First obstruction, then joy. After darkness comes light.
lines.detailZh: 上九“倾否，先否后喜”宣告否极泰来的到来。“倾否”意为否塞被倾覆、推翻，“先否后喜”意为先经历否塞之苦，后获得欢喜之乐。这是否卦最光明的结局——所有的苦难都不是白受的，否塞终将过去，喜悦终将到来。上九作为否卦的最后一爻，完美诠释了“否极泰来”的永恒真理：没有永远的黑暗，坚持到底就能迎来光明。
lines.detailEn: The top nine proclaims the arrival of 'after stagnation, peace.' 'The standstill comes to an end. First standstill, then good fortune.' All suffering has meaning; stagnation must pass and joy must come. As Pi's final line, it perfectly illustrates the eternal truth: there is no permanent darkness; persevere to the end and light will come.

overviewZh: 否卦是《周易》第十二卦，由上乾（天）下坤（地）组成，天在上、地在下，看似自然却暗藏不通。天气上升、地气下沉，阴阳不交，万物闭塞。与泰卦天地交融的和谐相反，否卦描绘的是上下隔绝、沟通断裂的困境。“否”的本义是闭塞不通，引申为不顺、困厄。然而否卦并非绝望之卦——“否极泰来”的智慧告诉我们，闭塞到了极点必然会转向通泰，黑暗的尽头就是黎明。
overviewEn: Pi (Standstill/Stagnation) is the 12th hexagram, composed of Qian (heaven) above and Kun (earth) below. Though this arrangement seems natural, it conceals blockage—heaven's energy rises while earth's energy sinks, yin and yang cannot interact, and all things stagnate. Opposite to Tai's harmonious interchange, Pi depicts isolation between high and low, broken communication. Yet Pi is not a hexagram of despair—the wisdom of 'after stagnation comes peace' teaches that when blockage reaches its extreme, it must transform into openness; at the end of darkness comes dawn.
judgmentDetailZh: 否卦卦辞“否之匪人，不利君子贞，大往小来”直接点明了否卦的本质。“否之匪人”意为否塞之时不利于正人君子——小人当道、正道不行。“大往小来”与泰卦的“小往大来”完全相反：阳气（大）向外消散，阴气（小）向内聚集，正是衰退萧条的征兆。《彖传》曰：“天地不交而万物不通也，上下不交而天下无邦也”——天地之气不交融则万物不通达，君臣上下不沟通则国家不成其为国家。这揭示了否卦的根本原因：不是外部环境的问题，而是内部沟通的断裂。
judgmentDetailEn: The judgment 'Standstill. Evil people do not further the perseverance of the superior man. The great departs, the small approaches' directly reveals Pi's nature. 'Evil people' means that during stagnation, petty people prevail while the righteous path is blocked. 'The great departs, the small approaches' is the exact opposite of Tai—yang energy disperses while yin energy gathers, signs of decline. The Commentary states: 'Heaven and earth do not unite, and all beings fail to communicate'—the root cause is not external circumstances but internal breakdown of communication.
imageDetailZh: 象辞“天地不交，否。君子以俭德辟难，不可荣以禄”给出了否卦时期的生存智慧。“俭德辟难”——收敛自己的才德以避开灾难，不是放弃原则，而是在恶劣环境中保全自身的策略。“不可荣以禄”——不能贪图荣华富贵。在小人当道的时期，越是追求名利越容易招来祸患。真正的智者选择韬光养晦、洁身自好，等待时机转变。这与老子“知其荣，守其辱”的思想一脉相承。
imageDetailEn: The Image advises: 'The superior man falls back upon his inner worth and avoids the difficulties. He does not permit himself to be honored with revenue.' During stagnation, conceal your talents to avoid disaster—not abandoning principles but strategically preserving yourself in hostile environments. Don't pursue fame and fortune when petty people hold power; the more you chase glory, the more danger you attract. The truly wise choose to lie low and maintain integrity, waiting for circumstances to change.
modernApplicationZh: 【事业】否卦提示当前环境不利，不宜冒进。如果公司内部沟通不畅、上下隔阂严重，这正是“否”的状态。此时最明智的做法是收敛锋芒、做好本职工作、积蓄实力等待转机。不要在逆境中强出头，“俭德辟难”才是上策。【感情】否卦在感情中代表沟通障碍和冷战。双方各执己见、互不理解，关系陷入僵局。解决之道不是强行突破，而是先各自冷静反思，等待“否极泰来”的转机。【财运】“大往小来”预示投入大而回报小，不是投资的好时机。建议保守理财、减少开支、储备现金，等待市场回暖。【健康】否卦对应气血不通、经络阻滞。注意保暖、适度运动促进循环，避免久坐不动。心情郁闷时要及时疏导，防止气滞成疾。
modernApplicationEn: 【Career】Pi indicates unfavorable conditions—don't advance recklessly. If internal communication is poor and barriers exist between levels, this is the state of stagnation. The wisest approach is to restrain yourself, do your job well, and build strength while waiting for change.【Love】Pi represents communication barriers and cold wars in relationships. Both sides hold firm positions without understanding. The solution isn't forcing a breakthrough but allowing space for reflection, waiting for the turning point.【Wealth】'The great departs, the small approaches' suggests large investments with small returns—not a good time to invest. Conserve finances, reduce spending, and build cash reserves.【Health】Pi corresponds to blocked qi and blood flow. Stay warm, exercise moderately to promote circulation, and avoid prolonged sitting. Address emotional stagnation promptly to prevent it from becoming illness.
historicalStoryZh: 否卦最经典的历史案例是屈原与楚国的故事。屈原身处楚怀王朝廷，忠心耿耿却遭小人谗言排挤，朝廷上下沟通断裂，正是“否”的典型状态。屈原选择了“俭德辟难”——被流放后以诗歌明志，“举世皆浊我独清，众人皆醉我独醒”，在否塞之世保持了高洁的品格。另一个典故是诸葛亮在《出师表》中描述的后汉衰亡：“亲小人，远贤臣”导致上下不交，国家陷入“否”的困境，最终走向灭亡。
historicalStoryEn: The classic historical case of Pi is Qu Yuan and the state of Chu. Despite his loyalty, Qu Yuan was slandered by petty officials, and communication between the court's upper and lower levels broke down—a textbook state of stagnation. Qu Yuan chose to 'fall back upon inner worth': exiled, he expressed his integrity through poetry—'All the world is muddy, I alone am clear; all men are drunk, I alone am sober'—maintaining noble character in a stagnant world. Another example is the fall of the Later Han, where 'favoring petty people and distancing worthy ministers' led to the breakdown of communication that destroyed the dynasty.
relatedHexagramsNote: 否卦的综卦为泰卦（第11卦），否泰互为表里，“否极泰来”描述二者的循环转化；错卦为泰卦。互卦为未济卦（第64卦），暗示否塞之中蕴含着未完成的可能性。否与泰是《周易》中最经典的对立统一卦对。

judgmentZh: 同人于野，亨。利涉大川，利君子贞。
judgmentEn: Fellowship with Men in the open. Success. It furthers one to cross the great water. The perseverance of the superior man furthers.
imageZh: 天与火，同人；君子以类族辨物。
imageEn: Heaven together with fire: the image of Fellowship with Men. Thus the superior man organizes the clans and makes distinctions between things.
interpretationZh: 同人卦象征与人和同，天火同升，志同道合。在旷野中与人和同，光明正大，可成大事。
interpretationEn: Tong Ren symbolizes fellowship and harmony among people. Heaven and fire move together upward. Open fellowship in the wilderness leads to great achievements.

lines.textZh: 初九：同人于门，无咎。
lines.textEn: Nine at the beginning: Fellowship with men at the gate. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 在门口与人和同，刚开始便以开放之心待人，无过失。
lines.interpretationEn: Fellowship begins at the gate. An open heart from the start brings no blame.

lines.textZh: 六二：同人于宗，吝。
lines.textEn: Six in the second place: Fellowship with men in the clan. Humiliation.
lines.interpretationZh: 只在宗族内部和同，心胸狭隘，有所困窘。
lines.interpretationEn: Fellowship limited to the clan is narrow-minded and leads to humiliation.

lines.textZh: 九三：伏戎于莽，升其高陵，三岁不兴。
lines.textEn: Nine in the third place: He hides weapons in the thicket; he climbs the high hill in front of it. For three years he does not rise.
lines.interpretationZh: 在草丛中埋伏兵力，登上高丘观望，三年不能兴起，心怀猜忌难成事。
lines.interpretationEn: Hiding weapons in the thicket and climbing the hill to spy. For three years nothing is accomplished. Suspicion prevents success.

lines.textZh: 九四：乘其墉，弗克攻，吉。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fourth place: He climbs up on his wall; he cannot attack. Good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 登上城墙却不进攻，知难而退，反而吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: Climbing the wall but refraining from attack. Restraint brings good fortune.

lines.textZh: 九五：同人，先号啕而后笑，大师克相遇。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fifth place: Men bound in fellowship first weep and lament, but afterward they laugh. After great struggles they succeed in meeting.
lines.interpretationZh: 同心之人先经历悲泣，后终欢笑，经过艰难斗争终于相聚。
lines.interpretationEn: Those in fellowship first weep, then laugh. After great struggles they finally meet.

lines.textZh: 上九：同人于郊，无悔。
lines.textEn: Nine at the top: Fellowship with men in the meadow. No remorse.
lines.interpretationZh: 在郊外与人和同，虽未达到旷野般的广阔，但也无悔恨。
lines.interpretationEn: Fellowship in the meadow. Though not the widest circle, there is no remorse.

judgmentZh: 大有：元亨。
judgmentEn: Possession in Great Measure. Supreme success.
imageZh: 火在天上，大有；君子以遏恶扬善，顺天休命。
imageEn: Fire in heaven above: the image of Possession in Great Measure. Thus the superior man curbs evil and furthers good, and thereby obeys the benevolent will of heaven.
interpretationZh: 大有卦象征大丰收，火在天上，光明普照。柔居尊位，上下应之，万物亨通。
interpretationEn: Da You symbolizes great possession and abundance. Fire shines high in heaven, illuminating all. A yielding line in the place of honor receives support from all, bringing supreme success.

lines.textZh: 初九：无交害，匪咎，艰则无咎。
lines.textEn: Nine at the beginning: No relationship with what is harmful; there is no blame in this. If one remains conscious of difficulty, one remains without blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 不与有害之事交往，本身无过。保持警惕艰苦之心，则无咎。
lines.interpretationEn: Avoid harmful associations. Remaining aware of difficulties keeps one free of blame.

lines.textZh: 九二：大车以载，有攸往，无咎。
lines.textEn: Nine in the second place: A big wagon for loading. One may undertake something. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 大车装载货物，有所前往，无过失。才能充足，堪当重任。
lines.interpretationEn: A great wagon for loading. One may set forth. Capable of bearing heavy responsibility.

lines.textZh: 九三：公用亨于天子，小人弗克。
lines.textEn: Nine in the third place: A prince offers it to the Son of Heaven. A petty man cannot do this.
lines.interpretationZh: 公侯将财富献给天子，小人做不到如此大度。
lines.interpretationEn: A prince offers his wealth to the Son of Heaven. A petty man is incapable of such generosity.

lines.textZh: 九四：匪其彭，无咎。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fourth place: He makes a difference between himself and his neighbor. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 不炫耀自己的丰盛，保持谦逊，无过失。
lines.interpretationEn: He does not flaunt his abundance. Modesty brings no blame.

lines.textZh: 六五：厥孚交如，威如，吉。
lines.textEn: Six in the fifth place: He whose truth is accessible, yet dignified, has good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 以诚信与人交往，又不失威严，吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: One whose sincerity is approachable yet dignified has good fortune.

lines.textZh: 上九：自天祐之，吉无不利。
lines.textEn: Nine at the top: He is blessed by heaven. Good fortune. Nothing that does not further.
lines.interpretationZh: 得到上天的庇佑，吉利，无往不利。
lines.interpretationEn: Blessed by heaven. Good fortune in everything undertaken.

judgmentZh: 谦：亨，君子有终。
judgmentEn: Modesty creates success. The superior man carries things through.
imageZh: 地中有山，谦；君子以裒多益寡，称物平施。
imageEn: Within the earth, a mountain: the image of Modesty. Thus the superior man reduces that which is too much, and augments that which is too little. He weighs things and makes them equal.
interpretationZh: 谦卦象征谦逊，山在地中，高而不显。谦虚者亨通，君子以谦德善终。
interpretationEn: Qian symbolizes modesty. A mountain hidden within the earth — great yet unassuming. The modest person prospers and the superior man achieves a good end.

lines.textZh: 初六：谦谦君子，用涉大川，吉。
lines.textEn: Six at the beginning: A superior man modest about his modesty may cross the great water. Good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 谦而又谦的君子，可以渡过大河，吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: A superior man modest about his modesty. He may cross the great water. Good fortune.
lines.detailZh: 初六“谦谦君子，用涉大川，吉”描绘了谦虚到极致的君子形象。双重的“谦”字强调谦虚之上更加谦虚，这样的人即使面对“涉大川”般的重大挑战也能逢凶化吉。因为谦虚的人不会轻敌冒进，会充分准备、虚心请教，反而比自负的人更容易成功。在现代语境中，这相当于面对重大项目时保持敬畏心，充分调研、广泛咨询，成功概率反而最高。
lines.detailEn: The first six depicts the image of an extremely modest person. The doubled 'modest' emphasizes humility upon humility—such a person can safely cross even great rivers (face major challenges). Because humble people never underestimate difficulties, they prepare thoroughly and seek advice widely, actually succeeding more easily than the overconfident.

lines.textZh: 六二：鸣谦，贞吉。
lines.textEn: Six in the second place: Modesty that comes to expression. Perseverance brings good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 谦德之名远播，坚守正道则吉。
lines.interpretationEn: Modesty that finds expression. Perseverance brings good fortune.
lines.detailZh: 六二“鸣谦，贞吉”中的“鸣”意为声名远播——谦虚的美德已经被众人所知和赞颂。这不是刻意宣传，而是真诚的谦虚自然散发出的感染力。六二居中得位，是谦德最自然、最纯粹的表达。当一个人的谦虚发自内心而非做作，周围的人自然会感受到并给予尊重。这种“不求名而名自来”的境界，正是谦卦的精髓。
lines.detailEn: The second six—'Modesty that comes to expression. Perseverance brings good fortune'—describes modesty so genuine that its reputation naturally spreads. This is not deliberate publicity but the infectious power of sincere humility. When a person's modesty comes from the heart rather than affectation, others naturally sense it and respond with respect.

lines.textZh: 九三：劳谦，君子有终，吉。
lines.textEn: Nine in the third place: A superior man of modesty and merit carries things to conclusion. Good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 勤劳而谦逊，君子有好的结局，吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: A superior man with merit yet modest carries things through. Good fortune.
lines.detailZh: 九三是谦卦唯一的阳爻，“劳谦，君子有终，吉”描述了一位功劳卓著却依然谦虚的君子。“劳”指辛勤付出和显著功绩，“谦”指功成不居。这是谦卦最难能可贵的境界——有功劳的人保持谦虚远比无功之人谦虚更难。正因如此，“君子有终”——这样的人必定能善始善终，获得最好的结局。
lines.detailEn: The third nine is Kun's only yang line—'A superior man of modesty and merit carries things to conclusion. Good fortune.' This describes someone with outstanding achievements who remains humble. Having merit yet staying modest is far harder than being humble without accomplishments—precisely why such a person 'carries things to conclusion' with the best possible outcome.

lines.textZh: 六四：无不利，撝谦。
lines.textEn: Six in the fourth place: Nothing that would not further modesty in movement.
lines.interpretationZh: 无往不利，发挥谦逊之德。
lines.interpretationEn: Nothing that would not further. Modesty expressed in action.
lines.detailZh: 六四“无不利，撝谦”中“撝”意为发挥、施展。此爻说的是在一切行动中都发挥谦虚的品德，没有什么不利的。六四处于上卦之始，接近权力中心，在这个位置上保持谦虚尤为重要——越是接近高位，越需要谦逊。这一爻告诉我们：谦虚不是某些场合的策略，而应该成为贯穿一切行为的品格。
lines.detailEn: The fourth six—'Nothing that would not further modesty in movement'—means exercising humility in all actions brings universal benefit. Near the center of power, maintaining modesty becomes especially crucial—the closer to high position, the greater the need for humility. This line teaches that modesty should not be a situational strategy but a character trait pervading all conduct.

lines.textZh: 六五：不富以其邻，利用侵伐，无不利。
lines.textEn: Six in the fifth place: Not boasting of wealth before one's neighbor. It is favorable to attack with force. Nothing that would not further.
lines.interpretationZh: 不以财富在邻人面前炫耀，必要时可用武力征伐，无不利。
lines.interpretationEn: Not boasting of wealth before neighbors. When necessary, forceful action is favorable. Nothing unfurthered.
lines.detailZh: 六五“不富以其邻，利用侵伐，无不利”看似与谦虚矛盾，实则揭示了谦德的另一面。“不富以其邻”意为不以财富笼络邻国，而是以谦德感化；“利用侵伐”并非鼓励侵略，而是说当不得不采取强硬行动时，出于谦德的正义之举也是合理的。真正的谦虚不是无原则的退让，该坚持的原则必须坚持，该采取的行动必须果断。
lines.detailEn: The fifth six seems to contradict modesty but actually reveals its other dimension. 'Not rich through neighbors' means winning allegiance through virtue rather than wealth. 'It is favorable to attack' doesn't encourage aggression but indicates that when firm action is necessary, righteous action rooted in modesty is justified. True modesty is not unprincipled retreat—principles must be upheld and necessary action taken decisively.

lines.textZh: 上六：鸣谦，利用行师，征邑国。
lines.textEn: Six at the top: Modesty that comes to expression. It is favorable to set armies marching to chastise one's own city and one's country.
lines.interpretationZh: 谦德远闻，可以出兵征讨，整顿自己的城邑国家。
lines.interpretationEn: Modesty that finds expression. It is favorable to march armies to set one's own domain in order.
lines.detailZh: 上六“鸣谦，利用行师，征邑国”与六二的“鸣谦”呼应，但处于更高的位置。谦虚的声名已经广为传播，此时即使动用军队也是正当的——因为天下人都知道你的谦德，你的行动自然被视为正义之举。这一爻说明：长期积累的谦虚信誉是最强大的软实力。当你需要采取重大行动时，过去的谦德会成为最有力的支撑。
lines.detailEn: The top six echoes the second six's 'modesty that comes to expression' but from a higher position. When your reputation for modesty is widely known, even mobilizing forces is justified—because everyone recognizes your virtue, your actions are naturally seen as righteous. This line demonstrates that long-accumulated humble credibility is the most powerful soft power. When major action is needed, past modesty becomes your strongest support.

overviewZh: 谦卦是《周易》六十四卦中唯一六爻皆吉的卦，由上坤（地）下艮（山）组成，象征高山隐于地下。山本高大，却甘居地下，这就是谦虚的意象——有实力却不张扬，有才华却不自满。谦卦在六十四卦中地位特殊，《系辞》赞曰“谦，德之柄也”，意为谦虚是一切美德的把手，是修身处世的根本。无论处于何种境遇，谦虚都能带来吉祥，这在六十四卦中绝无仅有。
overviewEn: Qian (Modesty) is the only hexagram in the I Ching where all six lines are auspicious. Composed of Kun (earth) above and Gen (mountain) below, it symbolizes a great mountain hidden beneath the earth. The mountain is naturally tall and imposing, yet it willingly rests below the ground—this is the image of true modesty: possessing strength without display, having talent without arrogance. The Xici Commentary praises it: 'Modesty is the handle of virtue,' meaning humility is the foundation of all moral cultivation. Regardless of one's circumstances, modesty always brings good fortune—unique among all sixty-four hexagrams.
judgmentDetailZh: 谦卦卦辞“谦：亨，君子有终”简洁而深刻。“亨”表示通达顺利，“君子有终”意为有德行的人能善始善终。《彖传》进一步阐释：“天道亏盈而益谦，地道变盈而流谦，鬼神害盈而福谦，人道恶盈而好谦”——天道、地道、鬼神之道、人道，四者皆损满而益谦。这是宇宙间最普遍的法则：骄傲自满必遭损害，谦虚退让必得福报。月满则亏、水满则溢，唯有谦虚才能持盈保泰。孔子特别推崇此卦，认为谦德是君子最重要的品质。
judgmentDetailEn: The judgment 'Modesty creates success. The superior man carries things through' is concise yet profound. The Commentary elaborates: 'The way of heaven diminishes the full and augments the modest; the way of earth transforms the full and flows toward the modest; spirits and gods harm the full and bless the modest; the way of humans hates the full and loves the modest'—heaven, earth, spirits, and humanity all reduce excess and benefit modesty. This is the most universal law: pride inevitably brings harm, while humility always brings blessings. Confucius especially revered this hexagram, considering modesty the most essential quality of a noble person.
imageDetailZh: 象辞“地中有山，谦。君子以裒多益寡，称物平施”描绘了一幅意味深长的画面：大地之中蕴藏着高山，外表平坦却内含巍峨。“裒多益寡”意为减少多余的、补充不足的，“称物平施”意为衡量事物轻重而公平分配。这是谦卦给出的实践指南——谦虚不是空洞的姿态，而是要落实到行动中：削减自己过多的部分去帮助不足的人，公平公正地对待每一件事。这种“损有余补不足”的精神，正是社会和谐的基石。
imageDetailEn: The Image 'Within the earth, a mountain: the image of Modesty. The superior man reduces that which is too much and augments that which is too little. He weighs things and makes them equal' paints a profound picture: a great mountain contained within the earth, outwardly level yet inwardly towering. This is Modesty's practical guide—humility is not an empty gesture but must be realized through action: reducing one's excess to help those in need, treating everything with fairness and justice. This spirit of 'diminishing surplus to supplement deficiency' is the cornerstone of social harmony.
modernApplicationZh: 【事业】谦卦提示在职场中保持谦逊低调是最稳妥的策略。功劳不必急于邀赏，能力不必处处炫耀。“地中有山”的智慧告诉我们：真正有实力的人不需要自我标榜，实力自然会被看见。领导者更应以谦德服人，而非以权势压人。【感情】谦卦在感情中代表真诚和尊重。放下自我中心，认真倾听对方的需求，不在关系中争高低。谦虚的态度能化解大部分矛盾，让关系更加和谐长久。【财运】谦卦象征稳健保守的理财之道。不贪大利、不冒大险，脚踏实地积累财富。“裒多益寡”也提醒我们适当回馈社会，财富才能长久。【健康】谦卦对应脾胃（坤土）和骨骼关节（艮山），提醒保持饮食节制、适度运动。心态谦和平静，有助于身心健康。
modernApplicationEn: 【Career】Modesty advises maintaining a humble, low-key approach as the safest workplace strategy. True competence doesn't need self-promotion—real ability will naturally be recognized. Leaders especially should win respect through humility, not authority.【Love】In relationships, Modesty represents sincerity and respect. Set aside ego, listen carefully to your partner's needs, and don't compete for dominance. A humble attitude resolves most conflicts.【Wealth】Modesty symbolizes conservative, steady financial management. Don't chase excessive profits or take unnecessary risks. 'Reducing surplus to supplement deficiency' also reminds us to give back to society.【Health】Modesty corresponds to the spleen/stomach (Kun earth) and bones/joints (Gen mountain). Maintain dietary moderation and moderate exercise. A humble, peaceful mindset promotes overall wellbeing.
historicalStoryZh: 谦卦最著名的典故是周公“一沐三捉发，一饭三吐哺”的故事。周公旦辅佐成王治理天下，位极人臣，却始终保持谦逊。每次洗头时听说有贤人来访，就三次握住湿发出去接见；每次吃饭时听说有人求见，就三次吐出口中食物去迎接。曹操在《短歌行》中引用此典：“周公吐哺，天下归心”，正是谦卦精神的最佳写照。另一个典故是孔子问礼于老子，孔子已是天下闻名的大学者，却仍虚心向老子请教，体现了“谦，德之柄也”的精神。
historicalStoryEn: The most famous story of Modesty involves the Duke of Zhou, who 'three times wrung out his hair during one bath and three times spat out his food during one meal' to receive visitors. Despite holding the highest position below the king, he remained unfailingly humble. Cao Cao quoted this in his famous poem: 'The Duke of Zhou spat out his food, and all hearts turned to him.'
