Another story involves Confucius humbly seeking instruction from Laozi—despite being the most renowned scholar of his time, Confucius still approached Laozi as a student, perfectly embodying the principle that 'modesty is the handle of virtue.'
relatedHexagramsNote: 谦卦的错卦为履卦（第10卦），一谦一履，谦虚与践行相对应；综卦为豫卦（第16卦），谦逊与欢乐相承接——谦虚之后自然带来喜悦。互卦为解卦（第40卦），内含化解困难的力量。

judgmentZh: 豫：利建侯行师。
judgmentEn: Enthusiasm. It furthers one to install helpers and to set armies marching.
imageZh: 雷出地奋，豫；先王以作乐崇德，殷荐之上帝，以配祖考。
imageEn: Thunder comes resounding out of the earth: the image of Enthusiasm. Thus the ancient kings made music in order to honor merit, and offered it with splendor to the Supreme Deity, inviting their ancestors to be present.
interpretationZh: 豫卦象征欢乐愉悦，雷从地中奋起，万物振奋。顺势而动，利于建功立业。
interpretationEn: Yu symbolizes enthusiasm and joyful harmony. Thunder bursts forth from the earth, stirring all things. Moving in accord with the time favors establishing great works.

lines.textZh: 初六：鸣豫，凶。
lines.textEn: Six at the beginning: Enthusiasm that expresses itself brings misfortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 得意忘形地宣扬欢乐，凶险。过度张扬招致灾祸。
lines.interpretationEn: Boasting of one's enthusiasm brings misfortune. Excessive display invites disaster.

lines.textZh: 六二：介于石，不终日，贞吉。
lines.textEn: Six in the second place: Firm as a rock. Not a whole day. Perseverance brings good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 坚如磐石，不等一天就能明辨是非，坚守正道则吉。
lines.interpretationEn: Firm as a rock. One discerns the truth before the day is over. Perseverance brings good fortune.

lines.textZh: 六三：盱豫，悔。迟有悔。
lines.textEn: Six in the third place: Enthusiasm that looks upward creates remorse. Hesitation brings remorse.
lines.interpretationZh: 仰望他人而生欢乐，会有悔恨。犹豫不决也会后悔。
lines.interpretationEn: Looking upward for enthusiasm creates remorse. Hesitation also brings remorse.

lines.textZh: 九四：由豫，大有得。勿疑，朋盍簪。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fourth place: The source of enthusiasm. He achieves great things. Doubt not. You gather friends around you as a hair clasp gathers the hair.
lines.interpretationZh: 作为欢乐的源泉，大有所得。不要疑虑，朋友如簪聚发般汇聚而来。
lines.interpretationEn: The source of enthusiasm achieves great things. Have no doubt. Friends gather around you like hair held by a clasp.

lines.textZh: 六五：贞疾，恒不死。
lines.textEn: Six in the fifth place: Persistently ill, and still does not die.
lines.interpretationZh: 长期处于困境如同久病，但始终不会灭亡。
lines.interpretationEn: Persistently ill yet never dying. Enduring hardship without succumbing.

lines.textZh: 上六：冥豫，成有渝，无咎。
lines.textEn: Six at the top: Deluded enthusiasm. But if after completion one changes, there is no blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 沉迷于欢乐而昏昧，但若能及时醒悟改变，则无过失。
lines.interpretationEn: Blinded by enthusiasm. But if one changes after realizing the error, there is no blame.

judgmentZh: 随：元亨利贞，无咎。
judgmentEn: Following has supreme success. Perseverance furthers. No blame.
imageZh: 泽中有雷，随；君子以向晦入宴息。
imageEn: Thunder in the middle of the lake: the image of Following. Thus the superior man at nightfall goes indoors for rest and recuperation.
interpretationZh: 随卦象征随从、跟随，泽中有雷，雷动而泽随之。顺应时势，随机应变，方能亨通无咎。
interpretationEn: Sui symbolizes following and adaptation. Thunder within the lake stirs movement. By adapting to the times and following what is right, one achieves success without blame.

lines.textZh: 初九：官有渝，贞吉。出门交有功。
lines.textEn: Nine at the beginning: The standard is changing. Perseverance brings good fortune. To go out of the door in company produces deeds.
lines.interpretationZh: 主管之事有所变化，坚守正道则吉。出门与人交往则有功绩。
lines.interpretationEn: Standards are shifting. Stay true and go forth to engage with others for achievement.

lines.textZh: 六二：系小子，失丈夫。
lines.textEn: Six in the second place: If one clings to the little boy, one loses the strong man.
lines.interpretationZh: 若依附小人，就会失去贤者。取舍须慎重。
lines.interpretationEn: Clinging to the petty causes one to lose the worthy. Choose wisely.

lines.textZh: 六三：系丈夫，失小子。随有求得，利居贞。
lines.textEn: Six in the third place: If one clings to the strong man, one loses the little boy. Through following one finds what one seeks. It furthers one to remain persevering.
lines.interpretationZh: 依附贤者则失去小人，随从正道则有所得，宜安居守正。
lines.interpretationEn: Clinging to the worthy, one lets go of the petty. Following the right path brings what is sought.

lines.textZh: 九四：随有获，贞凶。有孚在道，以明，何咎。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fourth place: Following creates success. Perseverance brings misfortune. To go one's way with sincerity brings clarity. How could there be blame in this?
lines.interpretationZh: 随从而有所获，但固执则凶。以诚信行于正道，光明磊落，何咎之有。
lines.interpretationEn: Following brings gain, but stubbornness leads to misfortune. Walk sincerely on the right path and no blame arises.

lines.textZh: 九五：孚于嘉，吉。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fifth place: Sincere in the good. Good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 诚信于美善之事，吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: Sincerity toward what is good and excellent brings good fortune.

lines.textZh: 上六：拘系之，乃从维之。王用亨于西山。
lines.textEn: Six at the top: He meets with firm allegiance and is still further bound. The king introduces him to the Western Mountain.
lines.interpretationZh: 被牢固地系住追随，王在西山举行祭祀以示诚意。
lines.interpretationEn: Firm allegiance binds one closely. The king offers sacrifice at the Western Mountain to honor this devotion.

nameEn: Work on What Has Been Spoiled
judgmentZh: 蛊：元亨，利涉大川。先甲三日，后甲三日。
judgmentEn: Work on What Has Been Spoiled has supreme success. It furthers one to cross the great water. Before the starting point, three days. After the starting point, three days.
imageZh: 山下有风，蛊；君子以振民育德。
imageEn: The wind blows low on the mountain: the image of Decay. Thus the superior man stirs up the people and strengthens their spirit.
interpretationZh: 蛊卦象征腐败与整治，山下有风，风被山阻而停滞。事物败坏之后需要整顿革新，拨乱反正。
interpretationEn: Gu symbolizes decay and the work of reform. Wind at the foot of the mountain is stagnant. After corruption, renovation and correction are needed.

lines.textZh: 初六：干父之蛊，有子，考无咎，厉终吉。
lines.textEn: Six at the beginning: Setting right what has been spoiled by the father. If there is a son, no blame rests upon the departed father. Danger. In the end, good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 整治父辈留下的弊病，有能干的儿子承担，先父可免于咎。虽有危险，终获吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: Correcting the father's decay. A capable son removes blame from the departed. Danger, but good fortune in the end.

lines.textZh: 九二：干母之蛊，不可贞。
lines.textEn: Nine in the second place: Setting right what has been spoiled by the mother. One must not be too persevering.
lines.interpretationZh: 整治母辈留下的弊病，不可过于强硬，宜柔和处理。
lines.interpretationEn: Correcting the mother's decay. Do not be too forceful; gentleness is needed.

lines.textZh: 九三：干父之蛊，小有悔，无大咎。
lines.textEn: Nine in the third place: Setting right what has been spoiled by the father. There will be a little remorse. No great blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 整治父辈的弊病，虽有小悔恨，但无大过失。
lines.interpretationEn: Correcting the father's decay. Some regret, but no great blame.

lines.textZh: 六四：裕父之蛊，往见吝。
lines.textEn: Six in the fourth place: Tolerating what has been spoiled by the father. In continuing one sees humiliation.
lines.interpretationZh: 宽容放纵父辈的弊病不加整治，继续下去则有困窘。
lines.interpretationEn: Tolerating the father's decay without correction. Continuing this way leads to humiliation.

lines.textZh: 六五：干父之蛊，用誉。
lines.textEn: Six in the fifth place: Setting right what has been spoiled by the father. One meets with praise.
lines.interpretationZh: 整治父辈的弊病，因此获得赞誉。
lines.interpretationEn: Correcting the father's decay brings praise and honor.

lines.textZh: 上九：不事王侯，高尚其事。
lines.textEn: Nine at the top: He does not serve kings and princes, sets himself higher goals.
lines.interpretationZh: 不为王侯效力，追求更高尚的志向，超然物外。
lines.interpretationEn: Not serving kings and princes. One sets higher goals and transcends worldly affairs.

judgmentZh: 临：元亨利贞。至于八月有凶。
judgmentEn: Approach has supreme success. Perseverance furthers. When the eighth month comes, there will be misfortune.
imageZh: 泽上有地，临；君子以教思无穷，容保民无疆。
imageEn: The earth above the lake: the image of Approach. Thus the superior man is inexhaustible in his will to teach, and without limits in his tolerance and protection of the people.
interpretationZh: 临卦象征亲临、治理，地在泽上，居高临下。阳气渐长，万物生发，但盛极必衰，八月有凶。
interpretationEn: Lin symbolizes approach and oversight. Earth above the lake looks down. Yang energy grows, but at its peak decline begins — by the eighth month, misfortune comes.

lines.textZh: 初九：咸临，贞吉。
lines.textEn: Nine at the beginning: Joint approach. Perseverance brings good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 感应而亲临，坚守正道则吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: Approaching together in mutual influence. Perseverance brings good fortune.

lines.textZh: 九二：咸临，吉无不利。
lines.textEn: Nine in the second place: Joint approach. Good fortune. Everything furthers.
lines.interpretationZh: 感应而亲临，吉利，无所不利。
lines.interpretationEn: Approaching together. Good fortune. Nothing that does not further.

lines.textZh: 六三：甘临，无攸利。既忧之，无咎。
lines.textEn: Six in the third place: Comfortable approach. Nothing that would further. If one is induced to grieve over it, one becomes free of blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 以甜言蜜语亲临，无所利益。若能忧虑反省，则无过失。
lines.interpretationEn: A complacent approach brings no benefit. If one reflects with concern, blame is avoided.

lines.textZh: 六四：至临，无咎。
lines.textEn: Six in the fourth place: Complete approach. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 亲自到来，诚心亲临，无过失。
lines.interpretationEn: Approaching with full commitment. No blame.

lines.textZh: 六五：知临，大君之宜，吉。
lines.textEn: Six in the fifth place: Wise approach. This is right for a great prince. Good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 以智慧亲临治理，这是大君应有的作为，吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: Approaching with wisdom. This befits a great ruler. Good fortune.

lines.textZh: 上六：敦临，吉无咎。
lines.textEn: Six at the top: Great-hearted approach. Good fortune. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 以敦厚之心亲临，吉利无过失。
lines.interpretationEn: Approaching with great-heartedness and sincerity. Good fortune. No blame.

judgmentZh: 观：盥而不荐，有孚颙若。
judgmentEn: Contemplation. The ablution has been made, but not yet the offering. Full of trust they look up to him.
imageZh: 风行地上，观；先王以省方，观民设教。
imageEn: The wind blows over the earth: the image of Contemplation. Thus the kings of old visited the regions of the world, contemplated the people, and gave them instruction.
interpretationZh: 观卦象征观察与示范，风行地上，无所不至。如祭祀时洗手净心的庄严时刻，以诚敬之心观察天下。
interpretationEn: Guan symbolizes contemplation and setting an example. Wind blows across the earth, reaching everywhere. Like the solemn moment of ablution before sacrifice, one observes the world with sincerity and reverence.

lines.textZh: 初六：童观，小人无咎，君子吝。
lines.textEn: Six at the beginning: Boylike contemplation. For an inferior man, no blame. For a superior man, humiliation.
lines.interpretationZh: 幼稚浅薄的观察，对小人无妨，但对君子则有愧。
lines.interpretationEn: Childish contemplation. Acceptable for the common man, but humiliating for the superior man.

lines.textZh: 六二：窥观，利女贞。
lines.textEn: Six in the second place: Contemplation through the crack of the door. Furthering for the perseverance of a woman.
lines.interpretationZh: 从门缝中窥视，视野狭隘，对女子守正有利，但不宜大丈夫。
lines.interpretationEn: Peeping through the door crack. A narrow view, suitable for a woman's perseverance but not for a man of action.

lines.textZh: 六三：观我生，进退。
lines.textEn: Six in the third place: Contemplation of my life decides the choice between advance and retreat.
lines.interpretationZh: 审视自己的生活行为，以此决定进退取舍。
lines.interpretationEn: Contemplating one's own life to decide whether to advance or retreat.

lines.textZh: 六四：观国之光，利用宾于王。
lines.textEn: Six in the fourth place: Contemplation of the light of the kingdom. It furthers one to exert influence as the guest of a king.
lines.interpretationZh: 观察国家的风光政教，适宜作为宾客辅佐君王。
lines.interpretationEn: Contemplating the glory of the kingdom. It furthers one to serve as a guest of the king.

lines.textZh: 九五：观我生，君子无咎。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fifth place: Contemplation of my life. The superior man is without blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 审视自己的德行与影响，君子如此则无过失。
lines.interpretationEn: Contemplating one's own life and influence. The superior man finds no blame.

lines.textZh: 上九：观其生，君子无咎。
lines.textEn: Nine at the top: Contemplation of his life. The superior man is without blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 观察他人的生活与德行，君子以此自省则无过失。
lines.interpretationEn: Contemplating the life of others. The superior man reflects and remains without blame.

judgmentZh: 噬嗑：亨。利用狱。
judgmentEn: Biting Through has success. It is favorable to let justice be administered.
imageZh: 雷电噬嗑；先王以明罚敕法。
imageEn: Thunder and lightning: the image of Biting Through. Thus the kings of former times made firm the laws through clearly defined penalties.
interpretationZh: 噬嗑卦象征咬合，口中有物阻隔，须咬断方能合拢。雷电交加，刚柔并济，象征以刑罚明法度，去除障碍。
interpretationEn: Shi He symbolizes biting through an obstacle. Thunder and lightning combine force and clarity, representing the use of law and penalties to remove obstructions.

lines.textZh: 初九：屦校灭趾，无咎。
lines.textEn: Nine at the beginning: His feet are fastened in the stocks, so that his toes disappear. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 脚戴刑具遮住脚趾，小惩大诫，及时制止过错则无咎。
lines.interpretationEn: Feet in the stocks hide the toes. A small punishment prevents greater wrongdoing. No blame.

lines.textZh: 六二：噬肤灭鼻，无咎。
lines.textEn: Six in the second place: Bites through tender meat, so that his nose disappears. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 咬入柔软的肉中，鼻子都没入其中，虽用刑过重但无过失。
lines.interpretationEn: Biting through soft meat so deeply the nose is covered. Firm action, though strong, brings no blame.

lines.textZh: 六三：噬腊肉，遇毒；小吝，无咎。
lines.textEn: Six in the third place: Bites on old dried meat and strikes on something poisonous. Slight humiliation. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 咬干腊肉遇到毒物，虽有小困窘但无大过。
lines.interpretationEn: Biting on dried meat and encountering poison. Slight humiliation but no blame.

lines.textZh: 九四：噬干胏，得金矢，利艰贞，吉。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fourth place: Bites on dried gristly meat. Receives metal arrows. It furthers one to be mindful of difficulties and to be persevering. Good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 咬带骨的干肉，得到金属箭矢，利于在艰难中坚守正道，吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: Biting on dried gristly meat and finding metal arrows. Persevere through difficulty for good fortune.

lines.textZh: 六五：噬干肉，得黄金，贞厉，无咎。
lines.textEn: Six in the fifth place: Bites on dried lean meat. Receives yellow gold. Perseveringly aware of danger. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 咬干肉得到黄金，坚守正道并警惕危险，则无过失。
lines.interpretationEn: Biting on dried meat and receiving yellow gold. Remain aware of danger. No blame.

lines.textZh: 上九：何校灭耳，凶。
lines.textEn: Nine at the top: His neck is fastened in the wooden cangue, so that his ears disappear. Misfortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 颈戴枷锁遮住耳朵，刑罚至极，积恶不改终致凶险。
lines.interpretationEn: Neck in the cangue so the ears disappear. Punishment reaches its extreme. Misfortune.

judgmentZh: 贲：亨。小利有攸往。
judgmentEn: Grace has success. In small matters it is favorable to undertake something.
imageZh: 山下有火，贲；君子以明庶政，无敢折狱。
imageEn: Fire at the foot of the mountain: the image of Grace. Thus does the superior man proceed when clearing up current affairs. But he dare not decide controversial issues in this way.
interpretationZh: 贲卦象征文饰，山下有火，光彩照耀。文饰虽美但不可过度，小事可行，大事不宜仅凭外表。
interpretationEn: Bi symbolizes grace and adornment. Fire beneath the mountain illuminates with beauty. Ornament aids small matters, but substance must underlie great decisions.

lines.textZh: 初九：贲其趾，舍车而徒。
lines.textEn: Nine at the beginning: He lends grace to his toes, leaves the carriage, and walks.
lines.interpretationZh: 装饰脚趾，舍弃车辆步行，安于朴素，不慕虚荣。
lines.interpretationEn: Adorning the toes, he leaves the carriage and walks. He chooses simplicity over vanity.

lines.textZh: 六二：贲其须。
lines.textEn: Six in the second place: Lends grace to the beard on his chin.
lines.interpretationZh: 装饰胡须，依附于上，随上而动。
lines.interpretationEn: Adorning the beard. Grace follows the higher principle, moving with what is above.

lines.textZh: 九三：贲如濡如，永贞吉。
lines.textEn: Nine in the third place: Graceful and moist. Constant perseverance brings good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 文饰润泽，光彩照人，长久坚守正道则吉。
lines.interpretationEn: Graceful and glistening. Lasting perseverance brings good fortune.

lines.textZh: 六四：贲如皤如，白马翰如，匪寇婚媾。
lines.textEn: Six in the fourth place: Grace or simplicity? A white horse comes as if on wings. He is not a robber; he will woo at the right time.
lines.interpretationZh: 朴素如白，白马飞驰而来，来者非寇而是求婚者。
lines.interpretationEn: Grace or plainness? A white winged horse approaches. Not a robber but a wooer.

lines.textZh: 六五：贲于丘园，束帛戋戋，吝，终吉。
lines.textEn: Six in the fifth place: Grace in hills and gardens. The roll of silk is meager and small. Humiliation, but in the end good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 在丘园中修饰，礼物虽薄但出于真心，虽有困窘终获吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: Grace in hills and gardens. The silk offering is small but sincere. Initial humiliation, but good fortune in the end.

lines.textZh: 上九：白贲，无咎。
lines.textEn: Nine at the top: Simple grace. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 朴素无华的装饰，返璞归真，无过失。
lines.interpretationEn: Simple, unadorned grace. Return to the essential. No blame.

judgmentZh: 剥：不利有攸往。
judgmentEn: Splitting Apart. It does not further one to go anywhere.
imageZh: 山附于地，剥；上以厚下安宅。
imageEn: The mountain rests on the earth: the image of Splitting Apart. Thus those above can ensure their position only by giving generously to those below.
interpretationZh: 剥卦象征剥落，山附于地，根基动摇。五阴剥一阳，小人势盛，君子宜静待时机，不宜妄动。
interpretationEn: Bo symbolizes splitting apart and decay. The mountain rests on earth with its base eroding. Five yin lines press upon one yang. The superior man waits quietly.

lines.textZh: 初六：剥床以足，蔑贞凶。
lines.textEn: Six at the beginning: The leg of the bed is split. Those who persevere are destroyed. Misfortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 床腿被剥落，根基受损，坚持不当则凶。
lines.interpretationEn: The leg of the bed is split. The foundation is damaged. Misfortune for those who persist wrongly.

lines.textZh: 六二：剥床以辨，蔑贞凶。
lines.textEn: Six in the second place: The bed is split at the edge. Those who persevere are destroyed. Misfortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 床沿被剥落，侵蚀加深，仍不知警惕则凶。
lines.interpretationEn: The bed is split at the edge. Erosion deepens. Misfortune for those who ignore the warning.

lines.textZh: 六三：剥之，无咎。
lines.textEn: Six in the third place: He splits with them. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 与剥落者决裂，脱离小人阵营，无过失。
lines.interpretationEn: He breaks away from the destructive group. No blame.

lines.textZh: 六四：剥床以肤，凶。
lines.textEn: Six in the fourth place: The bed is split up to the skin. Misfortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 床面被剥落，灾祸已近身，凶险。
lines.interpretationEn: The bed is split to the skin. Disaster is close at hand. Misfortune.

lines.textZh: 六五：贯鱼，以宫人宠，无不利。
lines.textEn: Six in the fifth place: A shoal of fishes. Favor comes through the court ladies. Everything acts to further.
lines.interpretationZh: 如鱼贯而入，以宫人之宠得势，顺应时势则无不利。
lines.interpretationEn: A string of fish. Favor through the court ladies. Everything furthers.

lines.textZh: 上九：硕果不食，君子得舆，小人剥庐。
lines.textEn: Nine at the top: There is a large fruit still uneaten. The superior man receives a carriage. The house of the inferior man is split apart.
lines.interpretationZh: 大果未被吃掉，君子得到车乘，小人则失去居所。一阳犹存，生机未绝。
lines.interpretationEn: A large fruit remains uneaten. The superior man gains a carriage; the inferior man's house splits apart. One yang endures — life persists.

judgmentZh: 复：亨。出入无疾，朋来无咎。反复其道，七日来复，利有攸往。
judgmentEn: Return. Success. Going out and coming in without error. Friends come without blame. To and fro goes the way. On the seventh day comes return. It furthers one to have somewhere to go.
imageZh: 雷在地中，复；先王以至日闭关，商旅不行，后不省方。
imageEn: Thunder within the earth: the image of the Turning Point. Thus the kings of antiquity closed the passes at the time of solstice. Merchants and strangers did not go about, and the ruler did not travel through the provinces.
interpretationZh: 复卦象征回归，一阳复生于五阴之下，如冬至阳气初回。雷在地中蓄势，万物将复苏，宜休养生息。
interpretationEn: Fu symbolizes return. One yang line reappears beneath five yin lines, like the winter solstice when light returns. Thunder rests within the earth, gathering strength for renewal.

lines.textZh: 初九：不远复，无祗悔，元吉。
lines.textEn: Nine at the beginning: Return from a short distance. No need for remorse. Great good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 迷途不远即返回，不至于后悔，大吉。
lines.interpretationEn: Returning before going far astray. No remorse needed. Great good fortune.

lines.textZh: 六二：休复，吉。
lines.textEn: Six in the second place: Quiet return. Good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 安然回归，从善如流，吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: Quiet, graceful return. Following the good. Good fortune.

lines.textZh: 六三：频复，厉无咎。
lines.textEn: Six in the third place: Repeated return. Danger. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 反复回归，虽有危险但无过失，需坚定心志。
lines.interpretationEn: Repeated return. Danger exists but no blame. Strengthen your resolve.

lines.textZh: 六四：中行独复。
lines.textEn: Six in the fourth place: Walking in the midst of others, one returns alone.
lines.interpretationZh: 行于众人之中却独自回归正道，特立独行。
lines.interpretationEn: Walking among others, yet returning alone to the right path.

lines.textZh: 六五：敦复，无悔。
lines.textEn: Six in the fifth place: Noblehearted return. No remorse.
lines.interpretationZh: 敦厚地回归正道，无所悔恨。
lines.interpretationEn: Noblehearted return to the good. No remorse.

lines.textZh: 上六：迷复，凶，有灾眚。用行师，终有大败，以其国君，凶；至于十年，不克征。
lines.textEn: Six at the top: Missing the return. Misfortune. Misfortune from within and without. If armies are set marching in this way, one will in the end suffer a great defeat, disastrous for the ruler of the country. For ten years it will not be possible to attack again.
lines.interpretationZh: 迷失而不知回归，凶险，有灾祸。若此时用兵，终将大败，十年不能再征。
lines.interpretationEn: Lost and unable to return. Misfortune and disaster. Armies sent forth now will suffer great defeat. Ten years before recovery.

judgmentZh: 无妄：元亨利贞。其匪正有眚，不利有攸往。
judgmentEn: Innocence. Supreme success. Perseverance furthers. If someone is not as he should be, he has misfortune, and it does not further him to undertake anything.
imageZh: 天下雷行，物与无妄；先王以茂对时，育万物。
imageEn: Under heaven thunder rolls: all things attain the natural state of innocence. Thus the kings of old, rich in virtue and in harmony with the time, fostered and nourished all beings.
interpretationZh: 无妄卦象征天真无妄，天下雷动，万物各得其性。行事应顺应天道，不可妄为。
interpretationEn: Wu Wang symbolizes innocence and the unexpected. Thunder rolls beneath heaven, and all things follow their true nature. Act in accord with the natural order.

lines.textZh: 初九：无妄，往吉。
lines.textEn: Nine at the beginning: Innocent behavior brings good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 以天真无妄之心行事，前往则吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: Acting with innocence and sincerity brings good fortune.

lines.textZh: 六二：不耕获，不菑畲，则利有攸往。
lines.textEn: Six in the second place: If one does not count on the harvest while plowing, nor on the use of the ground while clearing it, then it furthers one to undertake something.
lines.interpretationZh: 不计较耕耘的收获，不计较开荒的利用，则利于有所前往。
lines.interpretationEn: Work without calculating the reward. Selfless effort furthers undertakings.

lines.textZh: 六三：无妄之灾，或系之牛，行人之得，邑人之灾。
lines.textEn: Six in the third place: Undeserved misfortune. The cow that was tethered by someone is the wanderer's gain and the citizen's loss.
lines.interpretationZh: 无妄之灾从天而降，如系住的牛被路人牵走，是邑人的灾祸。
lines.interpretationEn: Undeserved misfortune strikes. What one person gains, another loses through no fault of their own.

lines.textZh: 九四：可贞，无咎。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fourth place: He who can be persevering remains without blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 能够坚守正道，则无过失。
lines.interpretationEn: One who can remain steadfast will be without blame.

lines.textZh: 九五：无妄之疾，勿药有喜。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fifth place: Use no medicine in an illness incurred through no fault of your own. It will pass of itself.
lines.interpretationZh: 无妄之疾不需用药，自然会痊愈，有喜庆之事。
lines.interpretationEn: An illness not caused by one's own fault needs no medicine. It will heal naturally.

lines.textZh: 上九：无妄，行有眚，无攸利。
lines.textEn: Nine at the top: Innocent action brings misfortune. Nothing furthers.
lines.interpretationZh: 无妄之时妄行，必有灾祸，无所利益。
lines.interpretationEn: Action without proper timing brings misfortune. Nothing is furthered.

nameEn: The Taming Power of the Great
judgmentZh: 大畜：利贞，不家食吉，利涉大川。
judgmentEn: The Taming Power of the Great. Perseverance furthers. Not eating at home brings good fortune. It furthers one to cross the great water.
imageZh: 天在山中，大畜；君子以多识前言往行，以畜其德。
imageEn: Heaven within the mountain: the image of The Taming Power of the Great. Thus the superior man acquaints himself with many sayings of antiquity and many deeds of the past, in order to strengthen his character thereby.
interpretationZh: 大畜卦象征大的蓄积，天在山中，蕴藏巨大能量。宜积蓄力量，学习前人智慧，厚积薄发。
interpretationEn: Da Xu symbolizes great accumulation. Heaven contained within the mountain stores immense energy. Gather strength and learn from the wisdom of the ancients.

lines.textZh: 初九：有厉，利已。
lines.textEn: Nine at the beginning: Danger is at hand. It furthers one to desist.
lines.interpretationZh: 有危险在前，宜停止前进，暂时收敛。
lines.interpretationEn: Danger is near. It is wise to halt and restrain oneself.

lines.textZh: 九二：舆说輹。
lines.textEn: Nine in the second place: The axletrees are taken from the wagon.
lines.interpretationZh: 车轴脱落，车不能行，被迫停下蓄势。
lines.interpretationEn: The axletrees are removed from the wagon. One is compelled to stop and gather strength.

lines.textZh: 九三：良马逐，利艰贞。曰闲舆卫，利有攸往。
lines.textEn: Nine in the third place: A good horse that follows others. Awareness of danger, with perseverance, furthers. Practice chariot driving and armed defense daily. It furthers one to have somewhere to go.
lines.interpretationZh: 良马追逐奔驰，宜在艰难中坚守正道。日常练习驾车防卫，利于有所前往。
lines.interpretationEn: A fine horse gives chase. Persevere through difficulty. Daily practice in defense furthers one's goals.

lines.textZh: 六四：童牛之牿，元吉。
lines.textEn: Six in the fourth place: The headboard of a young bull. Great good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 给小牛套上横木防其抵触，防患于未然，大吉。
lines.interpretationEn: A headboard on a young bull prevents it from goring. Prevention brings great fortune.

lines.textZh: 六五：豮豕之牙，吉。
lines.textEn: Six in the fifth place: The tusk of a gelded boar. Good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 阉割的猪虽有獠牙但已无攻击性，化解危险于无形，吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: The tusk of a gelded boar. The danger is neutralized at its source. Good fortune.

lines.textZh: 上九：何天之衢，亨。
lines.textEn: Nine at the top: One attains the way of heaven. Success.
lines.interpretationZh: 通达天道之大路，亨通无阻。
lines.interpretationEn: One attains the highway of heaven. Great success and free passage.

judgmentZh: 颐：贞吉。观颐，自求口实。
judgmentEn: The Corners of the Mouth. Perseverance brings good fortune. Pay heed to the providing of nourishment and to what a man seeks to fill his own mouth with.
imageZh: 山下有雷，颐；君子以慎言语，节饮食。
imageEn: At the foot of the mountain, thunder: the image of Providing Nourishment. Thus the superior man is careful of his words and temperate in eating and drinking.
interpretationZh: 颐卦象征颐养，山下有雷，上止下动如口嚼食物。宜慎言语、节饮食，注重身心修养。
interpretationEn: Yi symbolizes nourishment. Thunder at the foot of the mountain resembles the jaw in motion. Be careful with words and moderate in consumption.

lines.textZh: 初九：舍尔灵龟，观我朵颐，凶。
lines.textEn: Nine at the beginning: You let your magic tortoise go, and look at me with the corners of your mouth drooping. Misfortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 放弃自己的灵龟之智，却羡慕他人口中之食，凶险。
lines.interpretationEn: You abandon your own spiritual treasure and gaze enviously at others. Misfortune.

lines.textZh: 六二：颠颐，拂经，于丘颐，征凶。
lines.textEn: Six in the second place: Turning to the summit for nourishment, deviating from the path to seek nourishment from the hill. Continuing to do this brings misfortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 颠倒颐养之道，违背常理向山丘求食，前行则凶。
lines.interpretationEn: Seeking nourishment in the wrong way, deviating from the proper path. Continuing brings misfortune.

lines.textZh: 六三：拂颐，贞凶，十年勿用，无攸利。
lines.textEn: Six in the third place: Turning away from nourishment. Perseverance brings misfortune. Do not act thus for ten years. Nothing serves to further.
lines.interpretationZh: 违背颐养之道，坚持则凶，十年不可妄动，无所利益。
lines.interpretationEn: Turning away from proper nourishment. Persisting brings misfortune for ten years. Nothing furthers.

lines.textZh: 六四：颠颐吉，虎视眈眈，其欲逐逐，无咎。
lines.textEn: Six in the fourth place: Turning to the summit for provision of nourishment brings good fortune. Spying about with sharp eyes like a tiger with insatiable craving. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 向上求取颐养则吉，如虎视眈眈般专注追求，无过失。
lines.interpretationEn: Seeking nourishment from above brings fortune. Watch keenly like a tiger. No blame.

lines.textZh: 六五：拂经，居贞吉，不可涉大川。
lines.textEn: Six in the fifth place: Turning away from the path. To remain persevering brings good fortune. One should not cross the great water.
lines.interpretationZh: 虽偏离常道，但安居守正则吉，不可冒险渡大川。
lines.interpretationEn: Deviating from the path, yet remaining steadfast brings fortune. Do not cross the great water.

lines.textZh: 上九：由颐，厉吉，利涉大川。
lines.textEn: Nine at the top: The source of nourishment. Awareness of danger brings good fortune. It furthers one to cross the great water.
lines.interpretationZh: 作为颐养的源头，虽有危险但终获吉利，利于涉越大川。
lines.interpretationEn: The source of nourishment. Danger awareness brings fortune. It furthers one to cross the great water.

judgmentZh: 大过：栋桡，利有攸往，亨。
judgmentEn: Preponderance of the Great. The ridgepole sags to the breaking point. It furthers one to have somewhere to go. Success.
imageZh: 泽灭木，大过；君子以独立不惧，遁世无闷。
imageEn: The lake rises above the trees: the image of Preponderance of the Great. Thus the superior man, when he stands alone, is unconcerned, and if he has to renounce the world, he is undaunted.
interpretationZh: 大过卦象征大的过度，泽水淹没树木，形势非常。君子应独立不惧，即使遁世也无忧闷。
interpretationEn: Da Guo symbolizes great excess. The lake rises above the trees, an extraordinary situation. The superior man stands alone without fear and renounces the world without melancholy.

lines.textZh: 初六：藉用白茅，无咎。
lines.textEn: Six at the beginning: To spread white rushes underneath. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 用白茅铺垫于下，小心谨慎，无过失。
lines.interpretationEn: Spreading white rushes underneath for protection. Caution brings no blame.

lines.textZh: 九二：枯杨生稊，老夫得其女妻，无不利。
lines.textEn: Nine in the second place: A dry poplar sprouts at the root. An older man takes a young wife. Everything furthers.
lines.interpretationZh: 枯杨从根部生出新芽，老夫娶得少妻，无所不利。
lines.interpretationEn: A withered poplar sprouts anew. An old man takes a young wife. Everything furthers.

lines.textZh: 九三：栋桡，凶。
lines.textEn: Nine in the third place: The ridgepole sags to the breaking point. Misfortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 栋梁弯曲将断，凶险。
lines.interpretationEn: The ridgepole sags to the breaking point. Misfortune.

lines.textZh: 九四：栋隆，吉；有它吝。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fourth place: The ridgepole is braced. Good fortune. If there are ulterior motives, it is humiliating.
lines.interpretationZh: 栋梁高隆得到支撑，吉利；但若有其他企图则有憾。
lines.interpretationEn: The ridgepole is braced and supported. Good fortune. But ulterior motives bring humiliation.

lines.textZh: 九五：枯杨生华，老妇得其士夫，无咎无誉。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fifth place: A withered poplar puts forth flowers. An older woman takes a young husband. No blame. No praise.
lines.interpretationZh: 枯杨开花而非生芽，老妇嫁得少夫，无咎但也无荣誉。
lines.interpretationEn: A withered poplar puts forth flowers. An older woman takes a young husband. No blame, no praise.

lines.textZh: 上六：过涉灭顶，凶，无咎。
lines.textEn: Six at the top: One must go through the water. It goes over one's head. Misfortune. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 涉水过深，水没过头顶，凶险，但无可责怪。
lines.interpretationEn: Wading through water that goes over one's head. Misfortune, but no blame.

judgmentZh: 习坎：有孚，维心亨，行有尚。
judgmentEn: The Abysmal repeated. If you are sincere, you have success in your heart, and whatever you do succeeds.
imageZh: 水洊至，习坎；君子以常德行，习教事。
imageEn: Water flows on and reaches the goal: the image of the Abysmal repeated. Thus the superior man walks in lasting virtue and carries on the business of teaching.
interpretationZh: 坎卦象征重重险阻，水流不息，坎上加坎。以诚信之心面对危险，坚持正道则能通达。
interpretationEn: Kan symbolizes repeated danger, like water flowing ceaselessly into the abyss. Sincerity and steadfastness carry one through peril.

lines.textZh: 初六：习坎，入于坎窞，凶。
lines.textEn: Six at the beginning: Repetition of the Abysmal. In the abyss one falls into a pit. Misfortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 重重险陷，落入深坑，凶险。
lines.interpretationEn: Falling deeper into the abyss. Danger compounds upon danger.

lines.textZh: 九二：坎有险，求小得。
lines.textEn: Nine in the second place: The abyss is dangerous. One should strive to attain small things only.
lines.interpretationZh: 身处险境，只能求取小的收获，不可贪大。
lines.interpretationEn: In danger, seek only small gains. Do not overreach.

lines.textZh: 六三：来之坎坎，险且枕，入于坎窞，勿用。
lines.textEn: Six in the third place: Forward and backward, abyss on abyss. In danger like this, pause at first and wait. Otherwise you will fall into a pit in the abyss. Do not act this way.
lines.interpretationZh: 前后皆是险坑，进退两难，不可轻举妄动。
lines.interpretationEn: Abyss before and behind. Do not act rashly; pause and wait.

lines.textZh: 六四：樽酒簋贰，用缶，纳约自牖，终无咎。
lines.textEn: Six in the fourth place: A jug of wine, a bowl of rice with it; earthen vessels simply handed in through the window. There is certainly no blame in this.
lines.interpretationZh: 以简朴的酒食从窗户递入，诚心相交，终无过失。
lines.interpretationEn: Simple offerings passed through the window. Sincerity in humble form brings no blame.

lines.textZh: 九五：坎不盈，祗既平，无咎。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fifth place: The abyss is not filled to overflowing, it is filled only to the rim. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 水未溢出深坑，刚好填平，无过失。
lines.interpretationEn: The abyss is filled only to the rim, not overflowing. No blame.

lines.textZh: 上六：系用徽纆，寘于丛棘，三岁不得，凶。
lines.textEn: Six at the top: Bound with cords and ropes, shut up between thorn-hedged prison walls. For three years one does not find the way. Misfortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 被绳索捆绑，囚于荆棘丛中，三年不得脱身，凶险。
lines.interpretationEn: Bound and imprisoned among thorns. Three years without escape. Misfortune.

judgmentZh: 离：利贞，亨。畜牝牛，吉。
judgmentEn: The Clinging. Perseverance furthers. It brings success. Care of the cow brings good fortune.
imageZh: 明两作，离；大人以继明照于四方。
imageEn: That which is bright rises twice: the image of Fire. Thus the great man, by perpetuating this brightness, illumines the four quarters of the world.
interpretationZh: 离卦象征附丽光明，火焰重叠，光明相继。柔顺依附正道，如畜养母牛般温顺，则吉利亨通。
interpretationEn: Li symbolizes clinging to brightness, like double fire. By nurturing gentle perseverance, like tending a cow, one achieves lasting radiance.

lines.textZh: 初九：履错然，敬之无咎。
lines.textEn: Nine at the beginning: The footprints run crisscross. If one is serious about it, no blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 脚步交错纷乱，若能恭敬谨慎，则无过失。
lines.interpretationEn: Footprints run crisscross in confusion. Seriousness and respect prevent blame.

lines.textZh: 六二：黄离，元吉。
lines.textEn: Six in the second place: Yellow light. Supreme good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 黄色的光明，中正柔顺，大吉大利。
lines.interpretationEn: Yellow light — the color of the mean. Supreme good fortune.

lines.textZh: 九三：日昃之离，不鼓缶而歌，则大耋之嗟，凶。
lines.textEn: Nine in the third place: In the light of the setting sun, men either beat the pot and sing or loudly bewail the approach of old age. Misfortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 夕阳西下之时，若不击缶而歌享受当下，便会哀叹衰老，凶。
lines.interpretationEn: In the setting sun's light, either sing joyfully or lament old age. Clinging to the past brings misfortune.

lines.textZh: 九四：突如其来如，焚如，死如，弃如。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fourth place: Its coming is sudden; it flames up, dies down, is thrown away.
lines.interpretationZh: 突然而来，燃烧、熄灭、被抛弃，来势虽猛但不持久。
lines.interpretationEn: It comes suddenly — flares up, dies out, and is discarded. Intensity without endurance.

lines.textZh: 六五：出涕沱若，戚嗟若，吉。
lines.textEn: Six in the fifth place: Tears in floods, sighing and lamenting. Good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 泪如雨下，忧戚叹息，因忧患而警醒，反得吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: Floods of tears, sighs of sorrow. Through grief comes awareness, and thus good fortune.

lines.textZh: 上九：王用出征，有嘉折首，获匪其丑，无咎。
lines.textEn: Nine at the top: The king uses him to march forth and chastise. Then it is best to kill the leaders and take captive the followers. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 君王出征讨伐，斩杀首恶，宽待胁从，无过失。
lines.interpretationEn: The king marches forth to punish. Strike the leaders, spare the followers. No blame.

judgmentZh: 咸：亨，利贞，取女吉。
judgmentEn: Influence. Success. Perseverance furthers. To take a maiden to wife brings good fortune.
imageZh: 山上有泽，咸；君子以虚受人。
imageEn: A lake on the mountain: the image of Influence. Thus the superior man encourages people to approach him by his readiness to receive them.
interpretationZh: 咸卦象征感应交流，山上有泽，泽水下润山体。以虚怀若谷之心感召他人，则亨通吉利。
interpretationEn: Xian symbolizes mutual influence and attraction. A lake atop the mountain moistens it below. Openness and receptivity attract others.

lines.textZh: 初六：咸其拇。
lines.textEn: Six at the beginning: The influence shows itself in the big toe.
lines.interpretationZh: 感应在脚趾，刚刚萌动，尚未付诸行动。
lines.interpretationEn: Influence begins at the big toe. The impulse stirs but has not yet led to action.
lines.detailZh: 初六“咸其拇”——感应从脚趾开始。拇指是身体最末端、最不敏感的部位，感应刚刚萌生，微弱而不明显。这一爻描述的是感情的最初阶段——心中有了一丝悸动，但还不确定、不强烈。此时不宜急于行动，让感觉自然发展。就像种子刚刚发芽，需要时间和耐心。
lines.detailEn: The first six—'Influence manifests in the big toe'—describes the very beginning of attraction, faint and uncertain like sensation in the most distant part of the body. Don't rush to act; let feelings develop naturally, like a seed just beginning to sprout.

lines.textZh: 六二：咸其腓，凶，居吉。
lines.textEn: Six in the second place: The influence shows itself in the calves of the legs. Misfortune. Tarrying brings good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 感应在小腿，急于行动则凶，安居不动则吉。
lines.interpretationEn: Influence in the calves. Rushing forward brings misfortune; staying still brings fortune.
lines.detailZh: 六二“咸其腓，凶，居吉”——感应到了小腿肚。腓是小腿肌肉，容易冲动地跟随行动。“凶”警告不要被冲动驱使而盲目行动；“居吉”建议安静等待才是吉祥的。这一爻教导我们：感情初动时最容易冲动，但冲动往往导致错误。保持冷静、安住当下，让感情在稳定中成长。
lines.detailEn: The second six—'Influence in the calves. Misfortune. Tarrying brings good fortune'—warns against impulsive action driven by early attraction. The calves want to move, but rushing leads to mistakes. Stay calm and let feelings grow in stability.

lines.textZh: 九三：咸其股，执其随，往吝。
lines.textEn: Nine in the third place: The influence shows itself in the thighs. Holds to that which follows it. To continue is humiliating.
lines.interpretationZh: 感应在大腿，盲目追随他人，前往则有悔吝。
lines.interpretationEn: Influence in the thighs. Blindly following others leads to humiliation.
lines.detailZh: 九三“咸其股，执其随，往吝”——感应到了大腿。大腿跟随身体行动，象征盲目跟从。“执其随”意为执着于跟随他人，“往吝”意为这样前进会困窘。这一爻警告不要在感情中失去自我——不要因为喜欢一个人就完全放弃自己的判断和原则，盲目追随对方。保持独立人格，才能建立健康的关系。
lines.detailEn: The third nine—'Influence in the thighs. Holds to that which follows. Going forward brings humiliation'—warns against losing yourself in attraction. Don't abandon your judgment and principles just because you like someone. Maintain independent character to build healthy relationships.

lines.textZh: 九四：贞吉悔亡，憧憧往来，朋从尔思。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fourth place: Perseverance brings good fortune. Remorse disappears. If a man is agitated in mind and his thoughts go hither and thither, only those friends on whom he fixes his conscious thought will follow.
lines.interpretationZh: 坚守正道则吉，悔恨消失。心神不定则朋友也犹豫，专心则朋友追随。
lines.interpretationEn: Perseverance brings fortune and remorse vanishes. Focus your thoughts, and friends will follow.
lines.detailZh: 九四“贞吉悔亡，憧憧往来，朋从尔思”——感应到了心脏的位置。“憧憧往来”形容心神不宁、思绪纷飞，正是恋爱中“心猿意马”的状态。但“贞吉”说明只要保持正固就是吉祥的。“朋从尔思”意为朋友会跟随你的心意——当你的感情真诚纯粹时，自然会吸引志同道合的人。这一爻提醒我们：感情中的焦虑是正常的，关键是保持真诚。
lines.detailEn: The fourth nine—'Perseverance brings good fortune. Remorse disappears. If a man is agitated in mind, friends will follow his thoughts'—describes the restless heart of someone in love. Anxiety in romance is normal; the key is maintaining sincerity. When your feelings are genuine and pure, you naturally attract kindred spirits.

lines.textZh: 九五：咸其脢，无悔。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fifth place: The influence shows itself in the back of the neck. No remorse.
lines.interpretationZh: 感应在脊背，坚定不移，无所悔恨。
lines.interpretationEn: Influence in the back of the neck. Firm and resolute, no remorse.
lines.detailZh: 九五“咸其脢，无悔”——感应到了后背（脢为背脊肉）。后背是人看不到的地方，象征一种超越表面的深层感应。九五居尊位，代表感情已经超越了肤浅的吸引，进入了深层的心灵共鸣。“无悔”说明这种深层的感应不会带来后悔。在关系中，当两个人的连接从外在吸引深入到内在理解，就达到了咸卦九五的境界。
lines.detailEn: The fifth nine—'Influence in the back of the neck. No remorse'—represents influence reaching a deep, unseen level. The back is what we cannot see ourselves, symbolizing connection beyond the surface. When two people's bond deepens from external attraction to internal understanding, they've reached this level of profound resonance.

lines.textZh: 上六：咸其辅颊舌。
lines.textEn: Six at the top: The influence shows itself in the jaws, cheeks, and tongue.
lines.interpretationZh: 感应在口舌面颊，仅停留在言语层面，缺乏真诚。
lines.interpretationEn: Influence in the jaws, cheeks, and tongue. Mere words without substance.
lines.detailZh: 上六“咸其辅颊舌”——感应到了嘴巴和舌头。这是咸卦最表面的一爻，反而排在最后。嘴巴是说话的器官，象征用言语来表达感情。但咸卦的最高境界是“无心之感”，到了上六却变成了“用嘴说”，反而是退步。这一爻警告我们：真正的感情不需要太多甜言蜜语，过度的言语表达反而显得不真诚。“此时无声胜有声”，心灵的默契远胜于口头的承诺。
lines.detailEn: The top six—'Influence in the jaws, cheeks, and tongue'—paradoxically represents the most superficial level despite being the final line. The mouth symbolizes expressing feelings through words, but Xian's highest ideal is 'influence without intention.' Resorting to excessive sweet talk actually represents regression. True feelings don't need many words; silent understanding far surpasses verbal promises.

overviewZh: 咸卦是《周易》第三十一卦，由上兑（泽）下艮（山）组成，泽在山上，水润山体，象征感应交融。“咸”即“感”，是感应、感动之意。咸卦是《周易》下经的第一卦，下经以人伦关系为主题，而咸卦讲的正是男女之间最原始、最纯粹的感应——少男（艮）在下主动追求，少女（兑）在上欣然回应，阴阳相感，情投意合。咸卦的核心精神是“无心之感”——真正的感应不是刻意追求，而是心灵自然的共鸣。
overviewEn: Xian (Influence/Wooing) is the 31st hexagram, composed of Dui (lake) above and Gen (mountain) below—a lake on the mountain, water moistening the mountain, symbolizing mutual influence and resonance. 'Xian' means 'influence' or 'feeling'—the natural attraction between beings. As the first hexagram of the I Ching's lower section (focused on human relationships), Xian addresses the most primal, purest resonance between male and female—the young man (Gen) below actively pursues while the young woman (Dui) above joyfully responds. The core spirit is 'influence without intention'—true resonance is not forced pursuit but natural harmony of hearts.
judgmentDetailZh: 咸卦卦辞“咸：亨，利贞，取女吉”直接点明了咸卦与婚姻的关系。“取女吉”意为娶妻吉祥，这是《周易》中最明确的婚姻吉兆。《彖传》释曰：“咸，感也。柔上而刚下，二气感应以相与。止而说，男下女，是以亨利贞，取女吉也”——柔（兑）在上、刚（艮）在下，阴阳二气相互感应而结合。“止而说”即“止而悦”，艮为止、兑为悦，先有内心的安定（止），才有真诚的喜悦（悦）。男子谦逊地居于女子之下（男下女），这种真诚谦逊的态度才能赢得美好的姻缘。
judgmentDetailEn: The judgment 'Influence. Success. Perseverance furthers. To take a maiden to wife brings good fortune' directly connects Xian to marriage—the I Ching's clearest marriage blessing. The Commentary explains: 'The soft is above, the firm below; the two energies respond to each other in mutual attraction. Stillness combined with joy, the man placing himself below the woman'—first inner stillness (Gen), then genuine joy (Dui). The man humbly positions himself below the woman; this sincere, modest attitude wins a beautiful union.
imageDetailZh: 象辞“山上有泽，咸。君子以虚受人”描绘了山顶有湖泽的意象——山虽高大，顶部却有凹陷能容纳水泽，这就是“虚”的力量。“虚受人”意为以虚怀若谷的态度接纳他人。只有内心空灵、没有成见，才能真正感受到他人的心意。这与佛教“空杯心态”异曲同工——杯子满了就装不进新的水，心里满了就感受不到新的感动。在人际关系中，放下自我、虚心倾听，才能建立真正的心灵连接。
imageDetailEn: The Image 'A lake on the mountain: Influence. The superior man encourages people to approach him by his readiness to receive them' depicts a lake atop a mountain—though the mountain is tall, its summit has a hollow that holds water. This is the power of 'emptiness.' Only with an open, unprejudiced heart can one truly sense others' feelings. Like the Buddhist 'empty cup' concept—a full cup cannot receive new water; a full heart cannot receive new feelings. In relationships, setting aside ego and listening with openness creates genuine heart-to-heart connection.
modernApplicationZh: 【事业】咸卦提示人际关系和团队默契是成功的关键。“以虚受人”——放下架子、虚心倾听同事和客户的需求，才能建立真正的信任和合作。在谈判和销售中，真诚的感染力远胜于技巧性的说服。【感情】咸卦是最佳的感情卦，代表两情相悦、心灵相通。如果正在追求某人，咸卦鼓励真诚表达，不要玩套路；如果已在关系中，这是感情升温、加深默契的好时机。关键是“无心之感”——越自然越好。【财运】咸卦提示通过人脉和口碑获得财富。真诚待人、建立良好的信誉，财富自然随之而来。不适合投机取巧，适合长期经营人际关系。【健康】咸卦对应口腔和生殖系统（兑为口，艮为手），注意口腔保健和生殖健康。情绪上保持开放和愉悦，有助于整体健康。
modernApplicationEn: 【Career】Xian highlights that interpersonal relationships and team chemistry are keys to success. 'Receiving others with openness'—set aside pretense and genuinely listen to colleagues and clients to build real trust. In negotiations, sincere influence far outweighs tactical persuasion.【Love】Xian is the best hexagram for romance, representing mutual attraction and heart-to-heart connection. If pursuing someone, be genuinely expressive without games; if already together, this is a great time to deepen intimacy. The key is 'influence without intention'—the more natural, the better.【Wealth】Xian suggests gaining wealth through relationships and reputation. Treat people sincerely and build good credibility; wealth follows naturally.【Health】Xian corresponds to the mouth and reproductive system. Maintain oral health and reproductive wellness. Emotionally, stay open and joyful for overall wellbeing.
historicalStoryZh: 咸卦最著名的典故是“高山流水遇知音”的故事。春秋时期，俞伯牙善弹琴，钟子期善听琴。伯牙弹奏时心中想着高山，子期说“善哉，峨峨兮若泰山”；心中想着流水，子期说“善哉，洋洋兮若江河”。两人之间的心灵感应，正是咸卦“无心之感”的最高境界——不需要言语解释，心意自然相通。子期去世后，伯牙摔琴绝弦，终身不再弹奏，因为世间再无能“感”其心意之人。这个故事完美诠释了咸卦关于心灵共鸣的深刻内涵。
historicalStoryEn: The most famous story of Xian is 'High Mountains and Flowing Water'—the tale of finding a kindred spirit. During the Spring and Autumn period, Yu Boya played the qin while Zhong Ziqi listened. When Boya thought of mountains, Ziqi said 'Magnificent, towering like Mount Tai'; when Boya thought of rivers, Ziqi said 'Magnificent, vast like the great rivers.' Their heart-to-heart resonance perfectly embodies Xian's highest ideal of 'influence without intention'—no words needed, hearts naturally in tune. After Ziqi's death, Boya smashed his qin and never played again, for no one remained who could 'feel' his heart.
relatedHexagramsNote: 咸卦的综卦为恒卦（第32卦），咸为感应之始、恒为持久之道，先有感动才有恒久；错卦为损卦（第41卦）。咸与恒作为下经的开篇两卦，揭示了人伦关系的核心：先有心灵的感应（咸），才有关系的持久（恒）。

judgmentZh: 恒：亨，无咎，利贞，利有攸往。
judgmentEn: Duration. Success. No blame. Perseverance furthers. It furthers one to have somewhere to go.
imageZh: 雷风，恒；君子以立不易方。
imageEn: Thunder and wind: the image of Duration. Thus the superior man stands firm and does not change his direction.
interpretationZh: 恒卦象征恒久不变，雷风相与，刚柔相应。君子立身处世，坚守正道，始终如一。
interpretationEn: Heng symbolizes endurance and constancy. Thunder and wind reinforce each other. The superior man stands firm in his principles without wavering.

lines.textZh: 初六：浚恒，贞凶，无攸利。
lines.textEn: Six at the beginning: Seeking duration too hastily brings misfortune persistently. Nothing that would further.
lines.interpretationZh: 急于求取恒久，操之过急，坚持则凶，无所利益。
lines.interpretationEn: Seeking duration too hastily. Forcing constancy brings persistent misfortune.

lines.textZh: 九二：悔亡。
lines.textEn: Nine in the second place: Remorse disappears.
lines.interpretationZh: 悔恨消失，居中守正，恰到好处。
lines.interpretationEn: Remorse disappears. Holding to the middle way brings balance.

lines.textZh: 九三：不恒其德，或承之羞，贞吝。
lines.textEn: Nine in the third place: He who does not give duration to his character meets with disgrace. Persistent humiliation.
lines.interpretationZh: 不能持守德行，必遭羞辱，即使坚持也有悔吝。
lines.interpretationEn: Failing to maintain one's virtue invites disgrace. Persistent humiliation.

lines.textZh: 九四：田无禽。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fourth place: No game in the field.
lines.interpretationZh: 田中无猎物，方向不对，久守无获。
lines.interpretationEn: No game in the field. Persisting in the wrong place yields nothing.

lines.textZh: 六五：恒其德，贞，妇人吉，夫子凶。
lines.textEn: Six in the fifth place: Giving duration to one's character through perseverance. This is good fortune for a woman but misfortune for a man.
lines.interpretationZh: 恒守其德，对妇人而言吉利，对男子而言则凶。柔顺守恒宜阴不宜阳。
lines.interpretationEn: Persevering in one's character. Good fortune for a woman, misfortune for a man. Yielding constancy suits the receptive.

lines.textZh: 上六：振恒，凶。
lines.textEn: Six at the top: Restlessness as an enduring condition brings misfortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 躁动不安作为常态，凶险。恒久之道在于安定，非在于躁动。
lines.interpretationEn: Restlessness as a permanent state brings misfortune. Duration requires stillness, not agitation.

judgmentZh: 遁：亨，小利贞。
judgmentEn: Retreat. Success. In what is small, perseverance furthers.
imageZh: 天下有山，遁；君子以远小人，不恶而严。
imageEn: Mountain under heaven: the image of Retreat. Thus the superior man keeps the inferior man at a distance, not angrily but with reserve.
interpretationZh: 遁卦象征退避隐遁，天下有山，山虽高却不及天。小人势力渐长，君子宜适时退让，保全正道。
interpretationEn: Dun symbolizes retreat and withdrawal. The mountain under heaven cannot reach the sky. When inferior forces grow, the wise withdraw to preserve integrity.

lines.textZh: 初六：遁尾，厉，勿用有攸往。
lines.textEn: Six at the beginning: At the tail in retreat. This is dangerous. One must not wish to undertake anything.
lines.interpretationZh: 退避时落在最后，处境危险，不宜有所行动。
lines.interpretationEn: Lagging behind in retreat is dangerous. Do not undertake anything.

lines.textZh: 六二：执之用黄牛之革，莫之胜说。
lines.textEn: Six in the second place: He holds him fast with yellow oxhide. No one can tear him loose.
lines.interpretationZh: 用黄牛皮绳牢牢绑住，无人能解开，坚守不退。
lines.interpretationEn: Held fast with yellow oxhide. No one can tear him away. Firm resolve.

lines.textZh: 九三：系遁，有疾厉，畜臣妾吉。
lines.textEn: Nine in the third place: A halted retreat is nerve-wracking and dangerous. To retain people as men- and maidservants brings good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 被牵绊而不能退避，有危险。蓄养仆从则吉。
lines.interpretationEn: A halted retreat is dangerous and exhausting. Retaining servants brings fortune.

lines.textZh: 九四：好遁，君子吉，小人否。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fourth place: Voluntary retreat brings good fortune to the superior man and downfall to the inferior man.
lines.interpretationZh: 从容退避，君子吉利，小人则不然。
lines.interpretationEn: Voluntary retreat. The superior man finds fortune; the inferior man does not.

lines.textZh: 九五：嘉遁，贞吉。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fifth place: Friendly retreat. Perseverance brings good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 美好的退避，坚守正道则吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: A gracious retreat. Perseverance brings good fortune.

lines.textZh: 上九：肥遁，无不利。
lines.textEn: Nine at the top: Cheerful retreat. Everything serves to further.
lines.interpretationZh: 远走高飞，从容退隐，无往不利。
lines.interpretationEn: A cheerful, unencumbered retreat. Everything furthers.

judgmentZh: 大壮：利贞。
judgmentEn: The Power of the Great. Perseverance furthers.
imageZh: 雷在天上，大壮；君子以非礼弗履。
imageEn: Thunder in heaven above: the image of the Power of the Great. Thus the superior man does not tread upon paths that do not accord with established order.
interpretationZh: 大壮卦象征强大的力量，雷在天上，声势浩大。阳刚之力正盛，但须以正道约束，非礼勿行。
interpretationEn: Da Zhuang symbolizes great power. Thunder above heaven is mighty. Yang force is at its peak, but must be guided by propriety.

lines.textZh: 初九：壮于趾，征凶，有孚。
lines.textEn: Nine at the beginning: Power in the toes. Continuing brings misfortune. This is certainly true.
lines.interpretationZh: 力量在脚趾，急于前进则凶，确实如此。
lines.interpretationEn: Power in the toes. Advancing rashly brings misfortune. This is certain.

lines.textZh: 九二：贞吉。
lines.textEn: Nine in the second place: Perseverance brings good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 坚守正道则吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: Perseverance brings good fortune.

lines.textZh: 九三：小人用壮，君子用罔，贞厉。羝羊触藩，羸其角。
lines.textEn: Nine in the third place: The inferior man works through power. The superior man does not act thus. To continue is dangerous. A goat butts against a hedge and gets its horns entangled.
lines.interpretationZh: 小人逞强用力，君子不这样做。公羊抵篱笆，角被缠住，蛮力行事有危险。
lines.interpretationEn: The inferior man uses brute force. The superior man does not. A goat butts a hedge and tangles its horns.

lines.textZh: 九四：贞吉悔亡，藩决不羸，壮于大舆之輹。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fourth place: Perseverance brings good fortune. Remorse disappears. The hedge opens; there is no entanglement. Power depends upon the axle of a big cart.
lines.interpretationZh: 坚守正道则吉，悔恨消失。篱笆打开不再缠绕，力量如大车之轴般稳固。
lines.interpretationEn: Perseverance brings fortune and remorse vanishes. The hedge opens. Power rests on the axle of a great cart.

lines.textZh: 六五：丧羊于易，无悔。
lines.textEn: Six in the fifth place: Loses the goat with ease. No remorse.
lines.interpretationZh: 轻易失去公羊，但无悔恨，放弃刚强反而得安。
lines.interpretationEn: The goat is lost with ease. No remorse. Letting go of stubbornness brings peace.

lines.textZh: 上六：羝羊触藩，不能退，不能遂，无攸利，艰则吉。
lines.textEn: Six at the top: A goat butts against a hedge. It cannot go backward, it cannot go forward. Nothing serves to further. If one notes the difficulty, this brings good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 公羊抵篱笆，进退两难，无所利。认识到困难则吉。
lines.interpretationEn: A goat stuck in a hedge, unable to advance or retreat. Recognizing the difficulty brings fortune.

judgmentZh: 晋：康侯用锡马蕃庶，昼日三接。
judgmentEn: Progress. The powerful prince is honored with horses in large numbers. In a single day he is granted audience three times.
imageZh: 明出地上，晋；君子以自昭明德。
imageEn: The sun rises over the earth: the image of Progress. Thus the superior man himself brightens his bright virtue.
interpretationZh: 晋卦象征进步晋升，太阳升出地面，光明普照。如康侯受封赏马匹，一日三次觐见，事业蒸蒸日上。
interpretationEn: Jin symbolizes progress and advancement. The sun rises above the earth, spreading light. Like a prince honored with horses and granted triple audience in one day.

lines.textZh: 初六：晋如，摧如，贞吉。罔孚，裕无咎。
lines.textEn: Six at the beginning: Progressing, but turned back. Perseverance brings good fortune. If one meets with no confidence, one should remain calm. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 前进又受挫，坚守正道则吉。不被信任时保持宽裕从容，无过失。
lines.interpretationEn: Advancing but pushed back. Persevere for fortune. If not trusted, remain calm. No blame.

lines.textZh: 六二：晋如，愁如，贞吉。受兹介福，于其王母。
lines.textEn: Six in the second place: Progressing, but in sorrow. Perseverance brings good fortune. Then one obtains great happiness from one's ancestress.
lines.interpretationZh: 前进中有忧愁，坚守正道则吉。从祖母处获得大福。
lines.interpretationEn: Advancing in sorrow. Perseverance brings fortune. Great happiness comes from the ancestress.

lines.textZh: 六三：众允，悔亡。
lines.textEn: Six in the third place: All are in accord. Remorse disappears.
lines.interpretationZh: 众人信任赞同，悔恨消失。
lines.interpretationEn: All are in agreement. Remorse vanishes.

lines.textZh: 九四：晋如鼫鼠，贞厉。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fourth place: Progress like a hamster. Perseverance brings danger.
lines.interpretationZh: 像鼫鼠一样贪婪地前进，坚持下去有危险。
lines.interpretationEn: Advancing like a hamster, hoarding greedily. Perseverance brings danger.

lines.textZh: 六五：悔亡，失得勿恤，往吉，无不利。
lines.textEn: Six in the fifth place: Remorse disappears. Take not gain and loss to heart. Undertakings bring good fortune. Everything serves to further.
lines.interpretationZh: 悔恨消失，不必忧虑得失，前往则吉，无往不利。
lines.interpretationEn: Remorse vanishes. Do not worry about gain or loss. Going forward brings fortune in all things.

lines.textZh: 上九：晋其角，维用伐邑，厉吉无咎，贞吝。
lines.textEn: Nine at the top: Making progress with the horns is permissible only for the purpose of punishing one's own city. To be conscious of danger brings good fortune. No blame. Perseverance brings humiliation.
lines.interpretationZh: 以角猛进，只可用于征伐自己的城邑。知危则吉，无咎，但固执则有憾。
lines.interpretationEn: Advancing with horns, only to discipline one's own domain. Awareness of danger brings fortune. Stubbornness brings humiliation.

judgmentZh: 明夷：利艰贞。
judgmentEn: Darkening of the Light. In adversity it furthers one to be persevering.
imageZh: 明入地中，明夷；君子以莅众，用晦而明。
imageEn: The light has sunk into the earth: the image of Darkening of the Light. Thus does the superior man live with the great mass: he veils his light, yet still shines.
interpretationZh: 明夷卦象征光明受损，太阳沉入地下。处于黑暗逆境之中，宜韬光养晦，内心保持光明。
interpretationEn: Ming Yi symbolizes the darkening of the light. The sun has sunk beneath the earth. In dark times, conceal your brilliance outwardly while keeping inner light alive.

lines.textZh: 初九：明夷于飞，垂其翼。君子于行，三日不食。有攸往，主人有言。
lines.textEn: Nine at the beginning: Darkening of the light during flight. He lowers his wings. The superior man does not eat for three days on his wanderings. But he has somewhere to go. The host has occasion to gossip.
lines.interpretationZh: 光明受损如鸟飞行垂翼，君子出行三日不食，虽有目的地，但主人有闲言。
lines.interpretationEn: The light darkens in flight; wings droop. The wanderer goes hungry for three days. He has a destination, but the host gossips.

lines.textZh: 六二：明夷，夷于左股，用拯马壮，吉。
lines.textEn: Six in the second place: Darkening of the light injures him in the left thigh. He gives aid with the strength of a horse. Good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 光明受损伤及左腿，用壮马之力救援，吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: The light is wounded in the left thigh. Rescue comes with the strength of a horse. Good fortune.

lines.textZh: 九三：明夷于南狩，得其大首，不可疾贞。
lines.textEn: Nine in the third place: Darkening of the light during the hunt in the south. Their great leader is captured. One must not expect perseverance too hastily.
lines.interpretationZh: 在南方狩猎时光明受损，擒获大首领，但不可急于求成。
lines.interpretationEn: During the southern hunt, the great leader is captured. Do not expect quick results.

lines.textZh: 六四：入于左腹，获明夷之心，出于门庭。
lines.textEn: Six in the fourth place: He penetrates the left side of the belly. One gets at the very heart of the darkening of the light, and leaves gate and courtyard.
lines.interpretationZh: 深入左腹，洞悉明夷的核心，然后离开门庭。
lines.interpretationEn: Penetrating the left belly, one grasps the heart of the darkness and departs through the gate.

lines.textZh: 六五：箕子之明夷，利贞。
lines.textEn: Six in the fifth place: Darkening of the light as with Prince Chi. Perseverance furthers.
lines.interpretationZh: 如箕子般韬光养晦，坚守正道则有利。
lines.interpretationEn: Darkening of the light as with Prince Chi. Conceal your brilliance. Perseverance furthers.

lines.textZh: 上六：不明晦，初登于天，后入于地。
lines.textEn: Six at the top: Not light but darkness. First he climbed up to heaven, then he plunged into the depths of the earth.
lines.interpretationZh: 不是光明而是黑暗。先登上天空，后坠入地下，由盛转衰。
lines.interpretationEn: Not light but darkness. First ascending to heaven, then plunging into the earth. From height to ruin.

judgmentZh: 家人：利女贞。
judgmentEn: The Family. The perseverance of the woman furthers.
imageZh: 风自火出，家人；君子以言有物而行有恒。
imageEn: Wind comes forth from fire: the image of the Family. Thus the superior man has substance in his words and duration in his way of life.
interpretationZh: 家人卦象征家庭，风从火中生出，火旺则风生。治家之道在于言行一致，持之以恒。
interpretationEn: Jia Ren symbolizes the family. Wind emerges from fire — when the hearth burns bright, its influence spreads. Governing the family requires consistency in word and deed.

lines.textZh: 初九：闲有家，悔亡。
lines.textEn: Nine at the beginning: Firm seclusion within the family. Remorse disappears.
lines.interpretationZh: 治家之初即立规矩，防患于未然，悔恨消失。
lines.interpretationEn: Establish firm rules for the family from the start. Prevention removes remorse.

lines.textZh: 六二：无攸遂，在中馈，贞吉。
lines.textEn: Six in the second place: She should not follow her whims. She must attend within to the food. Perseverance brings good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 不可随心所欲，专注于家中饮食之事，坚守正道则吉。
lines.interpretationEn: Do not follow whims. Attend to nourishing the household. Perseverance brings fortune.

lines.textZh: 九三：家人嗃嗃，悔厉吉；妇子嘻嘻，终吝。
lines.textEn: Nine in the third place: When tempers flare up in the family, too great severity brings remorse. Good fortune nonetheless. When woman and child dally and laugh, it leads in the end to humiliation.
lines.interpretationZh: 治家严厉虽有悔恨但终究吉利；若妇孺嬉笑无度，终有困窘。
lines.interpretationEn: Strictness in the family may cause regret but leads to fortune. Excessive laxity ends in humiliation.

lines.textZh: 六四：富家，大吉。
lines.textEn: Six in the fourth place: She is the treasure of the house. Great good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 使家庭富裕兴旺，大吉大利。
lines.interpretationEn: She enriches the family. Great good fortune.

lines.textZh: 九五：王假有家，勿恤，吉。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fifth place: As a king he approaches his family. Fear not. Good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 君王以仁爱治家，不必忧虑，吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: The king approaches his family with love. No need to worry. Good fortune.

lines.textZh: 上九：有孚威如，终吉。
lines.textEn: Nine at the top: His work commands respect. In the end good fortune comes.
lines.interpretationZh: 有诚信且有威严，最终吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: Sincerity combined with dignity. In the end, good fortune comes.

judgmentZh: 睽：小事吉。
judgmentEn: Opposition. In small matters, good fortune.
imageZh: 上火下泽，睽；君子以同而异。
imageEn: Above, fire; below, the lake: the image of Opposition. Thus amid all fellowship the superior man retains his individuality.
interpretationZh: 睽卦象征乖离对立，火向上燃，泽水向下流，方向相背。小事可成，大事宜慎。君子求同存异。
interpretationEn: Kui symbolizes opposition. Fire flames upward, the lake seeps downward — they move apart. Small matters succeed. The superior man maintains individuality within fellowship.

lines.textZh: 初九：悔亡，丧马勿逐，自复；见恶人，无咎。
lines.textEn: Nine at the beginning: Remorse disappears. If you lose your horse, do not run after it; it will come back of its own accord. When you see evil people, guard yourself against mistakes.
lines.interpretationZh: 悔恨消失。丢失马匹不必追赶，它会自己回来。见到恶人，谨慎应对则无咎。
lines.interpretationEn: Remorse vanishes. A lost horse returns on its own. When meeting evil people, guard against error.

lines.textZh: 九二：遇主于巷，无咎。
lines.textEn: Nine in the second place: One meets his lord in a narrow street. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 在小巷中偶遇主人，不期而遇，无过失。
lines.interpretationEn: Meeting one's lord in a narrow lane. An unexpected encounter. No blame.

lines.textZh: 六三：见舆曳，其牛掣，其人天且劓，无初有终。
lines.textEn: Six in the third place: One sees the wagon dragged back, the oxen halted, a man's hair and nose cut off. Not a good beginning, but a good end.
lines.interpretationZh: 看到车被拖住，牛被阻止，人受刑罚。开头不好但结局尚可。
lines.interpretationEn: The wagon is dragged back, the oxen halted, the man punished. A bad start but a good end.

lines.textZh: 九四：睽孤，遇元夫，交孚，厉无咎。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fourth place: Isolated through opposition, one meets a like-minded man with whom one can associate in good faith. Despite the danger, no blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 因乖离而孤独，遇到志同道合之人，以诚信相交，虽有危险但无咎。
lines.interpretationEn: Isolated by opposition, one meets a kindred spirit. Sincere fellowship amid danger brings no blame.

lines.textZh: 六五：悔亡，厥宗噬肤，往何咎。
lines.textEn: Six in the fifth place: Remorse disappears. The companion bites his way through the wrappings. If one goes to him, how could it be a mistake?
lines.interpretationZh: 悔恨消失，同宗之人穿透阻隔来亲近，前往则无过失。
lines.interpretationEn: Remorse vanishes. The companion breaks through barriers. Going to meet him — how could that be wrong?

lines.textZh: 上九：睽孤，见豕负涂，载鬼一车，先张之弧，后说之弧，匪寇婚媾，往遇雨则吉。
lines.textEn: Nine at the top: Isolated through opposition, one sees one's companion as a pig covered with dirt, as a wagon full of devils. First one draws a bow against him, then one lays the bow aside. He is not a robber; he will woo at the right time. As one goes, rain falls; then good fortune comes.
lines.interpretationZh: 因乖离而孤立，误解同伴，先敌视后释然。对方并非敌人而是求婚者，前行遇雨则吉。
lines.interpretationEn: Isolated and suspicious, one misjudges a companion. Hostility gives way to understanding. Going forward, rain falls and fortune comes.

judgmentZh: 蹇：利西南，不利东北。利见大人，贞吉。
judgmentEn: Obstruction. The southwest furthers. The northeast does not further. It furthers one to see the great man. Perseverance brings good fortune.
imageZh: 山上有水，蹇；君子以反身修德。
imageEn: Water on the mountain: the image of Obstruction. Thus the superior man turns his attention to himself and molds his character.
interpretationZh: 蹇卦象征艰难险阻，山上有水，行路艰难。宜向平坦之处（西南）前进，反省自身，修养德行。
interpretationEn: Jian symbolizes obstruction. Water on the mountain makes the path treacherous. Move toward the easy ground. Turn inward and cultivate virtue.

lines.textZh: 初六：往蹇，来誉。
lines.textEn: Six at the beginning: Going leads to obstructions, coming meets with praise.
lines.interpretationZh: 前往则遇险阻，退回则获赞誉。宜暂时退守。
lines.interpretationEn: Going forward meets obstruction; turning back wins praise. Retreat for now.

lines.textZh: 六二：王臣蹇蹇，匪躬之故。
lines.textEn: Six in the second place: The king's servant is beset by obstruction upon obstruction, but it is not his own fault.
lines.interpretationZh: 王的臣子历经重重艰难，并非因为自身的缘故，而是为了尽忠职守。
lines.interpretationEn: The king's servant faces obstruction after obstruction, not through personal fault but from devotion to duty.

lines.textZh: 九三：往蹇，来反。
lines.textEn: Nine in the third place: Going leads to obstructions; hence he comes back.
lines.interpretationZh: 前往则遇阻碍，因此返回。宜退守等待时机。
lines.interpretationEn: Going forward meets obstruction, so one turns back. Wait for the right moment.

lines.textZh: 六四：往蹇，来连。
lines.textEn: Six in the fourth place: Going leads to obstructions, coming leads to union.
lines.interpretationZh: 前往则遇阻碍，退回则能与人联合。宜团结合作。
lines.interpretationEn: Going forward meets obstruction; turning back leads to union. Seek allies.

lines.textZh: 九五：大蹇，朋来。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fifth place: In the midst of the greatest obstructions, friends come.
lines.interpretationZh: 身处最大的困难之中，朋友前来相助。
lines.interpretationEn: In the greatest obstruction, friends arrive to help.

lines.textZh: 上六：往蹇，来硕，吉；利见大人。
lines.textEn: Six at the top: Going leads to obstructions, coming leads to great good fortune. It furthers one to see the great man.
lines.interpretationZh: 前往则遇阻碍，退回则有大收获，吉利。宜拜见大人物。
lines.interpretationEn: Going meets obstruction; returning brings great fortune. It furthers one to see the great man.

overviewZh: 解卦象征解除困难，雷雨交作，阴霾散去。困难已解，宜宽恕过失，迅速回归正常。
overviewEn: Xie symbolizes deliverance from difficulty. Thunder and rain clear the air. With hardship resolved, pardon mistakes and return swiftly to normalcy.
judgmentZh: 解：利西南，无所往，其来复吉。有攸往，夙吉。
judgmentEn: Deliverance. The southwest furthers. If there is no longer anything where one has to go, return brings good fortune. If there is still something where one has to go, hastening brings good fortune.
imageZh: 雷雨作，解；君子以赦过宥罪。
imageEn: Thunder and rain set in: the image of Deliverance. Thus the superior man pardons mistakes and forgives misdeeds.
interpretationZh: 解卦象征解除困难，雷雨交作，阴霾散去。困难已解，宜宽恕过失，迅速回归正常。
interpretationEn: Xie symbolizes deliverance from difficulty. Thunder and rain clear the air. With hardship resolved, pardon mistakes and return swiftly to normalcy.

lines.textZh: 初六：无咎。
lines.textEn: Six at the beginning: Without blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 困难刚刚解除，安静无为，没有过失。
lines.interpretationEn: Difficulty has just been resolved. Remain quiet and blameless.

lines.textZh: 九二：田获三狐，得黄矢，贞吉。
lines.textEn: Nine in the second place: One kills three foxes in the field and receives a yellow arrow. Perseverance brings good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 在田猎中获得三只狐狸，得到黄色箭矢，坚守正道则吉。
lines.interpretationEn: Three foxes are caught in the hunt and a yellow arrow is gained. Perseverance brings fortune.

lines.textZh: 六三：负且乘，致寇至，贞吝。
lines.textEn: Six in the third place: If a man carries a burden on his back and nonetheless rides in a carriage, he thereby encourages robbers to draw near. Perseverance leads to humiliation.
lines.interpretationZh: 背着重物却乘坐马车，招致盗贼前来。不当其位，坚持则有困窘。
lines.interpretationEn: Carrying a load while riding in a carriage invites robbers. Holding a position one does not deserve brings humiliation.

lines.textZh: 九四：解而拇，朋至斯孚。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fourth place: Deliver yourself from your great toe. Then the companion comes, and him you can trust.
lines.interpretationZh: 解除束缚你的小人，志同道合的朋友就会到来，彼此信任。
lines.interpretationEn: Free yourself from unworthy ties. Then trustworthy companions will come.

lines.textZh: 六五：君子维有解，吉；有孚于小人。
lines.textEn: Six in the fifth place: If only the superior man can deliver himself, it brings good fortune. Thus he proves to inferior men that he is in earnest.
lines.interpretationZh: 君子能自我解脱则吉利，以诚信感化小人。
lines.interpretationEn: The superior man delivers himself — good fortune. His sincerity wins over even petty people.

lines.textZh: 上六：公用射隼于高墉之上，获之，无不利。
lines.textEn: Six at the top: The prince shoots at a hawk on a high wall. He kills it. Everything serves to further.
lines.interpretationZh: 王公在高墙上射中隼鸟，有所斩获，无往不利。
lines.interpretationEn: The prince shoots a hawk upon the high wall and hits it. Everything furthers.

judgmentZh: 损：有孚，元吉，无咎，可贞，利有攸往。曷之用，二簋可用享。
judgmentEn: Decrease combined with sincerity brings about supreme good fortune without blame. One may be persevering in this. It furthers one to undertake something. How is this to be carried out? One may use two small bowls for the sacrifice.
imageZh: 山下有泽，损；君子以惩忿窒欲。
imageEn: At the foot of the mountain, the lake: the image of Decrease. Thus the superior man controls his anger and restrains his instincts.
interpretationZh: 损卦象征减损，山下有泽，泽水蒸发润泽山林。减损自己以利他人，诚心则大吉。损下益上，克制私欲，修养德行。
interpretationEn: Sun symbolizes decrease and sacrifice. The lake at the foot of the mountain evaporates to nourish it. Sincere self-sacrifice for the greater good brings supreme fortune.

lines.textZh: 初九：已事遄往，无咎，酌损之。
lines.textEn: Nine at the beginning: Going quickly when one's tasks are finished is without blame. But one must reflect on how much one may decrease others.
lines.interpretationZh: 办完自己的事赶快前去帮助，无过失，但要斟酌减损的程度。
lines.interpretationEn: Finish your own tasks quickly and go to help. Consider how much to give.

lines.textZh: 九二：利贞，征凶，弗损益之。
lines.textEn: Nine in the second place: Perseverance furthers. To undertake something brings misfortune. Without decreasing oneself, one is able to bring increase to others.
lines.interpretationZh: 宜坚守正道，冒进则凶。不减损自己也能使他人受益。
lines.interpretationEn: Persevere in your position. Rash action brings misfortune. One can benefit others without diminishing oneself.

lines.textZh: 六三：三人行则损一人，一人行则得其友。
lines.textEn: Six in the third place: When three people journey together, their number decreases by one. When one man journeys alone, he finds a companion.
lines.interpretationZh: 三人同行必减一人，一人独行则得良友。事物需要配合得当。
lines.interpretationEn: Three travelers lose one companion. A lone traveler finds a friend. Proper pairing is essential.

lines.textZh: 六四：损其疾，使遄有喜，无咎。
lines.textEn: Six in the fourth place: If a man decreases his faults, it makes the other hasten to come and rejoice. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 减损自己的毛病，使人欢喜前来，无过失。
lines.interpretationEn: Decrease your faults and others will joyfully come to you. No blame.

lines.textZh: 六五：或益之十朋之龟，弗克违，元吉。
lines.textEn: Six in the fifth place: Someone does indeed increase him. Ten pairs of tortoises cannot oppose it. Supreme good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 有人赠予价值十朋的神龟，不可违拒，大吉。
lines.interpretationEn: Someone bestows increase. Even ten pairs of tortoises cannot oppose this. Supreme good fortune.

lines.textZh: 上九：弗损益之，无咎，贞吉，利有攸往，得臣无家。
lines.textEn: Nine at the top: If one is increased without depriving others, there is no blame. Perseverance brings good fortune. It furthers one to undertake something. One obtains servants but no longer has a separate home.
lines.interpretationZh: 不减损别人而增益自己，无咎，坚守正道则吉，利于有所作为。
lines.interpretationEn: Increase without decreasing others. No blame. Perseverance brings fortune. One gains followers who share a common purpose.

judgmentZh: 益：利有攸往，利涉大川。
judgmentEn: Increase. It furthers one to undertake something. It furthers one to cross the great water.
imageZh: 风雷，益；君子以见善则迁，有过则改。
imageEn: Wind and thunder: the image of Increase. Thus the superior man, if he sees good, imitates it; if he has faults, he rids himself of them.
interpretationZh: 益卦象征增益，风雷激荡，相互助长。损上益下，利于行动和冒险。见贤思齐，有过即改。
interpretationEn: Yi symbolizes increase. Wind and thunder strengthen each other. The upper decreases to benefit the lower. A time for action, improvement, and crossing obstacles.

lines.textZh: 初九：利用为大作，元吉，无咎。
lines.textEn: Nine at the beginning: It furthers one to accomplish great deeds. Supreme good fortune. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 利于做大事，大吉，无过失。得到上方的增益支持。
lines.interpretationEn: It furthers one to accomplish great deeds. Supreme good fortune and no blame.

lines.textZh: 六二：或益之十朋之龟，弗克违，永贞吉。王用享于帝，吉。
lines.textEn: Six in the second place: Someone does indeed increase him; ten pairs of tortoises cannot oppose it. Constant perseverance brings good fortune. The king presents him before God. Good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 有人赠予十朋之龟，不可违拒，永远坚守正道则吉。君王祭祀上帝，吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: Someone bestows great increase. Ten pairs of tortoises cannot oppose it. Eternal perseverance brings fortune. The king makes offerings to God.

lines.textZh: 六三：益之用凶事，无咎。有孚中行，告公用圭。
lines.textEn: Six in the third place: One is enriched through unfortunate events. No blame, if you are sincere and walk in the middle, and report to the prince with a seal.
lines.interpretationZh: 因凶事而获益，无过失。有诚信行中道，以玉圭禀告公侯。
lines.interpretationEn: Increase comes through misfortune. No blame if sincere and following the middle way. Report to authority with proper ceremony.

lines.textZh: 六四：中行，告公从。利用为依迁国。
lines.textEn: Six in the fourth place: If you walk in the middle and report to the prince, he will follow. It furthers one to be used in the removal of the capital.
lines.interpretationZh: 行中道禀告公侯，公侯听从。利于作为依靠迁移国都。
lines.interpretationEn: Walking the middle way and reporting to the prince, he follows. It furthers one to assist in moving the capital.

lines.textZh: 九五：有孚惠心，勿问元吉。有孚惠我德。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fifth place: If in truth you have a kind heart, ask not. Supreme good fortune. Truly, kindness will be recognized as your virtue.
lines.interpretationZh: 有诚信和仁爱之心，不必问，大吉。诚信仁爱就是我的德行。
lines.interpretationEn: With a sincere and kind heart, do not ask — supreme good fortune. Your kindness is recognized as true virtue.

lines.textZh: 上九：莫益之，或击之，立心勿恒，凶。
lines.textEn: Nine at the top: He brings increase to no one. Indeed, someone even strikes him. He does not keep his heart constantly steady. Misfortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 没有人增益他，反而有人攻击他。心志不坚定，凶险。
lines.interpretationEn: No one increases him; indeed he is attacked. An inconstant heart brings misfortune.

judgmentZh: 夬：扬于王庭，孚号，有厉，告自邑，不利即戎，利有攸往。
judgmentEn: Breakthrough. One must resolutely make the matter known at the court of the king. It must be announced truthfully. Danger. It is necessary to notify one's own city. It does not further to resort to arms. It furthers one to undertake something.
imageZh: 泽上于天，夬；君子以施禄及下，居德则忌。
imageEn: The lake has risen up to heaven: the image of Breakthrough. Thus the superior man dispenses riches downward and refrains from resting on his virtue.
interpretationZh: 夬卦象征决断突破，泽水升于天上，终将倾泻而下。五阳决一阴，以正除邪，但须光明正大，不可诉诸武力。
interpretationEn: Guai symbolizes breakthrough and resolution. The lake rises to heaven and must overflow. Five yang lines resolve one yin. Act openly and justly, not by force.

lines.textZh: 初九：壮于前趾，往不胜为咎。
lines.textEn: Nine at the beginning: Mighty in the forward-striding toes. When one goes and is not equal to the undertaking, one makes a mistake.
lines.interpretationZh: 脚趾强壮急于前行，若力不能胜则有过失。
lines.interpretationEn: Mighty in the forward-striding toes. Going forward without adequate strength leads to blame.

lines.textZh: 九二：惕号，莫夜有戎，勿恤。
lines.textEn: Nine in the second place: A cry of alarm. Arms at evening and at night. Fear nothing.
lines.interpretationZh: 警惕呼号，夜间有兵戎之事，但不必忧虑。
lines.interpretationEn: A cry of alarm. Arms in the evening and night. Do not fear.

lines.textZh: 九三：壮于頄，有凶。君子夬夬，独行遇雨，若濡有愠，无咎。
lines.textEn: Nine in the third place: To be powerful in the cheekbones brings misfortune. The superior man is firmly resolved. He walks alone and is caught in the rain. He is bespattered, and people murmur against him. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 刚强表现在面颊上，有凶险。君子果断决行，独行遇雨被淋湿遭人怨怒，但无过失。
lines.interpretationEn: Showing strength in the face brings misfortune. The resolute man walks alone in the rain, bespattered and resented, but bears no blame.

lines.textZh: 九四：臀无肤，其行次且。牵羊悔亡，闻言不信。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fourth place: There is no skin on his thighs, and walking comes hard. If a man were to let himself be led like a sheep, remorse would disappear. But if these words are heard, they would not be believed.
lines.interpretationZh: 臀部没有皮肤，行走艰难。若能像羊一样被牵引则悔恨消失，但听到这话却不肯相信。
lines.interpretationEn: No skin on the thighs; walking is painful. Let yourself be led like a sheep and remorse vanishes. But these words are not believed.

lines.textZh: 九五：苋陆夬夬，中行无咎。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fifth place: In dealing with weeds, firm resolution is necessary. Walking in the middle remains free of blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 像拔除苋陆杂草一样果断决断，行中道则无过失。
lines.interpretationEn: Firm resolution in uprooting weeds. Walking the middle path remains free of blame.

lines.textZh: 上六：无号，终有凶。
lines.textEn: Six at the top: No cry. In the end misfortune comes.
lines.interpretationZh: 没有号令警示，最终有凶险。阴柔居顶，孤立无援。
lines.interpretationEn: No cry of warning. In the end, misfortune comes. The weak at the top stands alone.

judgmentZh: 姤：女壮，勿用取女。
judgmentEn: Coming to Meet. The maiden is powerful. One should not marry such a maiden.
imageZh: 天下有风，姤；后以施命诰四方。
imageEn: Under heaven, wind: the image of Coming to Meet. Thus does the prince act when disseminating his commands and proclaiming them to the four quarters of heaven.
interpretationZh: 姤卦象征不期而遇，天下有风，无所不至。一阴初生于五阳之下，阴柔势力渐长，宜警惕防范。
interpretationEn: Gou symbolizes unexpected encounter. Wind under heaven reaches everywhere. One yin arises beneath five yang lines — the dark force grows. Be vigilant.

lines.textZh: 初六：系于金柅，贞吉，有攸往，见凶，羸豕孚蹢躅。
lines.textEn: Six at the beginning: It must be checked with a brake of bronze. Perseverance brings good fortune. If one lets it take its course, one experiences misfortune. Even a lean pig has it in him to rage around.
lines.interpretationZh: 用金属制动器拴住，坚守正道则吉。若放任则见凶，瘦弱的猪也会躁动不安。
lines.interpretationEn: Check it with a bronze brake. Perseverance brings fortune. Left unchecked, even a lean pig rages about. Misfortune.

lines.textZh: 九二：包有鱼，无咎，不利宾。
lines.textEn: Nine in the second place: There is a fish in the tank. No blame. It does not further guests.
lines.interpretationZh: 厨房中有鱼，无过失，但不宜招待宾客。
lines.interpretationEn: A fish in the tank. No blame. But it does not further one to entertain guests.

lines.textZh: 九三：臀无肤，其行次且，厉，无大咎。
lines.textEn: Nine in the third place: There is no skin on his thighs, and walking comes hard. If one is mindful of the danger, no great mistake is made.
lines.interpretationZh: 臀部没有皮肤，行走艰难，有危险，但无大过失。
lines.interpretationEn: No skin on the thighs; walking is difficult. Danger, but no great blame if one remains mindful.

lines.textZh: 九四：包无鱼，起凶。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fourth place: No fish in the tank. This leads to misfortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 厨房中没有鱼，由此引发凶险。失去了应有的联系。
lines.interpretationEn: No fish in the tank. This gives rise to misfortune. The connection is lost.

lines.textZh: 九五：以杞包瓜，含章，有陨自天。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fifth place: A melon covered with willow leaves. Hidden lines. Then it drops down to one from heaven.
lines.interpretationZh: 用杞柳包裹甜瓜，内含文采，有从天而降的好运。
lines.interpretationEn: A melon wrapped in willow leaves. Hidden brilliance within. Something drops from heaven.

lines.textZh: 上九：姤其角，吝，无咎。
lines.textEn: Nine at the top: He comes to meet with his horns. Humiliation. But no blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 用角去迎接，虽有困窘，但无过失。刚强过度，不善交际。
lines.interpretationEn: Coming to meet with horns. Humiliation, but no blame. Excessive rigidity in social encounters.

judgmentZh: 萃：亨。王假有庙，利见大人，亨，利贞。用大牲吉，利有攸往。
judgmentEn: Gathering Together. Success. The king approaches his temple. It furthers one to see the great man. This brings success. Perseverance furthers. To bring great offerings creates good fortune. It furthers one to undertake something.
imageZh: 泽上于地，萃；君子以除戎器，戒不虞。
