imageEn: Over the earth, the lake: the image of Gathering Together. Thus the superior man renews his weapons in order to meet the unforeseen.
interpretationZh: 萃卦象征聚集，泽水汇聚于地上。人心聚合，宜以诚敬之心祭祀，团结众人共成大事。
interpretationEn: Cui symbolizes gathering and assembly. The lake collects over the earth. Unite people with sincerity and reverence to accomplish great things.

lines.textZh: 初六：有孚不终，乃乱乃萃，若号，一握为笑，勿恤，往无咎。
lines.textEn: Six at the beginning: If you are sincere, but not to the end, there will sometimes be confusion, sometimes gathering. If you call out, then after one grasp of the hand you can laugh again. Regret not. Going is without blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 诚信不能坚持到底，时聚时散。呼唤同伴，握手言欢，前往无咎。
lines.interpretationEn: Sincerity wavers, causing confusion. Call out for help, join hands, and move forward without blame.

lines.textZh: 六二：引吉，无咎，孚乃利用禴。
lines.textEn: Six in the second place: Letting oneself be drawn brings good fortune and remains blameless. If one is sincere, it furthers one to bring even a small offering.
lines.interpretationZh: 被引导而聚合则吉，无咎。心怀诚信，即使简单的祭祀也有利。
lines.interpretationEn: Being drawn into the gathering brings fortune. With sincerity, even a small offering furthers.

lines.textZh: 六三：萃如嗟如，无攸利，往无咎，小吝。
lines.textEn: Six in the third place: Gathering together amid sighs. Nothing that would further. Going is without blame. Slight humiliation.
lines.interpretationZh: 聚合中叹息不已，无所利益。前往虽无咎，但有小憾。
lines.interpretationEn: Gathering with sighs. No advantage here. Going forward is blameless but slightly humiliating.

lines.textZh: 九四：大吉，无咎。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fourth place: Great good fortune. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 大吉大利，没有过失。以公心聚众，自然顺遂。
lines.interpretationEn: Great good fortune and no blame. Gathering others with a public spirit brings success.

lines.textZh: 九五：萃有位，无咎。匪孚，元永贞，悔亡。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fifth place: If in gathering together one has position, this is without blame. If there are some who are not yet sincerely in the work, sublime and enduring perseverance is needed. Then remorse disappears.
lines.interpretationZh: 居位而聚众，无咎。若有人尚未诚服，需以恒久正道感化，悔恨自消。
lines.interpretationEn: Gathering from a position of authority, no blame. Win over doubters through enduring perseverance and remorse vanishes.

lines.textZh: 上六：赍咨涕洟，无咎。
lines.textEn: Six at the top: Lamenting and sighing, floods of tears. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 叹息流泪，悲伤不已，但并无过失。真情流露，终能感动人心。
lines.interpretationEn: Lamenting with floods of tears. No blame. Genuine emotion ultimately moves others.

judgmentZh: 升：元亨，用见大人，勿恤，南征吉。
judgmentEn: Pushing Upward has supreme success. One must see the great man. Fear not. Departure toward the south brings good fortune.
imageZh: 地中生木，升；君子以顺德，积小以高大。
imageEn: Within the earth, wood grows: the image of Pushing Upward. Thus the superior man of devoted character heaps up small things in order to achieve something high and great.
interpretationZh: 升卦象征上升，木在地中生长向上。顺应自然之德，积少成多，由小而大，循序渐进。
interpretationEn: Sheng symbolizes upward growth. Wood grows within the earth, rising steadily. Accumulate small gains to achieve greatness through gradual progress.

lines.textZh: 初六：允升，大吉。
lines.textEn: Six at the beginning: Pushing upward that meets with confidence brings great good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 诚信上升，大吉大利。初始阶段得到信任，顺利上进。
lines.interpretationEn: Ascending with trust and confidence. Great good fortune at the outset.

lines.textZh: 九二：孚乃利用禴，无咎。
lines.textEn: Nine in the second place: If one is sincere, it furthers one to bring even a small offering. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 心怀诚信，即使简单的祭祀也有利，无咎。
lines.interpretationEn: With sincerity, even a small offering furthers. No blame.

lines.textZh: 九三：升虚邑。
lines.textEn: Nine in the third place: One pushes upward into an empty city.
lines.interpretationZh: 上升进入空虚之城，畅通无阻，毫无障碍。
lines.interpretationEn: Pushing upward into an empty city. No obstacles on the path.

lines.textZh: 六四：王用亨于岐山，吉，无咎。
lines.textEn: Six in the fourth place: The king offers him Mount Ch'i. Good fortune. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 君王在岐山祭祀，吉利无咎。顺应天命，恭敬奉献。
lines.interpretationEn: The king makes offerings at Mount Ch'i. Good fortune and no blame through devoted service.

lines.textZh: 六五：贞吉，升阶。
lines.textEn: Six in the fifth place: Perseverance brings good fortune. One pushes upward by steps.
lines.interpretationZh: 坚守正道则吉，一步一步稳步上升。
lines.interpretationEn: Perseverance brings fortune. Ascend step by step in steady progress.

lines.textZh: 上六：冥升，利于不息之贞。
lines.textEn: Six at the top: Pushing upward in darkness. It furthers one to be unremittingly persevering.
lines.interpretationZh: 在昏暗中上升，利于不懈地坚守正道。盲目上进需以恒心自持。
lines.interpretationEn: Ascending in darkness. Unceasing perseverance is needed. Blind ambition requires steadfast discipline.

judgmentZh: 困：亨，贞，大人吉，无咎，有言不信。
judgmentEn: Oppression. Success. Perseverance. The great man brings about good fortune. No blame. When one has something to say, it is not believed.
imageZh: 泽无水，困；君子以致命遂志。
imageEn: There is no water in the lake: the image of Exhaustion. Thus the superior man stakes his life on following his will.
interpretationZh: 困卦象征困穷，泽中无水，枯竭之象。君子身处困境，宁可舍命也要实现志向，言语不被信任时以行动证明。
interpretationEn: Kun symbolizes oppression and exhaustion. The lake is without water. The superior man stakes his life on his convictions, proving himself through action when words are not believed.

lines.textZh: 初六：臀困于株木，入于幽谷，三岁不觌。
lines.textEn: Six at the beginning: One sits oppressed under a bare tree and strays into a gloomy valley. For three years one sees nothing.
lines.interpretationZh: 困坐于枯木之下，迷入幽暗山谷，三年不见光明。处境极为困顿。
lines.interpretationEn: Sitting oppressed under a bare tree, lost in a dark valley. For three years, no light. A time of deep hardship.

lines.textZh: 九二：困于酒食，朱绂方来，利用享祀，征凶，无咎。
lines.textEn: Nine in the second place: One is oppressed while at meat and drink. The man with the scarlet knee bands is just coming. It furthers one to offer sacrifice. To set forth brings misfortune. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 在酒食中受困，贵人即将到来。宜祭祀祈福，出征则凶，但无过失。
lines.interpretationEn: Oppressed amid plenty. A dignitary approaches. Offer sacrifice; do not march forth. No blame.

lines.textZh: 六三：困于石，据于蒺藜，入于其宫，不见其妻，凶。
lines.textEn: Six in the third place: One lets himself be oppressed by stone, and leans on thorns and thistles. He enters his house and does not see his wife. Misfortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 被巨石所困，倚靠荆棘，回到家中却不见妻子，凶险。进退失据，内外皆困。
lines.interpretationEn: Oppressed by stone, leaning on thorns. Entering home but not finding one's wife. Misfortune from being trapped inside and out.

lines.textZh: 九四：来徐徐，困于金车，吝，有终。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fourth place: He comes very slowly, oppressed in a golden carriage. Humiliation, but the end is reached.
lines.interpretationZh: 行动迟缓，困于华贵的车中。虽有困窘，但终能达成目标。
lines.interpretationEn: Coming slowly, oppressed in a golden carriage. Humiliation, but the goal is eventually reached.

lines.textZh: 九五：劓刖，困于赤绂，乃徐有说，利用祭祀。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fifth place: His nose and feet are cut off. Oppression at the hands of the man with the purple knee bands. Joy comes softly. It furthers one to make offerings and libations.
lines.interpretationZh: 遭受刑罚之困，被权贵所压迫。慢慢地喜悦到来，宜以祭祀求解。
lines.interpretationEn: Nose and feet cut off, oppressed by authority. Joy comes gradually. Offerings and devotion bring relief.

lines.textZh: 上六：困于葛藟，于臲卼，曰动悔有悔，征吉。
lines.textEn: Six at the top: He is oppressed by creeping vines. He moves uncertainly and says, 'Movement brings remorse.' If one feels remorse over this and makes a start, good fortune comes.
lines.interpretationZh: 被葛藤缠绕所困，摇摆不定。若能反省悔悟并付诸行动，则前往吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: Oppressed by entangling vines, wavering. If one repents and takes action, setting forth brings good fortune.

judgmentZh: 井：改邑不改井，无丧无得，往来井井。汔至，亦未繘井，羸其瓶，凶。
judgmentEn: The Well. The town may be changed, but the well cannot be changed. It neither decreases nor increases. They come and go and draw from the well. If one gets down almost to the water and the rope does not go all the way, or the jug breaks, it brings misfortune.
imageZh: 木上有水，井；君子以劳民劝相。
imageEn: Water over wood: the image of the Well. Thus the superior man encourages the people at their work, and exhorts them to help one another.
interpretationZh: 井卦象征水井，木上有水，汲水而上。井水不变，养育万民。君子效法井德，勤劳利民，互助互勉。
interpretationEn: Jing symbolizes the well. Water over wood draws nourishment upward. The well is constant, sustaining all. The superior man encourages mutual aid and diligent service.

lines.textZh: 初六：井泥不食，旧井无禽。
lines.textEn: Six at the beginning: One does not drink the mud of the well. No animals come to an old well.
lines.interpretationZh: 井底淤泥不可饮用，废弃的旧井连禽兽都不来。才德荒废则无人亲近。
lines.interpretationEn: The mud of the well is not drunk. No animals come to an old well. Neglected talent attracts no one.

lines.textZh: 九二：井谷射鲋，瓮敝漏。
lines.textEn: Nine in the second place: At the wellhole one shoots fishes. The jug is broken and leaks.
lines.interpretationZh: 井中只能射小鱼，水瓮破漏。才能未得善用，有才而无用武之地。
lines.interpretationEn: Shooting fish at the wellhole. The jug is broken and leaks. Talent is wasted without proper application.

lines.textZh: 九三：井渫不食，为我心恻，可用汲，王明，并受其福。
lines.textEn: Nine in the third place: The well is cleaned, but no one drinks from it. This is my heart's sorrow, for one might draw from it. If the king were clear-minded, good fortune might be enjoyed in common.
lines.interpretationZh: 井水已清却无人饮用，令人心痛。若遇明君赏识，则众人共享福泽。
lines.interpretationEn: The well is clean but unused. A sorrowful waste. If the king were wise, all could share its blessings.

lines.textZh: 六四：井甃，无咎。
lines.textEn: Six in the fourth place: The well is being lined. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 用砖石修砌井壁，无过失。修缮自身，打好基础。
lines.interpretationEn: The well is being lined with stone. No blame. Repair and strengthen the foundation.

lines.textZh: 九五：井冽，寒泉食。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fifth place: In the well there is a clear, cold spring from which one can drink.
lines.interpretationZh: 井水清冽，寒泉可饮。才德兼备，泽被众人。
lines.interpretationEn: A clear, cold spring in the well from which all may drink. Virtue and talent benefit everyone.

lines.textZh: 上六：井收勿幕，有孚元吉。
lines.textEn: Six at the top: One draws from the well without hindrance. It is dependable. Supreme good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 井水汲取无遮盖，人人可用。诚信可靠，大吉大利。
lines.interpretationEn: The well is open and dependable for all. With sincerity and reliability comes supreme good fortune.

judgmentZh: 革：己日乃孚，元亨利贞，悔亡。
judgmentEn: Revolution. On your own day you are believed. Supreme success, furthering through perseverance. Remorse disappears.
imageZh: 泽中有火，革；君子以治历明时。
imageEn: Fire in the lake: the image of Revolution. Thus the superior man sets the calendar in order and makes the seasons clear.
interpretationZh: 革卦象征变革，泽中有火，水火相激。变革需待时机成熟，方能取信于人，悔恨消亡。
interpretationEn: Ge symbolizes revolution. Fire within the lake — water and fire clash. Reform must come at the right time to win trust and eliminate regret.

lines.textZh: 初九：巩用黄牛之革。
lines.textEn: Nine at the beginning: Wrapped in the hide of a yellow cow.
lines.interpretationZh: 用黄牛皮革牢牢束缚，时机未到不可轻举妄动。
lines.interpretationEn: Bound with the hide of a yellow cow. The time for action has not yet come.
lines.detailZh: 初九“巩用黄牛之革”——用黄牛皮牢牢捆绑。初爻是变革的最初阶段，此时不宜轻举妄动。“黄牛”象征中庸稳健（黄为中色），“巩”意为巩固。变革之初，最重要的是稳固现有基础、做好充分准备，而不是急于打破现状。就像建筑改造前必须先加固地基一样。
lines.detailEn: The first nine—'Wrapped in the hide of a yellow cow'—advises firm restraint at revolution's earliest stage. Yellow symbolizes moderation; 'wrapped firmly' means consolidating foundations before acting. Like reinforcing a building's foundation before renovation, prepare thoroughly before breaking the status quo.

lines.textZh: 六二：己日乃革之，征吉，无咎。
lines.textEn: Six in the second place: When one's own day comes, one may create revolution. Starting brings good fortune. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 到了适当的时候再进行变革，出征则吉利，无过失。
lines.interpretationEn: When the proper day arrives, undertake revolution. Going forward brings fortune and no blame.
lines.detailZh: 六二“巳日乃革之，征吉，无咎”——时机成熟之日才进行变革，出征吉祥，没有过失。六二居中得位，是发动变革的最佳时机。“巳日”强调时机的重要性——不是不变，而是要在对的时间变。这一爻告诉我们：变革的成功不仅取决于方向是否正确，更取决于时机是否恰当。太早则准备不足，太晚则积重难返。
lines.detailEn: The second six—'When one's own day comes, one may create revolution. Starting brings good fortune. No blame'—identifies the optimal moment for change. Success depends not only on correct direction but on proper timing. Too early means insufficient preparation; too late means problems become intractable.

lines.textZh: 九三：征凶，贞厉，革言三就，有孚。
lines.textEn: Nine in the third place: Starting brings misfortune. Perseverance brings danger. When talk of revolution has gone the rounds three times, one may commit himself, and men will believe him.
lines.interpretationZh: 急于行动则凶，坚持也有危险。变革之议经过三次讨论确认后，方可取信于人。
lines.interpretationEn: Hasty action brings misfortune. After revolution is discussed three times, commitment wins trust.
lines.detailZh: 九三“征凶，贞厉。革言三就，有孚”——贸然出征是凶险的，坚守正道也有危险。“革言三就”意为变革的方案经过三次讨论和修改才最终确定。这一爻强调变革决策的审慎性——重大变革不能一拍脑袋就决定，必须反复论证、广泛征求意见、多次修改方案。经过“三就”的充分讨论，才能获得“有孚”（众人的信任）。
lines.detailEn: The third nine warns that hasty action is dangerous. 'When talk of revolution has gone the round three times, one may commit oneself, and men will believe'—major changes cannot be decided impulsively. Plans must be debated, revised, and refined through multiple rounds of discussion before earning collective trust.

lines.textZh: 九四：悔亡，有孚改命，吉。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fourth place: Remorse disappears. Men believe in him. Changing the form of government brings good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 悔恨消失，以诚信改变旧制，吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: Remorse vanishes. With sincerity, changing the mandate brings good fortune.
lines.detailZh: 九四“悔亡，有孚改命，吉”——后悔消失，以诚信改变天命，吉祥。九四已进入上卦，变革进入实质性阶段。“有孚改命”是革卦最核心的理念——变革必须建立在诚信的基础上，以正义之名改变旧的秩序。“改命”不是随意妄为，而是顺应天命的正当行为。当变革者的诚意被认可，一切后悔和阻力都会消失。
lines.detailEn: The fourth nine—'Remorse disappears. Men believe in the revolution. Changing the form of government brings good fortune'—marks revolution's substantive phase. 'Changing the mandate with sincerity' is Ge's core principle: revolution must be built on integrity, changing old order in the name of justice. When the reformer's sincerity is recognized, all resistance dissolves.

lines.textZh: 九五：大人虎变，未占有孚。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fifth place: The great man changes like a tiger. Even before he questions the oracle he is believed.
lines.interpretationZh: 大人物如猛虎般果断变革，不必占卜即可取信于人。
lines.interpretationEn: The great man transforms like a tiger. He is trusted even before consulting the oracle.
lines.detailZh: 九五“大人虎变，未占有孚”——伟大的人物像老虎换毛一样进行变革，不需要占卜就能获得信任。“虎变”形容变革的威猛和彻底——老虎换毛后花纹更加鲜明美丽，象征变革后的新秩序更加清晰有力。九五居尊位，代表变革的领导者。真正伟大的变革者，其正义性和必要性不言自明（未占有孚），天下人自然信服。
lines.detailEn: The fifth nine—'The great man changes like a tiger. Even before he questions the oracle, he is believed'—describes revolution's leader. 'Tiger change' symbolizes bold, thorough transformation—like a tiger's stripes becoming more vivid after molting, the new order emerges clearer and more powerful. A truly great reformer's righteousness is self-evident; people naturally believe without needing proof.

lines.textZh: 上六：君子豹变，小人革面，征凶，居贞吉。
lines.textEn: Six at the top: The superior man changes like a panther. The inferior man molts in the face. Starting brings misfortune. To remain persevering brings good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 君子如豹般优雅变革，小人只是表面顺从。此时不宜再动，安守正道则吉。
lines.interpretationEn: The superior man transforms like a panther. The inferior man changes only his face. Remain still and persevere for fortune.
lines.detailZh: 上六“君子豹变，小人革面，征凶，居贞吉”——君子像豹子换毛一样优雅地完成转变，小人则只是表面改变（革面）。变革完成后，不宜继续激进（征凶），应该安定下来巩固成果（居贞吉）。“豹变”比“虎变”更为细腻优雅，象征变革后期的精细调整。这一爻告诉我们：变革不能永远处于激烈状态，适时收手、巩固成果才是智慧。
lines.detailEn: The top six—'The superior man changes like a panther. The inferior man molts in the face. Starting brings misfortune. To remain persevering brings good fortune'—describes revolution's completion. The panther's change is more refined than the tiger's, symbolizing fine-tuning after major reform. Revolution cannot remain in a perpetual state of upheaval; knowing when to stop and consolidate is wisdom.

overviewZh: 革卦是《周易》第四十九卦，由上兑（泽）下离（火）组成，泽中有火，火热蒸发泽水，象征变革与更新。“革”的本义是兽皮，引申为去旧换新、变革改制。水火本不相容，在革卦中却相互作用——火蒸发水、水灭火，旧的秩序在冲突中被打破，新的秩序在变革中诞生。革卦是《周易》中最具革命性的卦象，它告诉我们：当旧制度已经不适应时代，变革不仅是必要的，而且是天命所归。
overviewEn: Ge (Revolution/Molting) is the 49th hexagram, composed of Dui (lake) above and Li (fire) below—fire within the lake, heat evaporating water, symbolizing transformation and renewal. 'Ge' originally means animal hide, extended to mean shedding the old for the new. Water and fire are naturally incompatible, yet in Ge they interact—fire evaporates water, water extinguishes fire—old order breaks down through conflict while new order emerges through revolution. Ge is the I Ching's most revolutionary hexagram, teaching that when old systems no longer serve the times, change is not only necessary but mandated by heaven.
judgmentDetailZh: 革卦卦辞“革：巳日乃孚，元亨利贞，悔亡”中“巳日乃孚”是关键——变革不能操之过急，要等到“巳日”（时机成熟之日）才能获得人们的信任（孚）。《彖传》进一步阐释：“天地革而四时成，汤武革命，顺乎天而应乎人”——天地的变革造就了四季更替，商汤和周武王的革命顺应天意、回应民心。这是《周易》对“革命”最经典的论述：真正的变革必须同时满足两个条件——顺乎天（符合历史规律）和应乎人（得到民众支持）。缺少任何一个，变革都不会成功。
judgmentDetailEn: The judgment 'Revolution. On your own day you are believed. Supreme success, furthering through perseverance. Remorse disappears' emphasizes that revolution cannot be rushed—trust comes only when the time is ripe. The Commentary elaborates: 'Heaven and earth bring about revolution, and the four seasons complete themselves. Tang and Wu brought about political revolution in conformity with heaven and in accord with men.' True revolution must satisfy two conditions simultaneously: conforming to heaven (historical necessity) and according with people (popular support). Without either, revolution fails.
imageDetailZh: 象辞“泽中有火，革。君子以治历明时”将变革与历法联系起来。“治历明时”意为修订历法以明确时令——这是古代最重要的“变革”之一，因为历法关系到农业生产和社会秩序。每当旧历法不再准确，就必须进行改革。这个比喻告诉我们：变革的目的不是为了破坏，而是为了让事物重新与时代同步。就像调整时钟一样，当旧的标准不再准确时，必须勇于更新。
imageDetailEn: The Image 'Fire in the lake: Revolution. The superior man sets the calendar in order and makes the seasons clear' connects revolution to calendar reform—one of antiquity's most important changes, as the calendar governed agriculture and social order. When the old calendar became inaccurate, reform was essential. This metaphor teaches that revolution's purpose is not destruction but resynchronization—like adjusting a clock when old standards no longer serve.
modernApplicationZh: 【事业】革卦是转型变革的最佳时机。如果公司的旧模式已经不适应市场，革卦鼓励大胆改革——但要注意“巳日乃孚”，变革需要充分准备和团队共识，不能独断专行。成功的变革需要“顺天应人”——既符合行业趋势，又得到团队支持。【感情】革卦在感情中代表关系的重大转变——可能是从朋友变为恋人，也可能是关系模式的根本改变。如果旧的相处方式已经产生问题，革卦鼓励坦诚沟通、共同改变。【财运】革卦象征投资策略的转型。如果旧的投资组合表现不佳，是时候大胆调整了。但变革要有依据，不能盲目跟风。【健康】革卦提示改变不良的生活习惯。如果长期的作息、饮食模式已经影响健康，现在是下定决心改变的好时机。“革”就是去旧换新，从根本上改善生活方式。
modernApplicationEn: 【Career】Ge signals the optimal time for transformation. If old business models no longer fit the market, Ge encourages bold reform—but remember 'trust comes when the time is ripe.' Successful change requires both industry alignment and team consensus.【Love】Ge represents major relationship transitions—perhaps from friends to lovers, or fundamental changes in how you relate. If old patterns cause problems, Ge encourages honest communication and mutual change.【Wealth】Ge symbolizes portfolio transformation. If old investments underperform, it's time for bold adjustment—but changes must be evidence-based, not trend-following.【Health】Ge prompts changing unhealthy habits. If long-standing lifestyle patterns harm your health, now is the time for decisive change. 'Revolution' means fundamentally improving your way of life.
historicalStoryZh: 革卦最经典的历史典故就是“汤武革命”。商汤推翻夏桀、周武王推翻商纣，这两次改朝换代被《周易》称为“革命”的典范——“顺乎天而应乎人”。商汤在伐夏之前发表了著名的《汤誓》，历数夏桀的罪行，争取民心；周武王在牧野之战前发表《牧誓》，同样以正义之名号召天下。两次革命都不是一时冲动，而是经过长期准备、等待时机成熟后的果断行动。近代的“戊戌变法”则是反面教材——变革操之过急、未获广泛支持，最终以失败告终，正应了“巳日乃孚”的警示。
historicalStoryEn: The classic historical examples are the revolutions of Tang and Wu. King Tang overthrew the tyrant Jie of Xia, and King Wu overthrew the tyrant Zhou of Shang—both cited by the I Ching as model revolutions 'in conformity with heaven and in accord with men.' Both leaders prepared extensively and waited for the right moment before acting decisively. The failed Hundred Days' Reform of 1898 serves as a counterexample—rushed without broad support, it collapsed, perfectly illustrating the warning that trust comes only when timing is right.
relatedHexagramsNote: 革卦的综卦为鼎卦（第50卦），革为破旧、鼎为立新，先革后鼎，破旧立新是完整的变革过程；错卦为蒙卦（第4卦）。革与鼎的关系揭示了变革的完整逻辑：不能只破不立，革除旧制之后必须建立新制。

judgmentZh: 鼎：元吉，亨。
judgmentEn: The Caldron. Supreme good fortune. Success.
imageZh: 木上有火，鼎；君子以正位凝命。
imageEn: Fire over wood: the image of the Caldron. Thus the superior man consolidates his fate by making his position correct.
interpretationZh: 鼎卦象征鼎器，木上有火，烹饪养贤。鼎为国之重器，象征革故鼎新，端正位置以凝聚天命。
interpretationEn: Ding symbolizes the caldron. Fire over wood cooks nourishment. The caldron is a vessel of state, representing renewal and consolidating one's destiny.

lines.textZh: 初六：鼎颠趾，利出否，得妾以其子，无咎。
lines.textEn: Six at the beginning: A caldron with legs upturned. Furthers removal of stagnating stuff. One takes a concubine for the sake of her son. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 鼎翻倒倾出脚，有利于倒出陈旧之物。纳妾得子，无过失。
lines.interpretationEn: The caldron is overturned to pour out the old. Taking a concubine for her son brings no blame.

lines.textZh: 九二：鼎有实，我仇有疾，不我能即，吉。
lines.textEn: Nine in the second place: There is food in the caldron. My comrades are envious, but they cannot harm me. Good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 鼎中有食物，同伴虽然嫉妒但无法伤害我，吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: The caldron is full. Envious companions cannot reach me. Good fortune.

lines.textZh: 九三：鼎耳革，其行塞，雉膏不食，方雨亏悔，终吉。
lines.textEn: Nine in the third place: The handle of the caldron is altered. One is impeded in his way of life. The fat of the pheasant is not eaten. Once rain falls, remorse is spent. Good fortune comes in the end.
lines.interpretationZh: 鼎耳被改变，行动受阻，美味不得享用。待到下雨时悔恨消散，终获吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: The caldron's handle is changed; progress is blocked. When rain comes, remorse fades and fortune arrives.

lines.textZh: 九四：鼎折足，覆公餗，其形渥，凶。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fourth place: The legs of the caldron are broken. The prince's meal is spilled and his person is soiled. Misfortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 鼎足折断，打翻了王公的食物，弄得一身狼狈，凶险。
lines.interpretationEn: The caldron's legs break. The prince's meal is spilled and he is soiled. Misfortune.

lines.textZh: 六五：鼎黄耳金铉，利贞。
lines.textEn: Six in the fifth place: The caldron has yellow handles, golden carrying rings. Perseverance furthers.
lines.interpretationZh: 鼎有黄色的耳和金色的铉，坚守正道则有利。
lines.interpretationEn: The caldron has yellow handles and golden rings. Perseverance furthers.

lines.textZh: 上九：鼎玉铉，大吉，无不利。
lines.textEn: Nine at the top: The caldron has rings of jade. Great good fortune. Nothing that would not act to further.
lines.interpretationZh: 鼎有玉铉，大吉大利，无往不利。
lines.interpretationEn: The caldron has jade rings. Great good fortune. Everything furthers.

judgmentZh: 震：亨。震来虩虩，笑言哑哑。震惊百里，不丧匕鬯。
judgmentEn: The Arousing brings success. Shock comes — oh, oh! Laughing words — ha, ha! The shock terrifies for a hundred miles, and he does not let fall the sacrificial spoon and chalice.
imageZh: 洊雷，震；君子以恐惧修省。
imageEn: Thunder repeated: the image of Shock. Thus in fear and trembling the superior man sets his life in order and examines himself.
interpretationZh: 震卦象征雷震，重雷叠至，震动惊惧。震后能笑，说明内心镇定。君子因恐惧而反省修身。
interpretationEn: Zhen symbolizes thunder and shock. Repeated thunder brings awe. The superior man uses fear as a catalyst for self-examination and inner cultivation.

lines.textZh: 初九：震来虩虩，后笑言哑哑，吉。
lines.textEn: Nine at the beginning: Shock comes — oh, oh! Then follow laughing words — ha, ha! Good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 雷震到来令人恐惧，之后谈笑自若，吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: Shock arrives with terror, then laughter follows. Good fortune.

lines.textZh: 六二：震来厉，亿丧贝，跻于九陵，勿逐，七日得。
lines.textEn: Six in the second place: Shock comes bringing danger. A hundred thousand times you lose your treasures and must climb the nine hills. Do not go in pursuit of them. After seven days you will get them back again.
lines.interpretationZh: 雷震带来危险，丧失财宝，登上高山避难。不必追寻，七日后自然失而复得。
lines.interpretationEn: Shock brings danger and loss of treasures. Climb to safety. Do not pursue; in seven days they return.

lines.textZh: 六三：震苏苏，震行无眚。
lines.textEn: Six in the third place: Shock comes and makes one distraught. If shock spurs to action, one remains free of misfortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 雷震令人心神不安，若因震动而奋起行动，则无灾祸。
lines.interpretationEn: Shock causes distress. If it spurs one to action, there will be no misfortune.

lines.textZh: 九四：震遂泥。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fourth place: Shock is mired.
lines.interpretationZh: 雷震陷入泥沼，行动受阻，难以振作。
lines.interpretationEn: Shock becomes mired. Movement is impeded and one cannot rally.

lines.textZh: 六五：震往来厉，亿无丧，有事。
lines.textEn: Six in the fifth place: Shock goes hither and thither. Danger. However, nothing at all is lost. Yet there are things to be done.
lines.interpretationZh: 雷震往来反复，虽有危险但不会有损失，仍有事务需要处理。
lines.interpretationEn: Shock comes and goes with danger. Nothing is lost, yet there is work to be done.

lines.textZh: 上六：震索索，视矍矍，征凶。震不于其躬，于其邻，无咎。婚媾有言。
lines.textEn: Six at the top: Shock brings ruin and terrified gazing around. Going ahead brings misfortune. If it has not yet reached one's own body but has reached one's neighbor first, there is no blame. One's comrades have something to talk about.
lines.interpretationZh: 雷震令人惊恐不安，四处张望。前行则凶。若雷震尚未及身而先及邻人，则无咎。婚姻之事有口舌。
lines.interpretationEn: Shock brings terror and anxious gazing. Going forward is misfortune. If shock strikes the neighbor first, no blame. There will be talk about marriages.

judgmentZh: 艮其背，不获其身，行其庭，不见其人，无咎。
judgmentEn: Keeping Still. Keeping his back so still that he no longer feels his body. He goes into his courtyard and does not see his people. No blame.
imageZh: 兼山，艮；君子以思不出其位。
imageEn: Mountains standing close together: the image of Keeping Still. Thus the superior man does not permit his thoughts to go beyond his situation.
interpretationZh: 艮卦象征止息，两山重叠，静止不动。止于背则不见身，行于庭则不见人，各安其位则无咎。
interpretationEn: Gen symbolizes stillness. Two mountains stand together, motionless. When one keeps still and stays within proper bounds, there is no blame.

lines.textZh: 初六：艮其趾，无咎，利永贞。
lines.textEn: Six at the beginning: Keeping his toes still. No blame. Continued perseverance furthers.
lines.interpretationZh: 止于脚趾，在行动之初就停下，无过失，宜长久坚守正道。
lines.interpretationEn: Keeping the toes still. Stopping at the very beginning brings no blame. Lasting perseverance furthers.

lines.textZh: 六二：艮其腓，不拯其随，其心不快。
lines.textEn: Six in the second place: Keeping his calves still. He cannot rescue him whom he follows. His heart is not glad.
lines.interpretationZh: 止于小腿，无法拯救所追随的人，心中不快。
lines.interpretationEn: Keeping the calves still. He cannot save the one he follows. His heart is not glad.

lines.textZh: 九三：艮其限，列其夤，厉薰心。
lines.textEn: Nine in the third place: Keeping his hips still. Making his sacrum stiff. Dangerous. The heart suffocates.
lines.interpretationZh: 止于腰部，使脊骨僵硬，危险。心中如被烟熏般窒息。
lines.interpretationEn: Keeping the hips still. The sacrum is rigid. Danger — the heart suffocates.

lines.textZh: 六四：艮其身，无咎。
lines.textEn: Six in the fourth place: Keeping his trunk still. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 止于躯干，全身静止，无过失。
lines.interpretationEn: Keeping the trunk still. No blame.

lines.textZh: 六五：艮其辅，言有序，悔亡。
lines.textEn: Six in the fifth place: Keeping his jaws still. The words have order. Remorse disappears.
lines.interpretationZh: 止于面颊，说话有条理有分寸，悔恨消失。
lines.interpretationEn: Keeping the jaws still. Words are well ordered. Remorse disappears.

lines.textZh: 上九：敦艮，吉。
lines.textEn: Nine at the top: Noblehearted keeping still. Good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 敦厚地止息，达到止的最高境界，吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: Noblehearted stillness. The highest form of keeping still. Good fortune.

judgmentZh: 渐：女归吉，利贞。
judgmentEn: Development. The maiden is given in marriage. Good fortune. Perseverance furthers.
imageZh: 山上有木，渐；君子以居贤德善俗。
imageEn: On the mountain, a tree: the image of Development. Thus the superior man abides in dignity and virtue, in order to improve the mores.
interpretationZh: 渐卦象征循序渐进，如山上之木缓慢生长。女子出嫁循礼而行则吉，凡事宜按部就班，不可急躁冒进。
interpretationEn: Jian symbolizes gradual progress, like a tree growing slowly on a mountain. Steady advancement following proper order leads to success.

lines.textZh: 初六：鸿渐于干，小子厉，有言，无咎。
lines.textEn: Six at the beginning: The wild goose gradually draws near the shore. The young son is in danger. There is talk. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 鸿雁渐进到水边，年轻人处境危险，虽有议论但无过失。
lines.interpretationEn: The wild goose approaches the shore. The young face danger and gossip, but no blame.

lines.textZh: 六二：鸿渐于磐，饮食衎衎，吉。
lines.textEn: Six in the second place: The wild goose gradually draws near the cliff. Eating and drinking in peace and concord. Good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 鸿雁渐进到磐石上，安然饮食，和乐融融，吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: The wild goose reaches the cliff. Eating and drinking in peace. Good fortune.

lines.textZh: 九三：鸿渐于陆，夫征不复，妇孕不育，凶。利御寇。
lines.textEn: Nine in the third place: The wild goose gradually draws near the plateau. The man goes forth and does not return. The woman carries a child but does not bring it forth. Misfortune. It furthers one to fight off robbers.
lines.interpretationZh: 鸿雁渐进到高原，丈夫出征不归，妻子怀孕不育，凶险。宜防御外敌。
lines.interpretationEn: The wild goose reaches the plateau. The husband departs and does not return. Misfortune. Defend against threats.

lines.textZh: 六四：鸿渐于木，或得其桷，无咎。
lines.textEn: Six in the fourth place: The wild goose gradually draws near the tree. Perhaps it will find a flat branch. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 鸿雁渐进到树上，或许能找到平坦的枝桠栖息，无过失。
lines.interpretationEn: The wild goose draws near the tree. Perhaps it finds a flat branch to rest on. No blame.

lines.textZh: 九五：鸿渐于陵，妇三岁不孕，终莫之胜，吉。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fifth place: The wild goose gradually draws near the summit. For three years the woman has no child. In the end nothing can hinder her. Good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 鸿雁渐进到山陵，妇人三年不孕，但终究无人能阻挡，吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: The wild goose reaches the summit. Three years without child, yet in the end nothing can prevent it. Good fortune.

lines.textZh: 上九：鸿渐于陆，其羽可用为仪，吉。
lines.textEn: Nine at the top: The wild goose gradually draws near the cloud heights. Its feathers can be used as ornaments. Good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 鸿雁渐进到云端高处，其羽毛可用作礼仪装饰，吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: The wild goose soars to the cloud heights. Its feathers serve as ornaments. Good fortune.

judgmentZh: 归妹：征凶，无攸利。
judgmentEn: The Marrying Maiden. Undertakings bring misfortune. Nothing that would further.
imageZh: 泽上有雷，归妹；君子以永终知敝。
imageEn: Thunder over the lake: the image of the Marrying Maiden. Thus the superior man understands the transitory in the light of the eternity of the end.
interpretationZh: 归妹卦象征少女出嫁，泽上有雷，以悦而动。但行动冒进则凶，需明白事物终始之理，知晓盛衰之道。
interpretationEn: Gui Mei symbolizes the marrying maiden. Thunder over the lake stirs joy into action. But rash undertakings bring misfortune; one must understand the eternal cycle of endings.

lines.textZh: 初九：归妹以娣，跛能履，征吉。
lines.textEn: Nine at the beginning: The marrying maiden as a concubine. A lame man who is able to tread. Undertakings bring good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 少女作为侧室出嫁，如跛者仍能行走，前行则吉。
lines.interpretationEn: The maiden marries as a concubine. Like a lame man who can still walk. Undertakings bring fortune.

lines.textZh: 九二：眇能视，利幽人之贞。
lines.textEn: Nine in the second place: A one-eyed man who is able to see. The perseverance of a solitary man furthers.
lines.interpretationZh: 独眼仍能视物，宜如隐士般坚守正道。
lines.interpretationEn: A one-eyed man who can still see. The perseverance of a solitary person furthers.

lines.textZh: 六三：归妹以须，反归以娣。
lines.textEn: Six in the third place: The marrying maiden as a slave. She marries as a concubine.
lines.interpretationZh: 少女以婢女身份出嫁，后又以侧室身份归来，地位卑微。
lines.interpretationEn: The maiden marries as a slave, then returns as a concubine. A lowly position.

lines.textZh: 九四：归妹愆期，迟归有时。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fourth place: The marrying maiden draws out the allotted time. A late marriage comes in due course.
lines.interpretationZh: 少女错过婚期，推迟出嫁，但终有适当的时机。
lines.interpretationEn: The maiden postpones the marriage. A late union comes in its own time.

lines.textZh: 六五：帝乙归妹，其君之袂，不如其娣之袂良，月几望，吉。
lines.textEn: Six in the fifth place: The sovereign I gave his daughter in marriage. The embroidered garments of the princess were not as gorgeous as those of the serving maid. The moon that is nearly full brings good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 帝乙嫁女，公主的衣饰不如侍女华丽，谦逊如近满之月，吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: The sovereign gives his daughter in marriage. The princess is less adorned than her maid. The nearly full moon brings fortune.

lines.textZh: 上六：女承筐无实，士刲羊无血，无攸利。
lines.textEn: Six at the top: The woman holds the basket, but there are no fruits in it. The man stabs the sheep, but no blood flows. Nothing that acts to further.
lines.interpretationZh: 女子捧着空筐，男子杀羊不见血，一切徒有其表，无所利益。
lines.interpretationEn: The woman holds an empty basket. The man stabs the sheep but no blood flows. Nothing furthers.

judgmentZh: 丰：亨，王假之，勿忧，宜日中。
judgmentEn: Abundance has success. The king attains abundance. Be not sad. Be like the sun at midday.
imageZh: 雷电皆至，丰；君子以折狱致刑。
imageEn: Both thunder and lightning come: the image of Abundance. Thus the superior man decides lawsuits and carries out punishments.
interpretationZh: 丰卦象征丰盛，雷电交加，光明与行动并至。王者达到丰盛之时，不必忧虑，宜如日中天般光明正大。
interpretationEn: Feng symbolizes abundance. Thunder and lightning arrive together — clarity and action unite. Like the sun at its zenith, act with full brilliance.

lines.textZh: 初九：遇其配主，虽旬无咎，往有尚。
lines.textEn: Nine at the beginning: When a man meets his destined ruler, they can be together ten days, and it is not a mistake. Going meets with recognition.
lines.interpretationZh: 遇到相配的主人，即使相处十日也无过失，前往则受到尊重。
lines.interpretationEn: Meeting one's destined partner. Even ten days together bring no blame. Going forward wins recognition.

lines.textZh: 六二：丰其蔀，日中见斗，往得疑疾，有孚发若，吉。
lines.textEn: Six in the second place: The curtain is of such fullness that the polestars can be seen at noon. Through going one meets with mistrust and hate. If one rouses him through truth, good fortune comes.
lines.interpretationZh: 帷幕遮蔽如此之厚，正午可见北斗。前往会遭猜疑，但以诚信感化则吉。
lines.interpretationEn: The curtain is so thick that polestars are seen at noon. Going meets suspicion. Sincerity dispels doubt and brings fortune.

lines.textZh: 九三：丰其沛，日中见沫，折其右肱，无咎。
lines.textEn: Nine in the third place: The underbrush is of such abundance that the small stars can be seen at noon. He breaks his right arm. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 草木繁茂遮天蔽日，正午可见小星。折断右臂，虽有损伤但无过失。
lines.interpretationEn: Such abundance of undergrowth that small stars appear at noon. The right arm is broken. No blame.

lines.textZh: 九四：丰其蔀，日中见斗，遇其夷主，吉。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fourth place: The curtain is of such fullness that the polestars can be seen at noon. He meets his ruler, who is of like kind. Good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 帷幕遮蔽，正午见北斗，遇到志同道合的主人，吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: The curtain is thick; polestars visible at noon. Meeting a like-minded ruler brings good fortune.

lines.textZh: 六五：来章，有庆誉，吉。
lines.textEn: Six in the fifth place: Lines are coming. Blessing and fame draw near. Good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 光明到来，有喜庆和美誉，吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: Brilliance approaches. Blessings and fame draw near. Good fortune.

lines.textZh: 上六：丰其屋，蔀其家，窥其户，阒其无人，三岁不觌，凶。
lines.textEn: Six at the top: His house is in a state of abundance. He screens off his family. He peers through the gate and no longer perceives anyone. For three years he sees nothing. Misfortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 房屋丰大却遮蔽家人，窥门而不见人影，三年不见人，凶险。
lines.interpretationEn: His house is grand but he screens off his family. Peering through the gate, no one is seen for three years. Misfortune.

judgmentZh: 旅：小亨，旅贞吉。
judgmentEn: The Wanderer. Success through smallness. Perseverance brings good fortune to the wanderer.
imageZh: 山上有火，旅；君子以明慎用刑而不留狱。
imageEn: Fire on the mountain: the image of the Wanderer. Thus the superior man is clear-minded and cautious in imposing penalties, and protracts no lawsuits.
interpretationZh: 旅卦象征旅行在外，山上有火，火势不久留。旅途中小事亨通，坚守正道则吉，宜谨慎行事，不可久留一处。
interpretationEn: Lü symbolizes the wanderer. Fire on the mountain does not linger. Small matters succeed on the journey. Persevere in what is right and remain cautious.

lines.textZh: 初六：旅琐琐，斯其所取灾。
lines.textEn: Six at the beginning: If the wanderer busies himself with trivial things, he draws down misfortune upon himself.
lines.interpretationZh: 旅途中琐碎计较，自招灾祸。
lines.interpretationEn: The wanderer busies himself with trifles and draws misfortune upon himself.

lines.textZh: 六二：旅即次，怀其资，得童仆贞。
lines.textEn: Six in the second place: The wanderer comes to an inn. He has his property with him. He wins the steadfastness of a young servant.
lines.interpretationZh: 旅人到达客栈，携带资财，得到忠诚的童仆。
lines.interpretationEn: The wanderer arrives at an inn with his property. He gains a loyal young servant.

lines.textZh: 九三：旅焚其次，丧其童仆，贞厉。
lines.textEn: Nine in the third place: The wanderer's inn burns down. He loses the steadfastness of his young servant. Danger.
lines.interpretationZh: 旅人的客栈被焚毁，失去忠诚的童仆，处境危险。
lines.interpretationEn: The wanderer's inn burns down. He loses his servant. Perseverance brings danger.

lines.textZh: 九四：旅于处，得其资斧，我心不快。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fourth place: The wanderer rests in a shelter. He obtains his property and an axe. My heart is not glad.
lines.interpretationZh: 旅人暂居一处，虽得到资财和工具，但心中不快。
lines.interpretationEn: The wanderer finds shelter and obtains property and an axe. Yet his heart is not glad.

lines.textZh: 六五：射雉一矢亡，终以誉命。
lines.textEn: Six in the fifth place: He shoots a pheasant. It drops with the first arrow. In the end this brings praise and office.
lines.interpretationZh: 射中野鸡，一箭命中，最终获得荣誉和官职。
lines.interpretationEn: He shoots a pheasant and it falls with one arrow. In the end, praise and office come.

lines.textZh: 上九：鸟焚其巢，旅人先笑后号啕。丧牛于易，凶。
lines.textEn: Nine at the top: The bird's nest burns up. The wanderer laughs at first, then must needs lament and weep. Through carelessness he loses his cow. Misfortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 鸟巢被焚毁，旅人先笑后哭。因疏忽失去牛，凶险。
lines.interpretationEn: The bird's nest burns. The wanderer laughs first, then weeps. He loses his cow through carelessness. Misfortune.

judgmentZh: 巽：小亨，利有攸往，利见大人。
judgmentEn: The Gentle. Success through what is small. It furthers one to have somewhere to go. It furthers one to see the great man.
imageZh: 随风，巽；君子以申命行事。
imageEn: Winds following one upon the other: the image of the Gently Penetrating. Thus the superior man spreads his commands abroad and carries out his undertakings.
interpretationZh: 巽卦象征风，柔顺而渗透。两风相随，无孔不入，以柔克刚，循序渐进方能成事。
interpretationEn: Xun symbolizes wind and gentle penetration. Two winds following each other penetrate everywhere. Gentle persistence achieves what force cannot.

lines.textZh: 初六：进退，利武人之贞。
lines.textEn: Six at the beginning: In advancing and in retreating, the perseverance of a warrior furthers.
lines.interpretationZh: 进退犹豫不决，宜以武人般的坚毅果断来行事。
lines.interpretationEn: Hesitating between advance and retreat. The resolve of a warrior is needed.
lines.detailZh: 初六“进退，利武人之贞”描述了巽卦初始的犹豫状态——进也不是、退也不是，举棋不定。“利武人之贞”建议此时需要武人般的果断和坚定。巽卦虽然强调柔顺，但过度的犹豫反而有害。这一爻教导我们：柔顺不等于优柔寡断，在需要决断的时候必须果断。巽的智慧是“柔中有刚”，而非一味的软弱。
lines.detailEn: The first six describes initial hesitation—advancing and retreating indecisively. 'The perseverance of a warrior furthers' advises that despite Xun's emphasis on gentleness, excessive hesitation is harmful. Gentleness does not mean indecisiveness; when decisions are needed, be decisive. Xun's wisdom is 'firmness within softness,' not pure weakness.

lines.textZh: 九二：巽在床下，用史巫纷若，吉无咎。
lines.textEn: Nine in the second place: Penetration under the bed. Priests and magicians are used in great number. Good fortune. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 谦卑至极如伏于床下，借助祝史巫觋之力，吉利无咎。
lines.interpretationEn: Extreme humility, penetrating beneath the bed. Employing many intermediaries brings fortune.
lines.detailZh: 九二“巽在床下，用史巫纷若，吉无咎”——巽顺到了床下的位置，用祝史和巫师纷纷祈祷。九二阳爻居阴位，过于谦卑。“床下”象征极度的谦逊，甚至有些过分。但“用史巫纷若”表示通过各种渠道反复沟通和祈求，最终“吉无咎”。这一爻说明：在某些情况下，极度的谦逊和反复的沟通虽然看起来卑微，但确实能解决问题。
lines.detailEn: The second nine—'Penetration under the bed. Priests and magicians are used in great number. Good fortune. No blame'—represents extreme humility, even seemingly excessive. Yet through multiple channels of repeated communication and supplication, good fortune results. Sometimes extreme humility and persistent communication, though appearing lowly, genuinely solve problems.

lines.textZh: 九三：频巽，吝。
lines.textEn: Nine in the third place: Repeated penetration. Humiliation.
lines.interpretationZh: 反复屈从，过于卑顺，有所困窘。
lines.interpretationEn: Repeated submission becomes humiliating. Excessive yielding brings shame.
lines.detailZh: 九三“频巽，吝”——反复犹豫、频繁改变主意，困窘。九三处于下卦之顶，阳爻居阳位，本应刚健果断，却陷入了“频巽”的状态——一会儿顺从这个，一会儿顺从那个，没有自己的主见。这一爻警告我们：柔顺是美德，但没有原则的柔顺就变成了随波逐流。真正的巽德是有方向的渗透，而非无目的的摇摆。
lines.detailEn: The third nine—'Repeated penetration. Humiliation'—warns against vacillation and constantly changing direction. Yielding is a virtue, but yielding without principles becomes aimless drifting. True Xun virtue is penetration with direction, not purposeless wavering.

lines.textZh: 六四：悔亡，田获三品。
lines.textEn: Six in the fourth place: Remorse vanishes. During the hunt three kinds of game are caught.
lines.interpretationZh: 悔恨消失，如田猎获得三种猎物，收获丰厚。
lines.interpretationEn: Remorse vanishes. Three kinds of game are caught in the hunt. Rich rewards.
lines.detailZh: 六四“悔亡，田获三品”——后悔消失，打猎获得三种猎物。六四进入上卦，柔顺得位，是巽德发挥作用的阶段。“田获三品”象征丰厚的收获——通过持续的柔顺渗透，终于获得了实质性的成果。“三品”在古代指祭祀用的三种猎物，代表最高规格的收获。这一爻鼓励我们：坚持巽的策略，耐心终将得到丰厚的回报。
lines.detailEn: The fourth six—'Remorse vanishes. During the hunt, three kinds of game are caught'—represents the stage where gentle persistence finally yields substantial results. 'Three kinds of game' symbolizes the highest level of harvest. Through sustained gentle penetration, rich rewards finally materialize. Patience with Xun's strategy ultimately brings abundant returns.

lines.textZh: 九五：贞吉悔亡，无不利。无初有终。先庚三日，后庚三日，吉。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fifth place: Perseverance brings good fortune. Remorse vanishes. Nothing that does not further. No beginning, but an end. Before the change, three days. After the change, three days. Good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 坚守正道则吉，悔恨消失。虽无好的开始但有好的结局，变革前后各需三日准备。
lines.interpretationEn: Perseverance brings fortune and remorse vanishes. No good beginning but a good end. Allow time before and after changes.
lines.detailZh: 九五“贞吉悔亡，无不利。无初有终。先庚三日，后庚三日，吉”是巽卦最吉利的一爻。“无初有终”意为开始时可能不顺利，但最终会有好结果。“先庚三日，后庚三日”与蛊卦的“先甲三日，后甲三日”相呼应，强调变革前后都需要充分的准备和善后。九五居尊位，代表以巽德治理天下的领导者——温和而坚定，渐进而彻底。
lines.detailEn: The fifth nine is Xun's most auspicious line. 'No beginning but an end' means initial difficulties lead to good outcomes. 'Before the change, three days; after the change, three days' emphasizes thorough preparation before and careful follow-through after any reform. This represents leadership through Xun virtue—gentle yet firm, gradual yet thorough.

lines.textZh: 上九：巽在床下，丧其资斧，贞凶。
lines.textEn: Nine at the top: Penetration under the bed. He loses his property and his ax. Perseverance brings misfortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 过度卑顺伏于床下，丧失了财物和权柄，坚持下去则凶。
lines.interpretationEn: Excessive submission beneath the bed. Property and authority are lost. Persisting brings misfortune.
lines.detailZh: 上九“巽在床下，丧其资斧，贞凶”——巽顺到了床下，还丢失了资财和斧头，坚守正道也是凶险的。上九是巽卦的极端——柔顺过度，已经失去了自我和行动的能力（丧其资斧）。斧头象征决断力，丢失斧头意味着完全丧失了果断行动的能力。这一爻严厉警告：柔顺到了极端就变成了软弱和无能。巽卦的智慧是“柔中有刚”，一旦失去了“刚”的部分，就只剩下无力的屈服。
lines.detailEn: The top nine—'Penetration under the bed. He loses his property and his ax. Perseverance brings misfortune'—represents Xun taken to its extreme. Excessive yielding leads to losing both resources (property) and decisiveness (the ax). This line sternly warns that gentleness pushed to the extreme becomes weakness and incompetence. Xun's wisdom is 'firmness within softness'; lose the firmness, and only powerless submission remains.

overviewZh: 巽卦是《周易》第五十七卦，由上巽（风）下巽（风）组成，重风相叠，象征风行天下、无孔不入。“巽”的本义是顺从、渗透，引申为柔顺、谦逊、深入。风的特性是无形却无处不在，能穿透一切缝隙——这正是巽卦的核心智慧：以柔克刚、以渐进代替激进、以渗透代替强攻。巽卦不是软弱的顺从，而是一种高明的策略——像风一样，看似温柔却能移山填海。
overviewEn: Xun (The Gentle/Wind) is the 57th hexagram, composed of Xun (wind) doubled—wind upon wind, symbolizing pervasive, penetrating influence. 'Xun' means yielding, penetrating, and gradual—the wind is invisible yet omnipresent, able to pass through every crack. This is Xun's core wisdom: overcoming hardness with softness, replacing radical action with gradual progress, substituting penetration for frontal assault. Xun is not weak submission but a sophisticated strategy—like wind, seemingly gentle yet capable of moving mountains and filling seas.
judgmentDetailZh: 巽卦卦辞“巽：小亨，利有攸往，利见大人”表明巽卦的通达是“小亨”——不是轰轰烈烈的大成功，而是润物细无声的渐进式成功。《彖传》释曰：“重巽以申命，刚巽乎中正而志行，柔皆顺乎刚，是以小亨”——重复的巽象征反复申明命令，以柔顺的方式贯彻刚正的意志。“利有攸往”鼓励前进，但方式是巽的方式——不是冲锋陷阵，而是循序渐进。“利见大人”提示在渐进的过程中需要有明确的方向和引导者。
judgmentDetailEn: The judgment 'The Gentle. Success through what is small. It furthers one to have somewhere to go. It furthers one to see the great man' indicates that Xun's success is 'small'—not dramatic breakthroughs but gradual, silent progress like rain nourishing the earth. The doubled Xun symbolizes repeatedly issuing commands, implementing firm will through gentle means. 'Having somewhere to go' encourages forward movement, but in Xun's way—not charging ahead but advancing step by step.
imageDetailZh: 象辞“随风，巽。君子以申命行事”描述了风接连不断吹拂的意象。“申命”意为反复申明命令——一次命令可能被忽视，但像风一样持续不断地传达，最终一定能深入人心。这是巽卦给出的管理智慧：重要的决策和理念不能只说一次就期望所有人理解执行，需要反复沟通、持续强调，像风一样不断渗透。在教育中，这就是“循循善诱”；在管理中，这就是“持续沟通”；在营销中，这就是“品牌渗透”。
imageDetailEn: The Image 'Winds following one upon the other: Xun. The superior man spreads his commands abroad and carries out his undertakings' describes winds blowing continuously. 'Spreading commands' means repeatedly communicating decisions—a single announcement may be ignored, but continuous delivery like persistent wind eventually penetrates every mind. In education, this is 'patient guidance'; in management, 'continuous communication'; in marketing, 'brand penetration.'
modernApplicationZh: 【事业】巽卦提示以渐进策略推进工作。不要期望一步到位，而是像风一样持续渗透。在推动变革时，“申命行事”——反复沟通、持续推进，比一次性的强制推行更有效。适合做长期规划、品牌建设、市场渗透等需要耐心的工作。【感情】巽卦在感情中代表温柔的坚持。不是猛烈的追求，而是持续的关心和陪伴。“随风”的方式——像春风一样温暖而持久，比暴风骤雨式的热情更能打动人心。【财运】巽卦象征稳健的长期投资策略。不追求暴利，而是通过持续的小额投入获得稳定回报。定投、复利、长期持有——这些都是巽卦的理财智慧。【健康】巽卦对应呼吸系统和神经系统（风为气），注意呼吸调理和情绪管理。练习深呼吸、冥想等“以柔克刚”的养生方法，效果优于剧烈运动。
modernApplicationEn: 【Career】Xun advises gradual strategy. Don't expect instant results; penetrate continuously like wind. When driving change, 'repeatedly communicate and steadily advance' works better than one-time enforcement. Ideal for long-term planning, brand building, and market penetration.【Love】Xun represents gentle persistence—not aggressive pursuit but continuous care and companionship. Like a spring breeze, warm and lasting, more touching than stormy passion.【Wealth】Xun symbolizes steady long-term investment. Don't chase windfall profits; achieve stable returns through consistent small contributions. Dollar-cost averaging, compound interest, long-term holding—all embody Xun's financial wisdom.【Health】Xun corresponds to respiratory and nervous systems. Practice breathing exercises and meditation—'overcoming hardness with softness' wellness methods often outperform intense exercise.
historicalStoryZh: 巽卦最经典的历史案例是刘备“三顾茅庐”。刘备为请诸葛亮出山，三次亲赴隆中拜访，每次都以最谦逊的姿态等候。这正是巽卦“柔顺渗透”的最佳体现——不是强迫，不是利诱，而是以真诚和耐心反复表达诚意，最终感动了诸葛亮。另一个典故是“水滴石穿”——水是天下至柔之物，却能穿透最坚硬的石头，靠的就是巽卦“持续渗透”的力量。老子说“天下莫柔弱于水，而攻坚强者莫之能胜”，这与巽卦的精神完全一致。
historicalStoryEn: The classic example of Xun is Liu Bei's 'Three Visits to the Thatched Cottage.' To recruit Zhuge Liang, Liu Bei visited three times with the utmost humility and patience—not through force or bribery but through sincere, repeated expression of genuine intent, ultimately moving Zhuge Liang to join his cause. Another example is 'water dripping through stone'—water, the softest substance, penetrates the hardest rock through persistent, gentle force. Laozi said 'Nothing in the world is softer than water, yet nothing is better at overcoming the hard and strong'—perfectly aligned with Xun's spirit.
relatedHexagramsNote: 巽卦的综卦为兑卦（第58卦），巽为入、兑为悦，一柔入一喜悦，渗透之后带来愉悦；错卦为震卦（第51卦），巽的柔顺与震的刚动形成对比。巽兑相综，揭示了“先以柔顺渗透，后获喜悦回报”的人生智慧。

judgmentZh: 兑：亨，利贞。
judgmentEn: The Joyous. Success. Perseverance is favorable.
imageZh: 丽泽，兑；君子以朋友讲习。
imageEn: Lakes resting one on the other: the image of the Joyous. Thus the superior man joins with his friends for discussion and practice.
interpretationZh: 兑卦象征泽，喜悦而和谐。两泽相连，互相滋润，以喜悦之心待人，朋友切磋共进。
interpretationEn: Dui symbolizes the lake and joyousness. Two lakes connected nourish each other. Joy shared with friends through mutual learning and encouragement.

lines.textZh: 初九：和兑，吉。
lines.textEn: Nine at the beginning: Contented joyousness. Good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 和谐的喜悦，内心平和而快乐，吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: Harmonious joy from within. Contentment brings good fortune.

lines.textZh: 九二：孚兑，吉，悔亡。
lines.textEn: Nine in the second place: Sincere joyousness. Good fortune. Remorse disappears.
lines.interpretationZh: 以诚信带来的喜悦，吉利，悔恨消失。
lines.interpretationEn: Joy grounded in sincerity. Good fortune and remorse vanishes.

lines.textZh: 六三：来兑，凶。
lines.textEn: Six in the third place: Coming joyousness. Misfortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 刻意追求外来的快乐，迎合取悦，凶险。
lines.interpretationEn: Seeking joy from external sources. Pandering to pleasure brings misfortune.

lines.textZh: 九四：商兑未宁，介疾有喜。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fourth place: Joyousness that is weighed is not at peace. After ridding himself of faults a man has joy.
lines.interpretationZh: 权衡取舍尚未安宁，去除弊病之后方有喜悦。
lines.interpretationEn: Deliberating over pleasures brings no peace. Joy comes after eliminating faults.

lines.textZh: 九五：孚于剥，有厉。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fifth place: Sincerity toward disintegrating influences is dangerous.
lines.interpretationZh: 信任那些有害的事物，有危险。对小人的信任会带来灾祸。
lines.interpretationEn: Trusting in destructive influences is dangerous. Beware of misplaced sincerity.

lines.textZh: 上六：引兑。
lines.textEn: Six at the top: Seductive joyousness.
lines.interpretationZh: 引诱他人的喜悦，沉溺于享乐，被欲望牵引。
lines.interpretationEn: Seductive joy that leads others astray. Drawn along by desire and indulgence.

judgmentZh: 涣：亨。王假有庙，利涉大川，利贞。
judgmentEn: Dispersion. Success. The king approaches his temple. It furthers one to cross the great water. Perseverance furthers.
imageZh: 风行水上，涣；先王以享于帝立庙。
imageEn: The wind drives over the water: the image of Dispersion. Thus the kings of old sacrificed to the Lord and built temples.
interpretationZh: 涣卦象征涣散，风行水上，波浪四散。以祭祀凝聚人心，以大义化解离散，方能重新团结。
interpretationEn: Huan symbolizes dispersion. Wind blowing over water scatters it. Unite scattered hearts through shared devotion and higher purpose.

lines.textZh: 初六：用拯马壮，吉。
lines.textEn: Six at the beginning: He brings help with the strength of a horse. Good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 用壮马之力来拯救，及时施救则吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: Rescue comes with the strength of a horse. Swift aid brings good fortune.

lines.textZh: 九二：涣奔其机，悔亡。
lines.textEn: Nine in the second place: At the dissolution he hurries to that which supports him. Remorse disappears.
lines.interpretationZh: 涣散之时奔向可依靠之处，悔恨消失。
lines.interpretationEn: In times of dispersion, hasten to a place of support. Remorse vanishes.

lines.textZh: 六三：涣其躬，无悔。
lines.textEn: Six in the third place: He dissolves his self. No remorse.
lines.interpretationZh: 化解自身的私欲执着，无所悔恨。
lines.interpretationEn: Dissolving selfish concerns. No remorse when ego is set aside.

lines.textZh: 六四：涣其群，元吉。涣有丘，匪夷所思。
lines.textEn: Six in the fourth place: He dissolves his bond with his group. Supreme good fortune. Dispersion leads to accumulation. This is something that ordinary men do not think of.
lines.interpretationZh: 化解小团体的私利，大吉。涣散之后重新聚合如丘，非常人所能想到。
lines.interpretationEn: Dissolving factional bonds brings supreme fortune. Dispersion leads to a higher gathering beyond ordinary thinking.

lines.textZh: 九五：涣汗其大号，涣王居，无咎。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fifth place: His loud cries are as dissolving as sweat. Dissolution! A king abides without blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 如汗水般发布重大号令，王者居正位化解涣散，无咎。
lines.interpretationEn: Great proclamations dissolve barriers like sweat. The king holds his position without blame.

lines.textZh: 上九：涣其血，去逖出，无咎。
lines.textEn: Nine at the top: He dissolves his blood. Departing, keeping at a distance, going out, is without blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 化解血光之灾，远离危险，离去则无咎。
lines.interpretationEn: Dissolving bloodshed by departing and keeping distance. No blame in withdrawal.

judgmentZh: 节：亨。苦节不可贞。
judgmentEn: Limitation. Success. Galling limitation must not be persevered in.
imageZh: 泽上有水，节；君子以制数度，议德行。
imageEn: Water over lake: the image of Limitation. Thus the superior man creates number and measure, and examines the nature of virtue and correct conduct.
interpretationZh: 节卦象征节制，泽上有水，水量有限。适度节制则亨通，过度苦节则不可持久。
interpretationEn: Jie symbolizes limitation. Water over the lake is finite. Moderate restraint brings success, but bitter limitation cannot endure.

lines.textZh: 初九：不出户庭，无咎。
lines.textEn: Nine at the beginning: Not going out of the door and the courtyard is without blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 不出门庭，知所节制，无过失。
lines.interpretationEn: Not going beyond the door and courtyard. Knowing one's limits brings no blame.

lines.textZh: 九二：不出门庭，凶。
lines.textEn: Nine in the second place: Not going out of the gate and the courtyard brings misfortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 不出门庭，错失时机，过度节制反而凶险。
lines.interpretationEn: Not going beyond the gate brings misfortune. Excessive restraint at the wrong time.

lines.textZh: 六三：不节若，则嗟若，无咎。
lines.textEn: Six in the third place: He who knows no limitation will have cause to lament. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 不知节制就会有悲叹，但能自省则无咎。
lines.interpretationEn: Without limitation comes lamentation. But self-awareness removes blame.

lines.textZh: 六四：安节，亨。
lines.textEn: Six in the fourth place: Contented limitation. Success.
lines.interpretationZh: 安然接受节制，自然而然地守度，亨通。
lines.interpretationEn: Contented acceptance of limitation. Natural restraint brings success.

lines.textZh: 九五：甘节，吉；往有尚。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fifth place: Sweet limitation brings good fortune. Going brings esteem.
lines.interpretationZh: 甘心节制，吉利；前往行事会受到尊崇。
lines.interpretationEn: Sweet limitation brings good fortune. Taking action earns esteem.

lines.textZh: 上六：苦节，贞凶，悔亡。
lines.textEn: Six at the top: Galling limitation. Perseverance brings misfortune. Remorse disappears.
lines.interpretationZh: 苦涩的节制，坚持下去则凶，但悔恨终会消失。
lines.interpretationEn: Bitter limitation. Persisting brings misfortune, yet remorse eventually disappears.

judgmentZh: 中孚：豚鱼吉，利涉大川，利贞。
judgmentEn: Inner Truth. Pigs and fishes. Good fortune. It furthers one to cross the great water. Perseverance furthers.
imageZh: 泽上有风，中孚；君子以议狱缓死。
imageEn: Wind over lake: the image of Inner Truth. Thus the superior man discusses criminal cases in order to delay executions.
interpretationZh: 中孚卦象征内心的诚信，风吹泽上，感化万物。以至诚之心待人，即使猪鱼之微也能感通，利于涉险。
interpretationEn: Zhong Fu symbolizes inner sincerity. Wind blowing over the lake penetrates all things. With utmost sincerity, even the lowliest creatures are moved.

lines.textZh: 初九：虞吉，有它不燕。
lines.textEn: Nine at the beginning: Being prepared brings good fortune. If there are secret designs, it is disquieting.
lines.interpretationZh: 有所准备则吉利，若心有旁骛则不安宁。
lines.interpretationEn: Preparedness brings fortune. Hidden agendas bring unease.

lines.textZh: 九二：鸣鹤在阴，其子和之。我有好爵，吾与尔靡之。
lines.textEn: Nine in the second place: A crane calling in the shade. Its young answers it. I have a good goblet. I will share it with you.
lines.interpretationZh: 鹤在树荫中鸣叫，幼鹤应声相和。诚信之感应，如同分享美酒。
lines.interpretationEn: A crane calls from the shade and its young responds. Sincerity resonates like sharing a fine goblet.

lines.textZh: 六三：得敌，或鼓或罢，或泣或歌。
lines.textEn: Six in the third place: He finds a comrade. Now he beats the drum, now he stops. Now he sobs, now he sings.
lines.interpretationZh: 遇到对手，时而击鼓进攻，时而停止；时而哭泣，时而歌唱，情绪不定。
lines.interpretationEn: Finding a match, one alternates between drumming and stopping, weeping and singing. Emotions are unstable.

lines.textZh: 六四：月几望，马匹亡，无咎。
lines.textEn: Six in the fourth place: The moon nearly at the full. The team horse goes astray. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 月亮将满，马匹走失，但无过失。接近圆满时宜舍弃旧伴。
lines.interpretationEn: The moon is nearly full. A horse strays from its team. No blame in letting go.

lines.textZh: 九五：有孚挛如，无咎。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fifth place: He possesses truth, which links together. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 以诚信紧密联结众人，无过失。
lines.interpretationEn: Sincerity binds all together. No blame.

lines.textZh: 上九：翰音登于天，贞凶。
lines.textEn: Nine at the top: Cockcrow penetrating to heaven. Perseverance brings misfortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 鸡鸣之声传至天际，虚声张扬，坚持则凶。
lines.interpretationEn: A cockcrow reaches heaven — empty noise. Persisting in pretense brings misfortune.

judgmentZh: 小过：亨，利贞，可小事，不可大事。飞鸟遗之音，不宜上宜下，大吉。
judgmentEn: Preponderance of the Small. Success. Perseverance furthers. Small things may be done; great things should not be done. The flying bird brings the message: It is not well to strive upward, it is well to remain below. Great good fortune.
imageZh: 山上有雷，小过；君子以行过乎恭，丧过乎哀，用过乎俭。
imageEn: Thunder on the mountain: the image of Preponderance of the Small. Thus in his conduct the superior man gives preponderance to reverence. In bereavement he gives preponderance to grief. In his expenditures he gives preponderance to thrift.
interpretationZh: 小过卦象征小有过越，山上有雷，雷声虽大但被山阻隔。宜做小事，不宜做大事，谦卑处下则大吉。
interpretationEn: Xiao Guo symbolizes slight excess. Thunder on the mountain is loud but contained. Attend to small matters, not great ones. Humility below brings great fortune.

lines.textZh: 初六：飞鸟以凶。
lines.textEn: Six at the beginning: The bird meets with misfortune through flying.
lines.interpretationZh: 飞鸟高飞而遭凶险，不宜冒进。
lines.interpretationEn: The bird flies too high and meets misfortune. Do not overreach.

lines.textZh: 六二：过其祖，遇其妣；不及其君，遇其臣，无咎。
lines.textEn: Six in the second place: She passes by her ancestor and meets her ancestress. He does not reach his prince and meets the official. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 越过祖父而遇到祖母，未及君主而遇到臣子，无过失。
lines.interpretationEn: Passing the grandfather, meeting the grandmother. Not reaching the prince, meeting the minister. No blame.

lines.textZh: 九三：弗过防之，从或戕之，凶。
lines.textEn: Nine in the third place: If one is not extraordinarily careful, somebody may come from behind and strike him. Misfortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 不加倍防备，可能被人从背后伤害，凶险。
lines.interpretationEn: Without extraordinary caution, one may be struck from behind. Misfortune.

lines.textZh: 九四：无咎，弗过遇之。往厉必戒，勿用永贞。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fourth place: No blame. He meets him without passing by. Going brings danger. One must be on guard. Do not act. Be constantly persevering.
lines.interpretationZh: 无过失，不越过而恰好遇到。前往有危险，必须警戒，不可妄动。
lines.interpretationEn: No blame. Meeting without overstepping. Going forward is dangerous — be cautious and do not act rashly.

lines.textZh: 六五：密云不雨，自我西郊，公弋取彼在穴。
lines.textEn: Six in the fifth place: Dense clouds, no rain from our western territory. The prince shoots and hits him who is in the cave.
lines.interpretationZh: 密云不雨，来自西郊。公侯射箭取得穴中之物，虽有努力但时机未到。
lines.interpretationEn: Dense clouds but no rain from the west. The prince shoots into the cave. Effort is made but the time is not yet ripe.

lines.textZh: 上六：弗遇过之，飞鸟离之，凶，是谓灾眚。
lines.textEn: Six at the top: He passes him by, not meeting him. The flying bird leaves him. Misfortune. This means bad luck and injury.
lines.interpretationZh: 错过而不相遇，飞鸟远离，凶险，这就是灾祸。
lines.interpretationEn: Missing the encounter entirely. The bird flies away. Misfortune — this is calamity.

judgmentZh: 既济：亨小，利贞，初吉终乱。
judgmentEn: After Completion. Success in small matters. Perseverance furthers. At the beginning good fortune, at the end disorder.
imageZh: 水在火上，既济；君子以思患而预防之。
imageEn: Water over fire: the image of the condition in After Completion. Thus the superior man takes thought of misfortune and arms himself against it in advance.
interpretationZh: 既济卦象征事已成就，水在火上，阴阳各得其位。虽已完成但须警惕，初始吉利而终将生乱。
interpretationEn: Ji Ji symbolizes completion. Water over fire — all lines are in their proper places. Though complete, one must remain vigilant. Good fortune at first, disorder at the end.

lines.textZh: 初九：曳其轮，濡其尾，无咎。
lines.textEn: Nine at the beginning: He brakes his wheels. He gets his tail in the water. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 拖住车轮减速，尾巴沾湿了水，谨慎行事则无过失。
lines.interpretationEn: Braking the wheels, the tail gets wet. Caution at the start brings no blame.
lines.detailZh: 初九“曳其轮，濡其尾，无咎”描绘了渡河之初的谨慎：拖住车轮减速，尾巴沾湿了水。刚刚完成大事的人，应该像刚渡过河一样保持警觉，不要因为成功就放松警惕。“曳轮”象征主动减速、控制节奏，这在成功之初尤为重要。现代启示：项目刚上线时不要急于庆功，先确保系统稳定运行。
lines.detailEn: The first nine depicts caution at the start of completion: dragging the wheels to slow down, the tail getting wet. One who has just accomplished something great should remain vigilant like someone who has just crossed a river. 'Dragging the wheels' symbolizes deliberately slowing down and controlling the pace—especially important at the beginning of success.

lines.textZh: 六二：妇丧其茀，勿逐，七日得。
lines.textEn: Six in the second place: The woman loses the curtain of her carriage. Do not run after it; on the seventh day you will get it.
lines.interpretationZh: 妇人丢失了车帘，不必追赶，七日后自然失而复得。
lines.interpretationEn: The woman loses her carriage curtain. Do not chase it; in seven days it returns.
lines.detailZh: 六二“妇丧其茀，勿逐，七日得”中“茀”是车帘，妇人丢失了车帘不必追赶，七天后自然会找回。这一爻教导我们在成功之后要有耐心和定力——暂时的小损失不必过于在意，不要因小失大。急于追回失去的东西反而可能造成更大的损失。保持从容，该来的自然会来。
lines.detailEn: The second six—'The woman loses the curtain of her carriage. Do not run after it; on the seventh day you will get it'—teaches patience and composure after success. Don't overreact to minor losses or chase after small things at the risk of greater harm. Maintain composure; what is meant to return will return naturally.

lines.textZh: 九三：高宗伐鬼方，三年克之，小人勿用。
lines.textEn: Nine in the third place: The Illustrious Ancestor disciplines the Devil's Country. After three years he conquers it. Inferior people must not be employed.
lines.interpretationZh: 高宗讨伐鬼方，三年才征服。大事需要时间，不可用小人。
lines.interpretationEn: The Illustrious Ancestor conquers the Devil's Country after three years. Do not employ inferior people.
lines.detailZh: 九三“高宗伐鬼方，三年克之，小人勿用”引用了殷高宗武丁征伐鬼方（北方部族）的历史。即使在国力鼎盛时期，征服边远之敌也需要三年之久，说明成功之后的挑战依然艰巨。“小人勿用”警告在关键时刻不能任用品德低下的人。这一爻提醒我们：守成比创业更难，需要更大的耐心和更严格的用人标准。
lines.detailEn: The third nine references Emperor Wu Ding's three-year campaign against the Gui Fang—even at the height of national power, conquering distant enemies required three years, showing that post-success challenges remain formidable. 'Do not employ inferior people' warns against appointing those of poor character at critical moments. Maintaining success is harder than achieving it.

lines.textZh: 六四：繻有衣袽，终日戒。
lines.textEn: Six in the fourth place: The finest clothes turn to rags. Be careful all day long.
lines.interpretationZh: 华丽的衣服下备有破布，整日保持警戒。
lines.interpretationEn: Fine garments may become rags. Stay vigilant throughout the day.
lines.detailZh: 六四“繻有衣袽，终日戒”中“繻”是精美的丝绸，“衣袽”是破旧的棉絮。即使穿着华丽的衣服，里面也要准备破旧的棉衣——这是居安思危的生动比喻。“终日戒”意为整天保持警戒。在繁华的表象下时刻准备应对困难，这正是既济卦“思患预防”精神的具体体现。
lines.detailEn: The fourth six—'The finest clothes turn to rags. Be careful all day long'—vividly illustrates preparedness amid prosperity. Even wearing silk, keep cotton padding ready underneath. 'Be careful all day long' means maintaining constant vigilance. Preparing for difficulties beneath the surface of prosperity perfectly embodies Ji Ji's spirit of preventive thinking.

lines.textZh: 九五：东邻杀牛，不如西邻之禴祭，实受其福。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fifth place: The neighbor in the east who slaughters an ox does not attain as much real happiness as the neighbor in the west with his small offering.
lines.interpretationZh: 东邻杀牛大祭，不如西邻简朴之祭实际受福。诚心胜过排场。
lines.interpretationEn: The eastern neighbor's grand sacrifice gains less than the western neighbor's simple offering. Sincerity surpasses display.
lines.detailZh: 九五“东邻杀牛，不如西邻之禴祭，实受其福”对比了两种祭祀方式：东边邻国宰杀大牛举行盛大祭典，不如西边邻国简朴的薄祭更能获得真正的福报。这一爻深刻揭示了“形式不如实质”的道理——成功之后不要追求排场和虚荣，真诚朴实的态度才能获得持久的福报。铺张浪费往往是衰败的开始。
lines.detailEn: The fifth nine contrasts two approaches: 'The neighbor in the east who slaughters an ox does not attain as much real happiness as the neighbor in the west with his small offering.' This profoundly reveals that substance matters more than form—after success, don't pursue extravagance and vanity. Sincere simplicity brings lasting blessings, while lavish waste often marks the beginning of decline.

lines.textZh: 上六：濡其首，厉。
lines.textEn: Six at the top: He gets his head in the water. Danger.
lines.interpretationZh: 头浸入水中，危险。过度冒进，陷入险境。
lines.interpretationEn: The head gets into the water. Danger from going too far.
lines.detailZh: 上六“濡其首，厉”与初九的“濡其尾”形成首尾呼应。初爻渡河时只是尾巴沾湿，到了上爻却是头都浸入水中——危险已经从小变大。这是既济卦最后的警告：如果在成功之后不保持警惕，最终会陷入灭顶之灾。“厉”即危险，从“濡尾”到“濡首”，完美诠释了“初吉终乱”的全过程。
lines.detailEn: The top six—'He gets his head in the water. Danger'—echoes the first line's 'getting the tail wet.' At the beginning, only the tail was dampened; by the end, the head is submerged—danger has escalated from minor to catastrophic. This is Ji Ji's final warning: without vigilance after success, one ultimately faces overwhelming disaster. From 'wet tail' to 'wet head' perfectly illustrates the complete arc of 'good fortune at the beginning, disorder at the end.'

overviewZh: 既济卦是《周易》第六十三卦，由上坎（水）下离（火）组成，水在火上，水火相交而各得其用。“既济”意为“已经渡过河”，象征事情已经完成、目标已经达到。这是六十四卦中唯一六爻全部当位的卦——阳爻居阳位、阴爻居阴位，秩序完美。然而正因为太完美，反而暗藏危机：物极必反，盛极而衰。既济卦的核心警示是“初吉终乱”——开始时一切顺利，但如果不保持警惕，结局可能混乱。
overviewEn: Ji Ji (After Completion) is the 63rd hexagram, composed of Kan (water) above and Li (fire) below—water over fire, each element serving its proper function. 'Ji Ji' means 'already crossed the river,' symbolizing tasks completed and goals achieved. It is the only hexagram where all six lines are perfectly positioned—yang in yang places, yin in yin places, perfect order. Yet precisely because of this perfection, danger lurks: what reaches its peak must decline. The core warning of Ji Ji is 'good fortune at the beginning, disorder at the end'—everything starts well, but without vigilance, the outcome may be chaotic.
judgmentDetailZh: 既济卦辞“亨小，利贞。初吉终乱”看似矛盾：既然已经成功，为何只是“小亨”？为何“初吉终乱”？这正是既济卦最深刻的智慧。事情完成之后，发展的空间反而变小了（亨小）；成功之后最需要的是守正（利贞）。“初吉终乱”是对所有成功者的警钟——成功的那一刻恰恰是危险的开始。《彖传》曰：“既济亨，小者亨也。利贞，刚柔正而位当也”，强调即使在完美的状态下，也只能获得小的通达，必须坚守正道才能维持。历史上无数王朝在鼎盛时期埋下衰亡的种子，正应了既济之理。
judgmentDetailEn: The judgment 'Success in small matters. Perseverance furthers. At the beginning good fortune, at the end disorder' seems paradoxical: if completion has been achieved, why only 'small success'? Why 'disorder at the end'? This is Ji Ji's most profound wisdom. After completion, room for growth actually shrinks; after success, what's most needed is maintaining correctness. 'Good fortune at the beginning, disorder at the end' is a warning bell for all who succeed—the moment of success is precisely when danger begins. Countless dynasties throughout history planted the seeds of their decline at the height of their power, perfectly illustrating Ji Ji's principle.
imageDetailZh: 象辞“水在火上，既济。君子以思患而预防之”揭示了既济卦最重要的实践智慧。水在火上，火能烧水、水能灭火，看似和谐实则暗含相克。“思患而预防之”——在一切顺利的时候就要思考可能出现的祸患，并提前做好防范。这就是“居安思危”的哲学源头。真正的智者不是在危机来临时才应对，而是在太平时期就未雨绸缪。企业在盈利最好的时候布局转型，国家在和平时期加强国防，个人在健康时注重养生——这都是既济卦的智慧。
imageDetailEn: The Image 'Water over fire: the image of the condition in After Completion. Thus the superior man takes thought of misfortune and arms himself against it in advance' reveals Ji Ji's most important practical wisdom. Water above fire appears harmonious but contains inherent conflict—fire heats water, water extinguishes fire. 'Taking thought of misfortune and arming against it' means contemplating potential disasters during times of success and preparing preventive measures. This is the philosophical origin of 'think of danger in times of safety.' The truly wise don't wait for crises to respond—they prepare during peaceful times.
modernApplicationZh: 【事业】既济卦提醒我们：项目完成、目标达成后是最危险的时刻。成功容易带来松懈和自满，团队可能失去方向感。此时最重要的是“思患预防”——复盘总结、规划下一阶段、保持危机意识。很多企业在最辉煌时倒下，正是忽视了既济的警示。【感情】关系稳定后容易进入“倦怠期”，既济卦提醒我们不要把稳定当作理所当然。持续经营感情、保持新鲜感、关注对方的变化，才能让关系长久。【财运】投资获利后最容易犯的错误是贪心不止。既济卦建议适时止盈、落袋为安，不要在成功后加大赌注。“初吉终乱”的教训适用于所有投资决策。【健康】身体状况良好时最容易忽视保养。既济卦提醒我们在健康时就要建立良好的生活习惯，定期体检，不要等到生病才后悔。
modernApplicationEn: 【Career】Ji Ji warns that the moment after achieving goals is the most dangerous. Success breeds complacency and loss of direction. The priority is 'thinking of misfortune in advance'—conducting reviews, planning next phases, maintaining crisis awareness. Many companies collapsed at their peak by ignoring this warning.【Love】After relationships stabilize, fatigue easily sets in. Ji Ji reminds us not to take stability for granted—continuously nurture the relationship and stay attentive to changes.【Wealth】The most common mistake after investment gains is endless greed. Ji Ji advises timely profit-taking. 'Good fortune at the beginning, disorder at the end' applies to all investment decisions.【Health】Good health is when we most easily neglect self-care. Ji Ji reminds us to establish healthy habits and regular checkups while we're well, not wait until illness strikes.
historicalStoryZh: 既济卦最经典的历史印证是唐玄宗与开元盛世。唐玄宗李隆基开创了中国历史上最辉煌的盛世——开元之治，政治清明、经济繁荣、文化灿烂，堪称“既济”的完美状态。然而正是在这鼎盛时期，玄宗开始沉迷享乐、宠信杨贵妃、重用李林甫和杨国忠，最终引发安史之乱，大唐由盛转衰。从开元盛世到安史之乱，完美诠释了既济卦“初吉终乱”的预言。另一个例子是诸葛亮在《出师表》中的“亲贤臣，远小人，此先汉所以兴隆也；亲小人，远贤臣，此后汉所以倾颓也”，同样是既济之理的体现。
historicalStoryEn: The most classic historical illustration of Ji Ji is Emperor Xuanzong and the Kaiyuan Prosperity of the Tang Dynasty. Xuanzong created the most brilliant golden age in Chinese history—exemplary governance, economic prosperity, and cultural brilliance, a perfect state of 'completion.' Yet at this very peak, Xuanzong began indulging in pleasure, favoring Yang Guifei, and empowering corrupt officials, ultimately triggering the An Lushan Rebellion that transformed Tang from prosperity to decline. From Kaiyuan glory to An Lushan chaos perfectly demonstrates Ji Ji's prophecy of 'good fortune at the beginning, disorder at the end.'
relatedHexagramsNote: 既济卦的综卦为未济卦（第64卦），一成一未成，互为表里；错卦为火水未济的反面。既济与未济作为六十四卦的最后两卦，寓意深远：事物永远在完成与未完成之间循环，没有绝对的终点。

judgmentZh: 未济：亨，小狐汔济，濡其尾，无攸利。
judgmentEn: Before Completion. Success. But if the little fox, after nearly completing the crossing, gets his tail in the water, there is nothing that would further.
imageZh: 火在水上，未济；君子以慎辨物居方。
imageEn: Fire over water: the image of the condition before transition. Thus the superior man is careful in the differentiation of things, so that each finds its place.
interpretationZh: 未济卦象征事未完成，火在水上，阴阳不交。如小狐渡河将济而未济，须谨慎辨别事物各安其位。
interpretationEn: Wei Ji symbolizes the state before completion. Fire over water — they do not interact. Like a young fox nearly across the stream, caution is needed to place everything properly.

lines.textZh: 初六：濡其尾，吝。
lines.textEn: Six at the beginning: He gets his tail in the water. Humiliating.
lines.interpretationZh: 尾巴浸入水中，有所困窘。冒进而力不足。
lines.interpretationEn: The tail gets in the water. Humiliation from overreaching without sufficient strength.
lines.detailZh: 初六“濡其尾，吝”与既济初九的“濡其尾”相呼应，但含义不同。既济是成功后的谨慎，未济是起步时的困难。小狐狸刚开始渡河就弄湿了尾巴，说明准备不足、时机未到。“吝”表示困窘，但不是凶——只是暂时的困难。这一爻教导我们：在条件不成熟时不要勉强行动，先积蓄力量、等待时机。
lines.detailEn: The first six echoes Ji Ji's first line but with different meaning. In Ji Ji, the wet tail signals caution after success; in Wei Ji, it signals difficulty at the start. The young fox wets its tail at the very beginning—preparation is insufficient, timing is wrong. 'Humiliation' indicates temporary difficulty, not disaster. Don't force action when conditions aren't ripe; gather strength and wait.

lines.textZh: 九二：曳其轮，贞吉。
lines.textEn: Nine in the second place: He brakes his wheels. Perseverance brings good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 拖住车轮不急于前进，坚守正道则吉。
lines.interpretationEn: Braking the wheels. Patience and perseverance bring good fortune.
lines.detailZh: 九二“曳其轮，贞吉”同样与既济相呼应。拖住车轮减速，保持正固则吉。在“未完成”的状态下，最重要的是控制节奏、不要冒进。九二阳爻居阴位虽不当位，但居中有中庸之德。这一爻告诉我们：即使处境不完美，只要保持稳健的节奏和正确的方向，结果依然是吉祥的。
lines.detailEn: The second nine—'He brakes his wheels. Perseverance brings good fortune'—emphasizes controlling pace and avoiding recklessness in an incomplete state. Though the yang line in a yin position is not ideally placed, its central position provides the virtue of moderation. Even in imperfect circumstances, maintaining steady pace and correct direction still brings good fortune.

lines.textZh: 六三：未济，征凶，利涉大川。
lines.textEn: Six in the third place: Before completion, attack brings misfortune. It furthers one to cross the great water.
lines.interpretationZh: 事未完成就出征则凶，但利于涉越大川，需选择正确方向。
lines.interpretationEn: Before completion, attacking brings misfortune. But crossing the great water furthers.
lines.detailZh: 六三“未济，征凶，利涉大川”看似矛盾：尚未完成就出征是凶险的，但渡大河却是有利的。这揭示了一个微妙的道理——在未完成的状态下，小规模的冒进是危险的（征凶），但如果是为了根本性的突破（涉大川），则值得冒险。关键在于区分“小打小闹”和“战略性突破”。
lines.detailEn: The third six seems contradictory: advancing while incomplete is dangerous, yet crossing the great water is favorable. This reveals a subtle truth—in an incomplete state, minor reckless advances are dangerous, but risking everything for a fundamental breakthrough is worthwhile. The key is distinguishing between 'small skirmishes' and 'strategic breakthroughs.'

lines.textZh: 九四：贞吉，悔亡，震用伐鬼方，三年有赏于大国。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fourth place: Perseverance brings good fortune. Remorse disappears. Shock, thus to discipline the Devil's Country. For three years, great realms are rewarded.
lines.interpretationZh: 坚守正道则吉，悔恨消失。奋力讨伐鬼方，三年后获大国封赏。
lines.interpretationEn: Perseverance brings fortune and remorse vanishes. After three years of campaigning, great rewards come.
lines.detailZh: 九四“贞吉，悔亡。震用伐鬼方，三年有赏于大国”与既济九三的“高宗伐鬼方”相呼应。在未济的状态下发动征伐需要极大的决心和毅力（震），经过三年艰苦奋战终于获得大国的赏赐。这一爻鼓励我们：在困难时期，只要方向正确、意志坚定，持续努力终将获得丰厚的回报。三年象征漫长的坚持。
lines.detailEn: The fourth nine echoes Ji Ji's reference to the Gui Fang campaign. In Wei Ji's incomplete state, launching a campaign requires tremendous determination. After three years of arduous struggle, great rewards finally come. This line encourages us: during difficult times, with correct direction and firm resolve, persistent effort will ultimately yield rich rewards. Three years symbolizes the long commitment required.

lines.textZh: 六五：贞吉，无悔，君子之光，有孚，吉。
lines.textEn: Six in the fifth place: Perseverance brings good fortune. No remorse. The light of the superior man is true. Good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 坚守正道则吉，无悔恨。君子的光辉出于诚信，吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: Perseverance brings fortune without remorse. The superior man's radiance shines with sincerity. Good fortune.
lines.detailZh: 六五“贞吉，无悔。君子之光，有孚，吉”是未济卦最光明的一爻。“君子之光”描述了在黑暗中闪耀的光芒——即使事情尚未完成，君子的品德和智慧依然如光芒般照耀。“有孚”意为有诚信，以真诚和信念坚持到底，必然吉祥。这一爻给所有“在路上”的人以希望：过程本身就有价值，坚持的光芒终将照亮前路。
lines.detailEn: The fifth six is Wei Ji's brightest line. 'The light of the superior man is true. Good fortune.' Even when things are incomplete, the virtue and wisdom of a noble person shine like light in darkness. 'Sincerity' means persevering with genuine faith to the end. This line offers hope to everyone 'on the journey': the process itself has value, and the light of perseverance will ultimately illuminate the path ahead.

lines.textZh: 上九：有孚于饮酒，无咎，濡其首，有孚失是。
lines.textEn: Nine at the top: There is drinking of wine in genuine confidence. No blame. But if one wets his head, he loses it in truth.
lines.interpretationZh: 以诚信之心饮酒庆贺，无过失。但若沉溺过度，头浸入水中，则失去诚信。
lines.interpretationEn: Drinking wine with genuine confidence — no blame. But excess leads to losing one's truth.
lines.detailZh: 上九“有孚于饮酒，无咎。濡其首，有孚失是”描绘了一个微妙的场景：以诚信之心饮酒庆祝是无咎的，但如果喝到头都浸入酒中（濡其首），就失去了分寸。这是未济卦最后的忠告：在漫长的奋斗过程中，适当的放松和庆祝是必要的，但不能过度。保持“有孚”（诚信和自律），享受过程但不沉溺其中。《周易》以这样一个充满人情味的画面作为全书的结尾，温暖而深刻。
lines.detailEn: The top nine paints a nuanced scene: drinking wine in good faith is blameless, but getting one's head soaked in wine means losing all sense of proportion. This is Wei Ji's final counsel: during the long journey of striving, appropriate relaxation and celebration are necessary, but excess must be avoided. Maintain sincerity and self-discipline; enjoy the process without drowning in it. The I Ching ends with this warmly human image—gentle yet profound.

overviewZh: 未济卦是《周易》第六十四卦，也是最后一卦，由上离（火）下坎（水）组成，火在水上，火焰向上、水流向下，二者背道而驰，无法交融。“未济”意为“尚未渡过河”，象征事情尚未完成、目标尚未达到。与既济卦六爻全部当位相反，未济卦六爻全部不当位——阳爻居阴位、阴爻居阳位，看似混乱却充满可能性。《周易》以未济作为终卦，寓意深远：世间没有绝对的完成，一切都在进行中，结束即是新的开始。
overviewEn: Wei Ji (Before Completion) is the 64th and final hexagram of the I Ching, composed of Li (fire) above and Kan (water) below. Fire rises upward while water flows downward—the two move apart, unable to interact. 'Wei Ji' means 'not yet crossed the river,' symbolizing tasks unfinished and goals not yet reached. Opposite to Ji Ji where all lines are perfectly placed, Wei Ji has all six lines out of position—yet this apparent disorder is full of potential. The I Ching ends with Wei Ji to convey a profound message: nothing in the world is ever truly complete; everything is in process; every ending is a new beginning.
judgmentDetailZh: 未济卦辞“亨。小狐汔济，濡其尾，无攸利”以小狐狸渡河为喻：小狐狸快要渡过河了，却在最后关头尾巴沾湿了水，功亏一篑。这个生动的比喻告诫我们：越是接近成功，越要谨慎小心。“行百里者半九十”，最后的十里往往是最难的。未济卦虽然表面看是“未完成”的状态，但《彖传》说“未济亨”——正因为尚未完成，所以还有无限的发展空间和可能性。这是一种充满希望的“未完成”，而非绝望的失败。关键在于保持耐心、谨慎行事，不要在最后关头功亏一篑。
judgmentDetailEn: The judgment uses the metaphor of a young fox crossing a river: 'Nearly across, the little fox gets its tail wet—nothing that would further.' The fox almost makes it but fails at the very last moment. This vivid image warns that the closer we are to success, the more careful we must be. Though Wei Ji appears to be a state of incompletion, the Commentary says 'Before Completion brings success'—precisely because things are not yet finished, infinite possibilities remain. This is a hopeful incompletion, not desperate failure. The key is maintaining patience and caution, not stumbling at the finish line.
imageDetailZh: 象辞“火在水上，未济。君子以慎辨物居方”描述了火与水各居其位却无法交融的状态。“慎辨物居方”意为谨慎地辨别事物的性质，让每样东西都处于恰当的位置。这是未济卦给出的行动指南——在混乱尚未理清的时候，最重要的不是急于行动，而是先把事情分类理清、各归其位。就像整理一间凌乱的房间，先分类再归位，效率远高于胡乱收拾。在管理中，这意味着先理清组织架构和职责分工，再推进具体工作。
imageDetailEn: The Image 'Fire over water: the image of the condition before transition. Thus the superior man is careful in the differentiation of things, so that each finds its place' describes fire and water each in their position but unable to interact. 'Careful differentiation' means discerning the nature of things and placing each in its proper position. In times of disorder, the priority is not rushing to act but first classifying and organizing—like tidying a messy room by sorting before placing, far more efficient than random cleanup.
modernApplicationZh: 【事业】未济卦提示我们正处于“尚未完成”的阶段，不要急躁。创业路上的挫折不是失败，而是“未济”——还在渡河的过程中。保持耐心，谨慎辨别方向，一步一步走稳。“小狐汔济”的教训是：越接近目标越要小心，不要在最后关头掉以轻心。【感情】关系中的不完美不必焦虑，未济卦告诉我们：完美的关系不存在，重要的是双方都在努力的过程中。接受“未完成”的状态，享受共同成长的旅程。【财运】投资尚未见到回报时不要轻易放弃，但也要“慎辨物居方”——重新审视投资组合，确保每笔资金都在合适的位置。耐心等待，但不盲目坚持。【健康】身体调理是一个长期过程，不要期望立竿见影。未济卦提醒我们保持耐心，坚持良好的生活习惯，健康的改善需要时间积累。
modernApplicationEn: 【Career】Wei Ji indicates we're in an 'unfinished' phase—don't be impatient. Setbacks on the entrepreneurial path aren't failure but 'before completion'—still crossing the river. Stay patient, carefully discern direction, and proceed step by step.【Love】Don't be anxious about imperfections in relationships. Wei Ji teaches that perfect relationships don't exist—what matters is that both parties are working at it. Accept the 'unfinished' state and enjoy the journey of growing together.【Wealth】Don't abandon investments prematurely when returns haven't materialized, but do 'carefully differentiate'—reassess your portfolio and ensure each allocation is properly placed. Be patient but not blindly persistent.【Health】Physical recovery is a long-term process—don't expect instant results. Wei Ji reminds us to maintain patience and consistent healthy habits; improvement takes time.
historicalStoryZh: 未济卦最深刻的历史寓意在于《周易》本身的编排智慧。周文王将未济卦放在六十四卦的最末，而非既济卦，这个选择意味深长：世界永远处于“未完成”的状态，没有绝对的终点。这与佛教“生生不息”、道家“道生一，一生二，二生三，三生万物”的思想一脉相承。历史上，刘邦建立汉朝后面临的局面正是“未济”——虽然推翻了秦朝，但异姓王割据、匈奴威胁、制度未立，一切都还在“渡河”的过程中。正是因为刘邦和后继者们认识到“未济”的现实，才能持续努力，最终成就了四百年大汉基业。
historicalStoryEn: Wei Ji's most profound historical significance lies in the I Ching's own arrangement. King Wen placed Wei Ji—not Ji Ji—as the final hexagram, a deeply meaningful choice: the world is always in a state of 'incompletion,' with no absolute endpoint. After Liu Bang established the Han Dynasty, he faced a classic Wei Ji situation—though the Qin had fallen, regional kings held power, the Xiongnu threatened borders, and institutions were unbuilt. Everything was still 'crossing the river.' Because Liu Bang and his successors recognized this reality of incompletion, they continued striving, ultimately building a dynasty that lasted four hundred years.
relatedHexagramsNote: 未济卦的综卦为既济卦（第63卦），一未成一已成，互为表里，共同构成《周易》的终章；错卦为既济卦的反面。未济与既济的关系揭示了宇宙最根本的规律：完成与未完成永远交替循环，没有绝对的终点。
